ratings.notes: High burst damage mage with area denial and a revive passive.

skills.name: Burning Wrath
skills.description: Faye's magic power increases by 1% for every 5% health missing, up to 20%.

skills.name: Flame Shield
skills.description: Surrounds herself with a ring of fire that deals 60 magic damage per second to nearby enemies and reduces incoming damage by 20%. Lasts 4 seconds. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Rebirth
skills.description: Instantly heals an ally for 200 health plus 10% of their max health. Can be self-cast. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Sister's Longing
skills.description: When Rafaela is on the enemy team, Faye gains 15% damage reduction against her and her abilities deal 20% more damage to Rafaela.

heroes.lore: Faye Juisin was an archangel betrayed by the Celestial Council and sent on a suicide mission. She survived by absorbing a primordial demon's fire, becoming the Phoenix of the Fallen Legion. Now she leads a rebellion against the corrupt Council, seeking to force heaven to remember mercy, while waiting for her sister Rafaela to see the truth.

relationships.description: Faye loves her sister and waits for her to understand why she fell. She will never raise her hand against Rafaela unless forced.
They fell for different reasons, but respect each other's pain. Rarely ally, but never fight.

pet.name: Hinotori
pet.description: A tiny firestorm sparrow of flame and feather, its wings a blur of orange and gold, body no larger than a fist. It darts and weaves around Faye's head, leaving trails of sparks. When she is sad, it perches on her shoulder and nuzzles her cheek; when angry, it circles her head, growing larger, flames brighter.
pet.fulldesc: Hinotori is a firestorm sparrow, a tiny creature of flame and feather, its wings a blur of orange and gold, its body no larger than Faye's fist. It darts and weaves around her head, leaving trails of sparks that fade into the air, and it chirps with a sound like crackling embers. The sparrow is not merely a pet; it is a fragment of the phoenix fire that resurrected Faye, a piece of her own soul given form. When she is sad, it perches on her shoulder and nuzzles her cheek; when she is angry, it circles her head, growing larger, its flames brighter, a harbinger of the inferno to come. It is her only remaining link to the heaven that cast her out, and she treasures it above all else. When Faye fights, Hinotori flies above her, raining small fireballs on her enemies; when she rests, it curls in her hair, its warmth a comfort in the cold of her exile.
abilities: Ember Rain – rains small fireballs on enemies within 10 meters.
Warmth – passively heals Faye for 10 health per second.
Flare – emits a blinding flash that stuns nearby enemies for 0.5 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Area-of-effect mage with high burst damage, revive passive, and team utility. Excels at zone control and sustained fights.

heroes.name: Grymnyr
heroes.shortDescription: A Viking warlord who sold his soul to the Abyssal Ocean, now bound as a warden of the eternal darkness. Grymnyr patrols the depths with his abyssal fire indigo shark Verox, wielding the Soulcutter sword and an anchor hook, guarding the gates against demons who would claim his bloodline—especially his descendant, Franco.
heroes.fullDescription: He rises from the churning depths like a mountain breached by a cataclysm, a figure carved from the nightmares of sailors and the greed of kings. Grymnyr appears as a man who was once the undisputed tyrant of the mortal seas—a Viking warlord whose name was whispered in terror from the fjords of the north to the distant shores of the south. Now, he is something far more terrible: a warden of the Abyssal Ocean, bound by a pact sealed in blood and soulfire. His facial structure is rough and weathered: a broad, squared jaw; a thick, prominent nose broken and reset many times; high, wind‑scoured cheekbones. His skin is old leather, scarred, with a permanent blue‑grey tinge. His remaining eye (left lost to a sea serpent) burns with ember‑orange glow. A jagged scar runs from right eye to jaw. Over his lost eye, he wears a patch of cracked black leather embossed with a silver skull. His hair is a wild untamed mane of fiery orange, tangled with salt and remnants of gold chains. His beard is thick, woven with ship's bone fragments. His physique is massive—dense corded muscle, seven feet tall, barrel chest, thick arms, narrow waist. He wears beaten iron cuirass in maroon and mahogany overlaid with violet and indigo swirling patterns, a stained pirate coat of faded blue, a black leather pirate hat with an indigo flame feather, and carries gold chains, rings, and a belt of keys. His weapons are the Soulcutter (a broad curved sword that ignites with ghostly purple flame) and an anchor hook (massive curved spike on a chain). His companion is Verox, an Abyssal Fire Indigo Shark twice the length of a longship, with indigo scales shimmering with internal flame.

story.part1: Before the Abyss claimed him, Grymnyr was the most feared pirate to ever sail the frozen seas of the north. His longship, the 'Sea Serpent's Bane,' flew a banner of a grinning skull wreathed in flame, and captains would rather scuttle their own ships than face him in battle. He amassed a treasure beyond counting—gold from a hundred conquered ports, jewels from a thousand sunken galleons, artifacts from civilizations that had crumbled to dust. But Grymnyr was not cruel for cruelty's sake; he was a Viking, and in his culture, the sea was a goddess who demanded tribute. He gave her blood, and she gave him victory. It was a simple exchange, until the day he faced a storm that even the sea goddess could not control.
story.part2: The Abyssal Ocean is not a place; it is a hunger. When Grymnyr's ship was caught in a maelstrom that swallowed the horizon, he knew he was facing something older than the gods. His crew was pulled under one by one, their screams swallowed by the churning water. Grymnyr fought the current, his massive arms straining, his anchor hook digging into the wood of his sinking ship. Then the Abyss spoke to him. It offered a bargain: his soul for the power to rule the depths, his freedom for the strength to guard the gate between worlds. Grymnyr, seeing his crew dead and his ship shattered, agreed. He plunged into the darkness, and when he emerged, he was no longer a man. He was a warden.
story.part3: For centuries, Grymnyr patrolled the Abyssal Ocean, guiding the souls of drowned sailors to their rest and turning back the demons that tried to use the depths as a gateway to the mortal world. He grew powerful, his body adapting to the crushing pressure, his sword learning to burn with abyssal flame. He took Verox, the indigo shark, as his mount, a beast as ancient and hungry as the Abyss itself. He built a fortress from the wrecks of a thousand ships, a twisted monument to his eternal duty. And he watched, from the darkness, as the world above forgot his name.
story.part4: The hardest part of his curse was watching his bloodline continue. He had a son before he sold his soul, a boy who grew into a man who grew into a father, and so on, down the centuries. Grymnyr watched from the depths as his descendants became pirates, merchants, warriors. He saw the good ones and the bad ones, and he loved them all with a fierce, helpless love. Then came Franco. The boy was different—not a pirate, but a fighter for justice, a man who wielded a chain and hook not for plunder but for protection. Grymnyr wept, the first tears he had shed in centuries, because Franco was everything he could have been. And because Franco would never know him.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has weakened the barriers between the Abyssal Ocean and the mortal world, and demons are slipping through the cracks. Grymnyr fights them daily, his Soulcutter blazing, his anchor hook pulling them back into the darkness. He has seen Franco's name in the prayers of sailors, heard the boy's legend growing. He knows that one day, Franco may face a demon that even his chain and hook cannot defeat. And when that day comes, Grymnyr will rise from the depths. He will not reveal himself as an ancestor; he will appear as a stranger, a warden, a guardian. He will fight beside his descendant, and when the battle is won, he will vanish back into the abyss, leaving Franco to wonder if he dreamed the whole thing. For Grymnyr knows that the truest legacy a man can leave is not the treasure he hoards, but the soul he saves. And he will spend eternity making sure that Franco never has to make the same choice he did.

weapons.name: Soulcutter & Anchor Hook
weapons.fulldesc: Grymnyr wields twin instruments of abyssal judgment. The Soulcutter is a pirate sword of ancient, brutal design, its blade broad and slightly curved, its edge serrated with the teeth of sea dragons. The steel is dark, almost black, but when drawn, the blade ignites with a ghostly, purple flame that casts no heat but drains all warmth from the air. The sword was forged from the iron of a fallen star and quenched in the blood of a demon king, and it burns with the abyssal fire that now consumes his soul. In his left hand, he grips an anchor hook—a massive, curved spike of black iron, its surface etched with runes that glow with the same violet light as his sword. The hook is attached to a chain of interlocking, obsidian links that coil around his arm and trail behind him, clinking softly with a sound like distant bells. With this, he can snatch a fleeing ship from a league away, drag a sea serpent from the depths, or pull any soul who tries to bypass his realm into the crushing darkness of the Abyssal Ocean.
weapons.look: Broad curved dark steel sword with serrated dragon-tooth edge, purple ghost flame; massive curved black iron anchor hook on obsidian chain
weapons.aura: Violet and indigo, trailing abyssal mist

active.name: Soul Cleave

ultimate.name: Kraken's Grasp
ultimate.description: Grymnyr slams his anchor hook into the ground, summoning spectral tentacles in a 20-meter radius that grab all enemies, pulling them toward him and dealing 300% physical damage over 3 seconds. Enemies are rooted for the duration. Cooldown: 85 seconds.

skills.name: Sea Dog's Charge
skills.description: Charges forward 10 meters, knocking aside enemies and dealing 120% physical damage. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Drowning Silence
skills.description: Grymnyr roars, silencing all enemies within 10 meters for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Bloodline Vigil
skills.description: When Franco is an ally, Grymnyr gains 15% damage reduction and Franco gains 15% damage bonus. When Franco is an enemy, Grymnyr cannot attack him for the first 3 seconds of combat.

heroes.lore: Grymnyr was a Viking pirate lord who sold his soul to the Abyssal Ocean to survive a maelstrom. Now he serves as a warden of the depths, guarding the gate between worlds. He watches over his descendant Franco from the shadows, praying that the boy never makes the same choice he did.

relationships.description: Grymnyr watches over his descendant from the depths, ready to protect him from demonic threats.
Fire demons often try to breach the Abyssal Ocean; Grymnyr has clashed with Thamuz's minions many times.

mount.name: Verox
mount.description: A massive shark twice the length of a longship, its hide a shifting mosaic of indigo scales shimmering with internal flame-like glow. Eyes are ember orange like Grymnyr's. Dorsal fin is a jagged crest of black coral, tail a massive sweeping blade. Moves through abyssal waters with silence, leaving a phosphorescent flame trail.
mount.fulldesc: Verox is an Abyssal Fire Indigo Shark of terrifying majesty. The creature is easily twice the length of a longship, its hide a shifting mosaic of indigo scales that shimmer with an internal, flame‑like glow. Its eyes are the same ember orange as Grymnyr's own, and they burn with a hunger that has been honed over centuries of hunting the depths. Its dorsal fin is a jagged crest of black coral, and its tail is a massive, sweeping blade that can crush the hull of a warship with a single stroke. Verox moves through the abyssal waters with a silence that belies its size, its passage marked only by the faint, phosphorescent trail of flame that follows in its wake. It is bound to Grymnyr by the same pact that claimed the pirate's soul, and together they patrol the eternal darkness, guardians of a realm that no living sailor should ever find. When Grymnyr rides Verox, the shark's flame trail illuminates the abyss, and its roar—a sound like grinding stone and rushing water—announces the Warden's approach.
abilities: Flame Trail – leaves a trail of phosphorescent flame that damages enemies.
Crushing Bite – deals massive damage and applies a bleed.
Abyssal Speed – can move at incredible speeds through water.

heroes.specialty: Tanky initiator with pull, crowd control, and anti-demon bonuses. Excels at starting fights and protecting allies.

dialogue.selection: The depths remember. So do I.

heroes.name: Shenku Fenghou
titles: The Jade Tiger of the Streets
heroes.shortDescription: A martial artist who fused ancient kungfu with the raw expressive energy of hip-hop culture, moving like a rhythm given form. Shenku Fenghou fights with his fists wrapped in jade-green chi energy, accompanied by his jade crow Jei Fei, seeking worthy opponents to test his skills against—especially the legendary fighter Chou.
heroes.fullDescription: He moves like a rhythm given form, a beat made flesh, his every step a syncopated pulse against the concrete jungle he calls his dojo. Shenku Fenghou appears as a man in his mid‑twenties, a martial artist who has fused the ancient discipline of kungfu with the raw, expressive energy of hip‑hop culture, creating something entirely new and utterly formidable. His facial structure is a striking blend of sharp angles and boyish charm: a strong, defined jawline; high, prominent cheekbones; a straight, slender nose; a perpetual mischievous smirk. His skin is fair, almost porcelain, with a warm golden undertone. His eyes are a striking luminous jade green flecked with gold, sparkling with infectious energy. His hair is a messy, voluminous blonde mane, cropped short at the sides, thick and wild on top in a gravity-defying crest. His physique is a balance of bulk and agility: six feet tall, broad shoulders, defined chest, thick corded arms, narrow waist, powerful hips and legs. He wears a modified black kungfu gi with jade green tiger patterns, sleeveless, with a wide black belt, white high-top sneakers laced with green. His weapon is his fists, channeling jade-green chi into spectral tiger claws. His companion is Jei Fei, a jade crow with luminous green feathers and jade eyes.

story.part1: Shenku Fenghou grew up in the streets of Mekhanika's lowest districts, where the only way to survive was to be faster, stronger, or smarter than everyone else. He chose all three. His mother worked three jobs to keep him fed; his father was a ghost who had left before Shenku could walk. The streets became his teacher, and the rhythm of the city—the beat of distant music, the pulse of the crowd, the syncopation of a fight—became his language. By the age of twelve, he had won his first underground tournament, knocking out a fighter twice his size with a combination of kungfu and breakdancing that left the audience stunned.
story.part2: The turning point came when Shenku met an old master named Shifu Tien, a man who saw the boy's potential and offered to train him in the ancient arts of chi. Shenku refused at first—he didn't trust old men with secrets—but Shifu Tien was patient. He showed Shenku that chi was not a weapon, but a flow, a rhythm, a beat that ran through all living things. Shenku understood immediately. He began training, not in a dojo, but in the streets, incorporating the old man's teachings into his own unique style. Within a year, he had mastered the Tiger Claw technique. Within three, he had developed his signature move: the Heavenly Jade Punch, a strike that glowed with green light and hit with the force of a freight train.
story.part3: Shifu Tien died when Shenku was nineteen, killed not by age or disease, but by a rival dojo that saw the old man as a threat. Shenku found his master's body in the alley behind their training ground, a note pinned to his chest: 'The streets belong to the strong. The weak die forgotten.' Shenku did not cry. He did not rage. He went to the rival dojo, fought all twelve of its masters at once, and left them broken but alive. He took their banner and burned it in the street. 'The strong protect,' he said to the crowd that gathered. 'The weak are the ones who forget that.'
story.part4: Now, Shenku is a legend in the underground fighting circuits of Mekhanika. He has never lost a match, not because he is unbeatable, but because he chooses his opponents carefully. He fights not for money or fame, but for the joy of the contest, for the chance to test himself against someone who might push him to his limits. He has heard of Chou, the legendary fighter who moves like a shadow and strikes like a thunderbolt. Shenku has never met him, but he wants to. Not to defeat him—to dance with him, to trade blows like verses in a rap battle, to see who can make the other smile first.
story.part5: The Sundering has brought new dangers to the streets, but Shenku sees opportunity. Demonic incursions mean more fights, more challenges, more chances to grow. He has begun to patrol the poorer districts, not as a hero, but as a guardian—a tiger watching over his territory. He knows that Chou is out there, somewhere, and he knows that their paths will cross. When they do, Shenku intends to greet him with a grin, a fist bump, and the kind of fight that will be spoken of for generations. For in his heart, he knows that the truest victory is not in defeating an opponent, but in pushing them—and himself—to heights they never thought possible. He is the Jade Tiger of the Streets, and his rhythm is the heartbeat of the city.

appearance.hairStyle: Messy voluminous crest, cropped short at sides, thick and wild on top

upper.arms: Muscular arms exposed, jade chi aura around hands when active
upper.waist: Wide black belt wrapped and tied traditionally, ends hanging past knees

lower.hips: Loose kungfu pants, black, tucked into high-tops
lower.feet: High-top sneakers, pristine white, laced with green

upper.chest: Defined, muscular

weapons.name: Jade Tiger Claws (Chi Fists)
weapons.fulldesc: Shenku's fists are his primary weapons, and when he channels his internal chi, they become something far more formidable. A shimmering, jade‑green aura envelops his hands, coalescing into a pair of spectral tiger claws that extend past his knuckles, humming with focused power. With these, he can strike pressure points with surgical precision, deliver blows that resonate through bone and spirit alike, and, when the moment calls for it, throw concussive blasts of energy from his palms—shockwaves of jade light that can shatter concrete and send opponents flying. He has mastered the art of the Tiger Claw, the Dragon Palm, and the Mantis Strike, and he moves through his forms with a fluid, almost musical grace, each motion a verse, each combination a chorus.
weapons.look: Spectral jade-green tiger claws extending from knuckles, shimmering aura
weapons.aura: Jade-green light, shimmering, rhythmic pulses

passive.name: Rhythm Fighter
passive.description: Each basic attack increases Shenku's attack speed by 5% for 3 seconds, stacking up to 6 times.

passive.name: Tiger's Focus
passive.description: Critical strikes reduce ability cooldowns by 0.5 seconds.

active.name: Tiger Claw Strike
active.description: Dash to an enemy and deliver three rapid strikes, each dealing 70% physical damage. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Dragon Palm
active.description: Unleashes a shockwave in a 8-meter line, dealing 150% physical damage and knocking back enemies. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Jade Tempest
ultimate.description: Shenku enters a heightened state for 8 seconds, gaining 50% attack speed, 30% movement speed, and his basic attacks become area-of-effect. Cooldown: 70 seconds.

special.name: Mantis Counter
special.description: After dodging an ability (using movement), Shenku's next basic attack deals 100% bonus damage and stuns for 0.75 seconds.

ratings.notes: High mobility fighter with attack speed scaling and dueling potential.

skills.name: Street Rhythm
skills.description: Shenku's movement speed increases by 5% for each unique enemy nearby, up to 20%.

skills.name: Beat Drop
skills.description: Shenku stomps, creating a shockwave that slows enemies in a 6-meter radius by 40% for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 9 seconds.

skills.name: Freestyle
skills.description: Shenku enters a flow state, negating the next crowd control effect that hits him within 3 seconds. If he successfully negates, he gains 20% damage for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Respect the Old School
skills.description: When facing Chou, Shenku's abilities deal 10% more damage and he takes 10% less damage from Chou.

heroes.lore: Shenku Fenghou grew up in the streets of Mekhanika, learning kungfu from an old master who saw his potential. He fused traditional martial arts with hip-hop culture, creating a unique fighting style. Now he seeks worthy opponents to test his skills, especially the legendary fighter Chou.

relationships.description: Shenku has heard of Chou's legend and wants to fight him—not to defeat, but to dance with.
The old man who taught Shenku the ways of chi; his memory drives Shenku to protect the weak.

pet.name: Jei Fei
pet.description: A jade crow of ethereal beauty, its feathers not black but a deep luminous green like polished jade, shimmering with inner light. Eyes are striking jade like Shenku's own. Can see the flow of chi in others and caws softly to warn Shenku of hidden strength or weakness.
pet.fulldesc: Jei Fei is a jade crow, a creature of ethereal beauty and sharp intelligence. Its feathers are not black, but a deep, luminous green, the color of polished jade, and they shimmer with an inner light that shifts and dances as it moves. Its eyes are the same striking jade as Shenku's own, and it watches the world with a keen, unblinking gaze. Jei Fei is no mere pet; it is a spirit companion, a fragment of the same ancient energy that fuels Shenku's fists. It perches on his shoulder during moments of rest, nuzzles his cheek with its sleek head, and when he fights, it circles above, screeching encouragement, its cry a sharp, clarion call that echoes through the streets. It is said that Jei Fei can see the flow of chi in others, and it will often caw softly to alert Shenku to an opponent's hidden strength or a weakness in their stance. When Shenku trains, Jei Fei mimics his movements, learning alongside him; when he fights, it dive-bombs enemies, distracting them at crucial moments.
abilities: Chi Sight – reveals enemy health bars and ability cooldowns (visual feedback).
Distraction – dive-bombs an enemy, causing them to miss their next basic attack.
Encouragement – increases Shenku's critical strike chance by 5% while perched on his shoulder.

heroes.specialty: Mobile fighter with attack speed scaling, crowd control immunity, and dueling potential. Excels in 1v1 situations and skirmishes.

heroes.name: Nene
titles: The Spirit Hunter of Twilight Whispers
heroes.shortDescription: A girl on the cusp of adolescence who walks the border between dreams and reality, hunting the spirits that prey on the sleeping. Nene is the cautious twin to Nana's bubbly innocence, wielding the massive Nightshade boomerang and accompanied by Sui Feng, a kitten familiar bound to her by ancient magic.
heroes.fullDescription: She steps from the mist like a half‑remembered dream, a figure of haunting beauty and solemn purpose. Nene appears as a girl on the cusp of adolescence, her age fixed at thirteen years, yet there is an ancient wisdom in her eyes that belies her youth. Her face is that of a Chinese child rendered in ethereal perfection: a soft, heart‑shaped visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, rosy lips often pressed into a contemplative line. Her skin is clear luminous porcelain with a faint flush of pink at her cheeks. Her eyes are large, expressive, deep poisonous purple shot through with sickly pink veins that pulse faintly when she draws upon her powers. Her hair is long purplish-pink silk past her waist, with stark white bangs. Two large tufted cat ears rise from her crown—pale pink inside, violet-grey outside. She stands 4'3", slender and lithe. She wears a deep poisonous purple Gothic Lolita dress with ruffles and lace, an oversized hooded cloak of sickly pink, a worn lavender silk scarf, mismatched boots (one black leather with silver buckles, one grey suede with purple laces). Her weapon is the Nightshade, a 10-foot boomerang of dark wood with swirling patterns that spreads hallucinogenic mist. Her companion is Sui Feng, a small kitten with creamy white fur and purple engravings that glow in the dark.

story.part1: Nene and her twin sister Nana were born under a blood moon, a portent that the elders of their village said would bring either great blessing or great tragedy. Nana was the first, emerging with a cry that shook the windows—a child of light and laughter. Nene was the second, silent and watchful, her eyes open and aware the moment she was born. The elders studied them, performed rituals, and declared that Nene had been touched by the spirit realm. She could see what others could not: the shadows that clung to the sleeping, the whispers that crawled through dreams, the nightmares that fed on fear. Nana, they said, would be her anchor—the light that kept Nene from being pulled into the darkness forever.
story.part2: The first spirit came when the twins were seven. Nana had a nightmare—a creature of writhing shadows that wrapped around her throat, stealing her breath. Nene woke to find her sister choking, and without thinking, she reached into the dream and pulled the creature out. It materialized in the room, a shrieking mass of claws and teeth, and Nene fought it with her bare hands. She won, but she was covered in scratches, and Nana never remembered the attack. That was when Nene understood her purpose: she would hunt the spirits that preyed on the innocent, and she would never let her sister know how close the darkness came.
story.part3: The Nightshade was a gift from the spirit that taught her to hunt—a creature of twilight that had grown tired of watching nightmares devour dreamers. The boomerang was crafted from wood that grew only in the space between waking and sleeping, and its throw spread a mist that blurred the line between real and imagined. Nene trained with it for years, learning to guide its arc with her mind, to call it back with a thought. Sui Feng came later—a kitten she found in a dream, its fur marked with glowing purple runes. The kitten was a fragment of the same magic that flowed through her, and it became her familiar, her only confidant, the sole being that knew the full weight of her duty.
story.part4: As the twins grew, Nana blossomed into a cheerful, playful girl who saw only the bright side of the world. Nene became quiet, watchful, her words sharp and her laughter rare. The other children thought she was strange, even scary, but Nana never judged her sister. She would hold Nene's hand in the dark, sing to her when the nightmares were loud, and never ask why her twin's eyes sometimes glowed with purple light. Nene loved her sister fiercely, but she could never tell her the truth. She could never say, 'I hunt the things that would eat your dreams. I fight so you can sleep.'
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn holes in the fabric between worlds, and spirits are crossing into the waking realm more frequently than ever. Nene patrols the twilight paths, the Nightshade spinning, Sui Feng at her side, hunting the nightmares that slip through. She has seen her sister's dreams darken, felt the presence of something ancient watching Nana from the edges of sleep. She does not know what it is, but she knows it will come. And when it does, Nene will be ready. She will fight as she has always fought—in silence, in shadow, in the spaces between heartbeats. And if she falls, she will fall knowing that Nana will never remember her name, never know that her twin died to keep her safe. For Nene is the Spirit Hunter, and her love is the only weapon that never dulls.

weapons.name: Nightshade
weapons.fulldesc: The Nightshade is a weapon that defies the scale of its wielder. It is a boomerang of immense proportions, standing a full ten feet tall—more than twice Nene's height—its curves sweeping out in a graceful, deadly arc. The weapon is crafted from a strange, dark wood that seems to absorb light, its surface etched with swirling patterns of sickly pink and poisonous purple that pulse with a faint, hypnotic glow. When thrown, the Nightshade does not simply return; it spreads a cloud of hallucinogenic mist in its wake, a fog of dark magic that bends perception, conjures fears from the subconscious, and blurs the line between what is real and what is imagined. Nene wields it with a deceptive ease, her small hands gripping the central grip with practiced familiarity, and she can send it spinning through the air to strike enemies from impossible angles, the mist trailing behind it like the tail of a phantom.
weapons.look: Massive 10-foot dark wood boomerang, purple and pink swirling patterns, hypnotic glow
weapons.aura: Hallucinogenic mist, purple and pink light

passive.name: Dream Mist
passive.description: Enemies hit by Nightshade are afflicted with Hallucination, causing their movement controls to be slightly randomized for 2 seconds.

passive.name: Twilight Return
passive.description: Nightshade always returns to Nene, passing through walls and obstacles.

active.name: Spirit Throw
active.description: Throws Nightshade in a large arc, dealing 150% physical damage to all enemies in its path and leaving a mist trail that lasts 4 seconds. Cooldown: 7 seconds.

active.name: Fearful Echo
active.description: Nightshade creates a spectral copy that flies in the opposite direction, dealing 80% damage to enemies hit by either. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Nightmare Parade
ultimate.description: Nene spins Nightshade around her, creating a 15-meter radius zone of dense hallucinogenic mist for 6 seconds. Enemies inside are slowed by 40%, take 100 magic damage per second, and have their vision reduced to 3 meters. Cooldown: 75 seconds.

special.name: Lucid Strikes
special.description: Nightshade deals 30% bonus damage to enemies who are asleep, stunned, or feared.

ratings.notes: Control-oriented marksman/assassin hybrid with vision manipulation.

skills.name: Spirit Sight
skills.description: Nene can see invisible and ethereal enemies within 12 meters.

skills.name: Dream Step
skills.description: Nene teleports to the location of her Nightshade after throwing it, or to a nearby shadow (8 meters). Cooldown: 9 seconds.

skills.name: Lullaby
skills.description: Nene sings a soft lullaby, putting an enemy to sleep for 2 seconds (damage breaks sleep). Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Twin Bond
skills.description: When Nana is an ally, Nene gains 10% damage reduction and Nana gains 10% damage bonus. When Nana is an enemy, Nene's abilities have 15% increased range against her.

heroes.lore: Nene was born under a blood moon, touched by the spirit realm. She hunts nightmares that prey on the sleeping, wielding the Nightshade boomerang and accompanied by her familiar Sui Feng. She protects her twin sister Nana from the darkness, never letting her know how close it comes.

relationships.description: Nene loves Nana fiercely and protects her from spirits, never revealing her true purpose.
Both are spirit-touched; they have met in the twilight realm and respect each other's duties.

pet.name: Sui Feng
pet.description: A small kitten, no larger than a teacup, with creamy white fur and intricate purple engravings that glow faintly in the dark. Large luminous pale lavender eyes. Moves with liquid grace, leaping from shadow to shadow.
pet.fulldesc: Sui Feng is a small kitten, no larger than a teacup, its fur a soft, creamy white, marked with intricate, purple engravings that glow faintly in the dark. The engravings are not painted on; they are part of the kitten's very being, runes that pulse with the same magic that flows through Nene's bracelet. Sui Feng has large, luminous eyes of pale lavender, and it moves with a liquid grace, leaping from shadow to shadow, often perching on Nene's shoulder or winding between her ankles. The kitten is not merely a pet; it is a familiar, a spirit companion bound to her by a pact older than memory. When danger threatens, Sui Feng can channel dark magic to buff Nene's abilities, wrapping her in a shroud of protective shadow or amplifying the hallucinogenic power of the Nightshade. It is her only confidant, the sole being who has witnessed the full depth of her caution and her quiet, fierce love for the sister from whom she is so different. When Nene sleeps, Sui Feng curls on her chest, its glowing runes pulsing in rhythm with her heartbeat, guarding her dreams as she guards others'.
abilities: Shadow Cloak – grants Nene 20% dodge chance for 4 seconds.
Mist Amplifier – increases Nightshade's mist duration by 2 seconds.
Dream Link – Nene can see through Sui Feng's eyes for 5 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Control marksman with vision manipulation, crowd control (sleep), and mobility. Excels at disrupting enemies and protecting allies from ambushes.

dialogue.selection: I see what hides in dreams.

heroes.name: Endou Kusanai
heroes.shortDescription: A boy who turned the slums into his stadium and the gods into his rivals. Endou Kusanai is a goalkeeper blessed by the street gods, his hands capable of summoning the God Hand—a spectral barrier of golden light. With his exo-legs, graffiti armor, and bulldog Enji, he stands as an unbreakable wall, waiting to prove that a goalkeeper can be as legendary as any striker.
heroes.fullDescription: He stands like a wall of chrome and defiance, a boy who turned the slums into his stadium and the gods into his rivals. Endou Kusanai appears as a young man forged in the crucible of the favela, his age a hard‑earned twenty, though his eyes carry the weight of a thousand matches played on broken concrete under a merciless sun. His face is a study in fierce, angular beauty: a sharp, defined jawline; high, prominent cheekbones; a straight, proud nose broken and healed many times with a heroic crook. His skin is fairy‑like in its luminescence, pale and translucent, marred by beautiful chaotic patterns of tribal graffiti inked into his flesh—chrome yellow, graffiti orange, scorched black. His eyes are hidden behind a cool orange visor that wraps around his temples; when lifted, they are honey‑gold, warm and intense. His hair is a wild glorious pink afro, cropped short on the sides, blending into orange and black cybernetic implants. His physique is lean yet dense, with orange exo-legs from hips down, replacing his lower body. He wears a cropped yellow jacket, spray-painted armor plates, orange pauldrons, black carbon fiber gauntlets. His weapon is his God Hand—gloves that summon spectral barriers and a massive golden hand. His companion is Enji, an orange bulldog with a leather harness and miniature goalpost tag.

story.part1: Endou Kusanai was born in the worst slum of Mekhanika, a place where the sun never seemed to shine and the only escape was a ball made of rags tied with string. His father was a drunk who left when Endou was five. His mother worked in a factory, her hands always raw, her eyes always tired. Endou found his first ball in a trash heap, and from the moment he kicked it, he knew he had found his purpose. The other children played striker—they wanted glory, goals, fame. Endou chose the goal. He stood between two broken crates, his hands raised, and he caught every shot they threw at him. The other kids laughed at first. They stopped laughing when he never let a single ball past him.
story.part2: The accident happened when Endou was twelve. A street race gone wrong, a speeding truck, a moment of impact that shattered his legs. The doctors said he would never walk again, let alone play. Endou refused to accept it. He sold everything he owned, begged, borrowed, and somehow scraped together enough for a pair of experimental exo-legs—orange prosthetics that fused to his hips and gave him the strength to stand, to run, to jump higher than he ever could before. The surgery left him with scars and a permanent debt to a shadowy benefactor, but Endou did not care. He could play again. That was all that mattered.
story.part3: The God Hand came not from training, but from desperation. During a match against a rival slum's team, Endou's exo-legs malfunctioned. He couldn't jump, couldn't dive, and the enemy striker—a brutal boy named Bruno—was bearing down on him with a shot that could crack concrete. Endou closed his eyes, raised his hands, and prayed to the street gods. When he opened them, a massive, golden, spectral hand had risen from the earth, its palm spread, blocking the shot that should have torn through the net. The crowd went silent. Then they roared. Endou had discovered his blessing: the God Hand, a barrier of pure will that could stop anything. He never lost a match after that day.
story.part4: Endou became a legend in the favela, but he never forgot where he came from. He used his winnings to build a proper pitch, to buy equipment for the other kids, to pay his mother's medical bills. He trained every day, his exo-legs humming, his gloves crackling with golden energy. Enji, the bulldog, had been with him since before the accident—a scruffy pup he found shivering in an alley. The dog grew with him, became his shadow, his cheerleader, his partner. When Endou summoned the God Hand, Enji would bark, and the shield would glow brighter, hold stronger. They were inseparable, a boy and his dog against the world.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought new challenges. Demons have begun to appear in the slums, drawn by the fear and despair of the people. Endou has adapted, using his God Hand not just for football, but for protection. He stands as a wall between the innocent and the darkness, his exo-legs crackling, his golden hand rising to block demonic projectiles. He has heard of Bruno—the star striker who left the slums to play in the big leagues, the boy who became a legend of a different kind. Endou does not envy him. He knows that one day, their paths will cross, and when they do, Endou will prove that a goalkeeper can be as legendary as any striker. He will stand his ground, his bulldog growling, his God Hand raised, and he will show Bruno—and the world—that the boy from the slums is a king.

weapons.name: God Hand
weapons.fulldesc: The gloves Endou wears are not mere fabric; they are conduits, their surfaces woven with threads of conductive metal and infused with the same energy that powers his exo‑legs. When he raises his palms, a shimmering barrier of kinetic energy erupts from his fingertips, taking whatever form he wills. It can be a sphere that surrounds him like an impenetrable bubble, a wall that stretches across the goal, or—when the moment demands the impossible—a massive, spectral hand, golden and translucent, that rises from the earth like the palm of a Buddha, its fingers spreading to block shots that should be unstoppable. This is the God Hand, a power that he discovered in the depths of his desperation, a gift from the street gods who saw in him a spirit worthy of their blessing. With it, he has caught rockets, stopped bullets, and turned aside the fury of the supernatural. He calls it his Majin Save, and he has never once failed to use it when the stakes were highest.
weapons.look: Gloves woven with conductive metal threads, glow golden when active; summons spectral golden hand or barrier
weapons.aura: Golden light, shimmering kinetic energy

passive.name: Majin Reflexes
passive.description: Endou has a 15% chance to automatically block any projectile attack (including basic attacks).

passive.name: Unbreakable Will
passive.description: Endou gains 5 armor and magic resist for every 10% health missing.

active.name: God Hand Block
active.description: Summons a spectral hand that blocks all damage from one direction for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Majin Punch
active.description: Endou throws a powerful punch empowered by the God Hand, dealing 200% physical damage to a single target and knocking them back. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Heaven's Wall
ultimate.description: Endou creates a massive golden barrier in a 15-meter line that lasts 5 seconds. The barrier blocks all enemy movement and projectiles. Allies passing through the barrier gain 30% movement speed for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 70 seconds.

special.name: Street King's Pride
special.description: When defending an objective (goal, tower, base), Endou's God Hand abilities have 25% reduced cooldown.

ratings.notes: Defensive tank with exceptional blocking and area denial.

skills.name: Favela Resilience
skills.description: Endou regenerates 1% of his max health per second while standing still.

skills.name: Exo Charge
skills.description: Endou dashes forward 8 meters, knocking aside enemies and gaining a shield for 15% of his max health. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Golden Catch
skills.description: Endou catches the next projectile ability aimed at him or an ally within 5 meters, negating it and reducing his ultimate cooldown by 5 seconds. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Keeper's Rivalry
skills.description: When facing Bruno, Endou's abilities have 15% increased effect (longer block duration, larger barrier).

heroes.lore: Endou Kusanai grew up in the slums of Mekhanika, losing his legs in an accident and replacing them with exo-legs. He discovered the God Hand—a spectral barrier of golden light—during a desperate match, and now uses his powers to protect his community from both rival teams and demonic threats. He dreams of proving that a goalkeeper can be as legendary as any striker.

relationships.description: Bruno is the star striker who left the slums; Endou wants to face him to prove his worth.
His bulldog, constant companion, and partner in every match.

pet.name: Enji
pet.description: A bulldog of pure stubborn loyalty, coat a warm rich orange, muzzle soft chocolate brown. Compact muscular body. Wears a small leather harness studded with orange glow, miniature goalpost tag around neck.
pet.fulldesc: Enji is a bulldog of pure, stubborn loyalty. His coat is a warm, rich orange, his muzzle a soft, chocolate brown, and his body is a compact, muscular engine of affection and ferocity. He wears a small, leather harness studded with the same orange glow as Endou's exo‑legs, and around his neck hangs a tag shaped like a miniature goalpost. Enji is not merely a pet; he is a partner, a creature whose own latent energy can be channeled to amplify Endou's barriers. When Endou summons his God Hand, Enji barks—a deep, resonant sound that sends ripples through the air—and the shield glows brighter, holds stronger, becomes something that even the gods would hesitate to challenge. Enji sits at his feet during quiet moments, rests his heavy head on Endou's knee, and in the heat of a match, he barks encouragement, a small, four‑legged cheerleader who believes in his master with every fiber of his being. When Endou is sad, Enji licks his face; when Endou is angry, Enji growls beside him. They have been together since before the accident, and they will be together until the end.
abilities: Encouraging Bark – increases nearby allies' damage by 5%.
Guardian Growl – reduces damage taken by Endou by 10% while Enji is within 5 meters.
Loyal Charge – Enji can bite an enemy, slowing them by 30% for 2 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Defensive tank with projectile blocking, area denial, and team protection. Excels at defending objectives and absorbing damage.

heroes.name: Shiela Fiesta
heroes.shortDescription: A specter draped in the dust of forgotten trails and the smoke of her own soul‑forged bullets. Shiela Fiesta rides the twilight line between the living and the dead, a bounty hunter who has collected more souls than she has ever counted, wielding the twin revolvers Wraith and Starfire, accompanied by the memory of the brother she lost.
heroes.fullDescription: She rides the twilight line between the living and the dead, a specter draped in the dust of forgotten trails and the smoke of her own soul‑forged bullets. Shiela Fiesta appears as a woman in her early twenties, yet her features carry the weathered beauty of one who has navigated the lawless territories of the Dark West since she could hold a revolver. Her face is a masterwork of high‑fantasy refinement: a sharp, aristocratic jawline; high, prominent cheekbones; a straight, slender nose. Her skin is fairy‑like in its luminescence, pale and flawless, with a faint, ethereal glow. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, shimmering with pale silver light—cold and unwavering. Her hair is a cascade of deep mahogany past her waist, worn loose. Her physique is classic hourglass: narrow wasp-thin waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, J-cup bust. She wears a bone-white leather duster coat scarred with bullet holes, a tattered bone-white skirt, short cowboy heels, a wide-brimmed faded black hat with a royal blue magical feather. Her weapons are Wraith (silver revolver firing ghostly soul projectiles) and Starfire (royal purple revolver firing laser beams). She possesses a faint ghostly blue aura, the mark of her pact with the spirits of the Dark West.

story.part1: The Dark West is a lawless expanse where the sun bleaches bones and the wind carries whispers of forgotten sins. Shiela Fiesta was born in a dusty town called Redemption's End, the daughter of a bounty hunter who taught her to shoot before she could read. Her brother Clint was her shadow, two years younger, always following her, always trying to match her shots. They were inseparable, the terror of the local outlaws, until the night a gang of revenants—undead gunslingers bound to a demon lord—rode into town. Their father died with a smile, his revolvers empty, his body surrounded by a dozen revenant corpses. Shiela and Clint survived by hiding in the well, listening to the screams of their neighbors. Shiela swore that night: she would hunt every revenant, every demon, every soul that prey on the innocent. Clint swore to follow her.
story.part2: The betrayal came not from an enemy, but from the brother she loved. Clint grew tired of the endless hunt, of the dust and the blood and the nights spent sleeping in graves. He made a deal with the same demon lord who had killed their father: his soul for power, his loyalty for a throne in the Dark West's underworld. Shiela found him standing over the bodies of a family they had been hired to protect, his revolvers smoking, his eyes no longer human. 'Join me,' he said. 'We could rule together.' Shiela raised her own revolver, but she could not pull the trigger. Clint walked away, and Shiela rode into the wastes alone, her heart a wound that would not heal.
story.part3: The spirits found her in the desert, half-dead from thirst and grief. They were the ghosts of bounty hunters who had come before, souls who had been betrayed and abandoned. They offered her a pact: her service in exchange for power that no mortal gunslinger had ever possessed. Shiela accepted. The spirits poured into her, filling her with their memories, their skills, their cold, undying purpose. Her skin began to glow with a faint blue aura, and her revolvers—her father's revolvers—transformed. Wraith could now fire the souls of the departed, projectiles that passed through flesh to strike the spirit itself. Starfire could fire concentrated beams of laser energy, precise and searing. Shiela rose from the desert a new woman: the Shadow Bounty Hunter, a ghost who still drew breath.
story.part4: For decades, Shiela hunted the Dark West, collecting bounties on outlaws, demons, and revenants. She never missed a target, never lost a duel, never showed mercy to those who preyed on the weak. Her name became a curse and a prayer in equal measure—outlaws whispered it before sleeping, and settlers carved it into their doorposts for protection. But she never hunted Clint. She could not. Every time she tracked him, she would find a reason to turn away, to take a different contract, to ride in a different direction. The spirits within her understood; they had loved and lost, too. They did not push her.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the Dark West, and demons are pouring through the rifts. Clint has risen high in the demon lord's ranks, commanding legions of revenants that threaten to overrun the settlements. Shiela knows she can no longer avoid him. She has sharpened her aim, loaded her revolvers, and set out on the trail. She does not know if she will kill him or save him. She does not know if the brother she loved still exists beneath the demon's mask. But she knows that the Dark West needs her, and she will not run again. She rides into the twilight, her blue aura glowing, her feather hat catching the wind, and she whispers to the spirits within: 'If I fall, finish the hunt.' For she is Shiela Fiesta, the Shadow Bounty Hunter, and her mark is finally set.

upper.waist: Wide belt of tarnished brass cinching duster

upper.chest: Full J-cup, prominent, commanding
upper.arms: Lean, corded, built for quick draw

weapons.name: Wraith & Starfire
weapons.fulldesc: Shiela's twin revolvers are legendary across the Dark West. Wraith is a revolver of polished silver, its barrel etched with the names of the dead she has laid to rest. When she fires it, it does not eject ordinary bullets; instead, it releases the souls of the departed, ghostly projectiles that pass through flesh to strike at the spirit itself. The souls scream as they fly, a sound that chills the blood of the living and terrifies the undead. Starfire is a revolver of deep, royal purple, its surface inlaid with veins of gold that glow with an inner light. Starfire fires concentrated beams of laser energy, each shot a precise, searing line of light that can cut through steel and stone. Together, the two revolvers grant her unlimited ammunition—for the dead are never truly gone, and the light of the Dark West never fully fades. She can fire spectral bullets that track their targets across dimensions, possess the bodies of those who underestimate her, and dodge through the ethereal plane itself, becoming smoke and shadow for the span of a heartbeat.
weapons.look: Left: polished silver with etched names; Right: royal purple with gold inlay, glowing inner light
weapons.aura: Ghostly blue and searing purple

passive.name: Soul Ammunition
passive.description: Basic attacks with Wraith deal bonus magic damage equal to 3% of the target's current health. Starfire's basic attacks ignore 15% of the target's armor.

passive.name: Ethereal Dodge
passive.description: After using an ability, Shiela becomes intangible for 0.5 seconds, dodging all incoming damage.

active.name: Soul Seeker (Wraith)
active.description: Fires a homing soul projectile that tracks the target for 3 seconds, dealing 150% magic damage. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Laser Line (Starfire)
active.description: Fires a piercing laser beam in a line, dealing 180% physical damage to all enemies in its path. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

ultimate.name: Ghost Dance
ultimate.description: Shiela enters the ethereal plane for 4 seconds, becoming untargetable and gaining 50% movement speed. While ethereal, she can fire both revolvers simultaneously, and her attacks ignore all armor and magic resist. Cooldown: 75 seconds.

special.name: Bounty Hunter's Mark
special.description: Shiela can mark a target as her bounty, gaining 20% movement speed toward them and dealing 15% bonus damage. Only one bounty at a time.

ratings.notes: Hybrid marksman with high mobility, intangibility, and single-target focus.

skills.name: Spirit Pact
skills.description: Shiela's blue aura heals her for 10 health per second and reveals nearby invisible enemies within 8 meters.

skills.name: Smoke Step
skills.description: Shiela turns into smoke and dashes 10 meters, leaving a cloud that blinds enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Possession
skills.description: Shiela possesses an enemy minion or small creature for 5 seconds, controlling its movement and basic attacks. Cooldown: 25 seconds.

skills.name: Brother's Shadow
skills.description: When Clint is an ally, Shiela gains 10% lifesteal. When Clint is an enemy, her abilities deal 10% more damage to him, but she takes 10% more damage from him.

heroes.lore: Shiela Fiesta was a bounty hunter's daughter who lost her father to revenants and her brother Clint to a demonic pact. She made a pact with the spirits of departed bounty hunters, gaining the power to hunt the supernatural. Now she roams the Dark West, collecting bounties and avoiding the brother she cannot bring herself to kill.

relationships.description: Clint made a deal with a demon and became a revenant lord. Shiela hunts him but cannot bring herself to finish the job.
Both have lost siblings to darkness; they share a quiet understanding when their paths cross.

heroes.specialty: Hybrid marksman/assassin with high mobility, intangibility, and single-target execution potential. Excels at dueling and chasing down marked targets.

heroes.name: Malit Dalit
heroes.shortDescription: A fragment of a dying star who once held the cosmos in his palms and now exists as a whisper between galaxies. Malit Dalit is a forgotten astral mage, his body composed of condensed gas and star energy, his eyes containing galaxies. He works in secret alongside Gord, the new Protector, lending his power to vanquish evils that threaten the balance of existence.
heroes.fullDescription: He drifts through the void like a fragment of a dying star, a being who once held the cosmos in his palms and now exists as a whisper between galaxies. Malit Dalit appears as a man of striking, Germanic features—a strong, square jaw, high cheekbones, a straight, noble nose—yet his face is merely the anchor for something far greater. His skin is human in tone, pale and fair, but cracked with fissures of pure purple energy that trace the meridians of his body like a cosmic river. Through these cracks, one can glimpse the stars that swirl within him. His eyes are windows into the heart of creation: sclera absolute black, irises a swirling maelstrom of violet, gold, and silver, pupils as black holes that drink in light. His hair is a gravity-defying mane of light violet spikes, crackling with static. His physique is massively muscular, dense and corded, built like a warrior. He wears robes of absolute black woven with threads of starlight and nebula hues, with floating obsidian shards orbiting him. His weapon is his very form—his fists strike with the force of a collapsing star, his palms unleash beams of cosmic energy. He rides Stardust, an aerial skateboard of condensed fire and celestial gas.

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the realms of Kretos were torn, there was only the Void and the Stars. Malit Dalit was born from the collapse of a supernova, a consciousness forged in the heart of destruction. For eons, he drifted through the cosmos, learning the language of gravity, the music of radiation, the poetry of entropy. When the first gods emerged from the chaos, they called him 'The Watcher,' for he had seen the birth of a thousand galaxies and the death of a thousand more. He did not seek worship; he sought understanding. And in his understanding, he found purpose: to protect the fragile worlds that bloomed in the darkness, to guard the flame of life against the cold of the void.
story.part2: The title of 'Protector of the Universe' was not given; it was earned. Malit fought cosmic horrors that would drive mortal minds insane, sealed rifts that spewed anti-matter into reality, and guided young civilizations away from self-destruction. He was a silent guardian, never interfering directly, always nudging, always watching. The gods respected him; the demons feared him. But eons are long, and even the most dedicated guardian grows weary. Malit began to fade, not in power, but in purpose. The universe no longer needed him—or so he believed. He retreated to a nebula at the edge of existence, wrapped himself in its gases, and slept for a million years.
story.part3: When he awoke, a new Protector had risen: Gord, a being of similar power but different nature. Gord was active, interventionist, a warrior who fought evil head-on. Malit watched him from the shadows, at first with suspicion, then with respect. Gord was everything Malit had once been, but without the weariness. The old mage made a decision: he would not challenge the new Protector. He would become his shadow, his silent partner, the guardian that no one knew existed. He began to work alongside Gord, appearing only when the danger was greatest, offering his power in ways that could not be traced back to him. Gord never asked who was helping him; he simply accepted the aid, grateful for the cosmic ally he could never seem to catch.
story.part4: The Sundering tore Malit from his comfortable obscurity. The rifts between worlds threatened to unravel the fabric of reality, and even Gord could not be everywhere at once. Malit began to reveal himself more often, appearing to heroes in their darkest moments, offering cryptic advice and devastating cosmic blasts. He became a myth, a legend whispered by the few who had seen him—a man made of stars, his body cracking with purple light, his eyes holding galaxies. He did not seek recognition; he sought results. The rifts began to close. The demons began to retreat. And Malit retreated with them, back into the shadows, back into the silence.
story.part5: Now, Malit Dalit drifts between the stars, Stardust humming beneath his feet, his body pulsing with the light of a billion suns. He knows that the Sundering is not over, that the demons are regrouping, that the realms of Kretos are still in danger. He watches Gord from afar, proud of the younger protector, ready to intervene when needed. He has heard that some mortals have begun to worship him as a god, and he laughs—a sound like the collision of galaxies. He is not a god. He is a relic, a forgotten guardian, a man made of starlight and regret. But he will not rest while the universe needs him. For he is Malit Dalit, the Forgotten Astral Mage, and his vigil has no end.

weapons.name: Stellar Body (Cosmic Fists)
weapons.fulldesc: Malit Dalit's body is composed of condensed gas and star energy, a physical shell that he can dissolve and reform at will. His fists can strike with the force of a collapsing star; his palms can unleash blasts of pure, cosmic energy—what the mortals have come to call a Kamehameha, a focused beam of stellar fire that can erase mountains from existence. He can create gravity wells that crush his enemies into singularities, tear dimensional rifts that swallow armies whole, and nullify the powers of those who dare to challenge him. He is a living weapon, a force of nature given form, and he has not yet found an enemy who could survive the full expression of his power.
weapons.look: Fists glow with purple and gold energy; palms emit stellar beams
weapons.aura: Purple, gold, silver, trailing stardust

passive.name: Cosmic Regeneration
passive.description: Malit regenerates 1% of his max health every second, doubled when out of combat.

passive.name: Void Adaptation
passive.description: Malit takes 25% reduced damage from all sources when in space or cosmic rifts.

active.name: Gravity Well
active.description: Creates a 6-meter zone at target location that pulls enemies toward the center and deals 100 magic damage per second for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Stellar Beam
active.description: Fires a beam of cosmic energy in a line, dealing 200% magic damage to all enemies hit and piercing through them. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Supernova
ultimate.description: Malit detonates his own energy, dealing 500% magic damage in a 25-meter radius and stunning all enemies for 3 seconds. After casting, Malit's body reforms over 5 seconds during which he is invulnerable but cannot act. Cooldown: 120 seconds.

special.name: Cosmic Nullification
special.description: Malit's presence nullifies enemy shields within 15 meters, preventing them from being applied.

ratings.notes: Extremely powerful mage with high sustain, crowd control, and team utility.

skills.name: Eternal Vigil
skills.description: Malit gains 1% magic power for every minute that passes in the match (up to 30%).

skills.name: Dimensional Rift
skills.description: Malit tears a rift at target location, teleporting himself and any allies within 5 meters to the rift's exit after 1 second. Cooldown: 30 seconds.

skills.name: Starfall
skills.description: Calls down 3 stars from the sky at marked locations, each dealing 120% magic damage in a 4-meter radius. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Forgotten Protector
skills.description: When Gord is an ally, Malit's ultimate cooldown is reduced by 20 seconds. When Gord is an enemy, Malit takes 15% less damage from him.

heroes.lore: Malit Dalit was the Protector of the Universe for eons before growing weary and fading into obscurity. When Gord rose as the new Protector, Malit became his silent partner, working from the shadows to help vanquish cosmic threats. He is a forgotten legend, a man made of starlight who still watches over creation.

relationships.description: Malit works alongside Gord from the shadows, never seeking credit or recognition.
They have crossed paths in the cosmic void; she respects his power but finds him too melancholic.

mount.name: Stardust
mount.description: An aerial skateboard forged from condensed fire and celestial gas, surface a shimmering translucent sheet of orange and gold, edges trailing comet tails of ionized particles. Responds to Malit's will, leaving a trail of glittering stardust.
mount.fulldesc: Stardust is an aerial skateboard forged from condensed fire and celestial gas, its surface a shimmering, translucent sheet of orange and gold, its edges trailing comet tails of ionized particles. Malit stands on it with the ease of a surfer on a wave, his robes billowing, his hair streaming behind him, and he moves through the void with a speed that defies physics. Stardust responds to his will, banking and diving, accelerating and stopping on a thought, and it leaves a trail of glittering stardust in its wake—a path of light that fades slowly, like the memory of a shooting star. It is not a living creature, but it is alive in the way that all cosmic constructs are: a piece of Malit's own energy given form, a companion in the endless silence of space.
abilities: Comet Trail – leaves a trail that damages enemies.
Cosmic Speed – increases Malit's movement speed by 40% while riding.
Stardust Burst – creates a cloud of stardust that blinds enemies.

heroes.specialty: Extremely high-damage mage with global presence, crowd control, and team utility. Scales over time and excels in long matches.

heroes.name: Vixyy Joe
heroes.shortDescription: A Spanish temptress who traded her soul for a winning hand and never looked back. Vixyy Joe runs the infernal casino where souls are wagered and fates are sealed, wielding the Ace of Spades—a deck of cursed obsidian cards—and accompanied by a supreme jade owl. She taught Harley everything he knows about magic and deception, and waits for the day when her former student will challenge her.
heroes.fullDescription: She steps out of the swirling smoke of the infernal casino like a hand dealt from the bottom of the deck—a woman whose very existence is a gamble, and who has never lost. Vixyy Joe appears as a vision of sinful beauty, a Spanish temptress who traded her soul for a winning hand and never looked back. Her age is fixed at twenty, yet her eyes hold the cunning of a woman who has cheated death more times than she can count. Her facial structure is a study in sharp, aristocratic elegance: a heart‑shaped face with a delicate pointed chin; high, prominent cheekbones; a straight, slender nose; full blood-red lips perpetually curved in a knowing smirk. Her skin is very fair, almost porcelain, smooth and luminous. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, emerald green that shift and deepen like a panther's. Her hair is a cascade of long black and maroon waves past her shoulders, worn loose. Over her left eye, she wears a gold monocle with an emerald-tinted lens. Her physique is classic hourglass: wasp-thin waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She wears a sharp black coat with blood-red lining, a red leather mini skirt, long black leather boots, a top hat with a silver skull band, and a black velvet choker with an emerald gem. Her weapons are the Ace of Spades—a deck of floating obsidian cards that steal luck and seal soul contracts—and a serpent-headed ebony cane. Her companion is a supreme jade owl, a construct bound to her by contract.

story.part1: Before she was the Queen of the Infernal Casino, Vixyy Joe was a street rat in the back alleys of Barcelona, a girl with nothing but quick fingers and a quicker smile. She learned to pick pockets, to read marks, to stack decks in games where the losers paid with their teeth. By sixteen, she had made a name for herself in the underground gambling dens of Spain—'La Viuda Negra,' the Black Widow, because no one who played against her ever walked away with their winnings. But Vixyy wanted more than gold. She wanted power. She wanted to control the game itself.
story.part2: The demon found her in a backroom poker game, a handsome man with eyes like embers. He offered her a wager: her soul against a deck of cards that could never lose. Vixyy, arrogant and desperate, accepted. The demon dealt. Vixyy drew the Ace of Spades—the card of death, of contracts, of souls. The demon smiled. 'You've won the deck,' he said. 'But the deck comes with a price. Every card you play, every soul you take, binds you deeper to the infernal. In the end, the deck will own you.' Vixyy looked at the Ace of Spades in her hand, felt its power thrumming through her veins, and smiled back. 'Then I'll make sure the game never ends.'
story.part3: The infernal casino rose from the ashes of a burned-out theater, a monument to Vixyy's gamble. She became its queen, dealing cards to souls who wagered years of their lives, their memories, their very existence. She never lost, not because the deck was rigged—though it was—but because she understood that the true game was not in the cards, but in the hearts of her opponents. She learned to read fear, to exploit hope, to turn desperation into profit. The demon who had given her the deck became her occasional partner, occasional rival, but never her master. Vixyy Joe belonged to no one.
story.part4: The child came to her door one night—a scrawny street rat with eyes too old for his face and hands that moved like lightning. Harley was his name, and he had heard of the Queen of the Infernal Casino. He wanted to learn. Vixyy saw herself in him, the same hunger, the same cunning, the same refusal to accept that the game was rigged. She took him in, taught him the art of the deal, the magic of deception, the weight of a soul contract. He was her finest student, her greatest success, and when he left to make his own name, she let him go with a smile and a warning: 'Never gamble what you are not willing to lose.'
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought new players to the infernal casino—demons, angels, heroes, and villains, all seeking advantage in the chaos. Vixyy watches them all, her cards orbiting, her jade owl perched on its stand, her cane tapping a slow rhythm. She has heard that Harley has become a legend, that he has made deals with powers that even she would hesitate to approach. She is proud of him, and a little afraid. Because she knows that one day, her former student will come back. He will want to challenge her, to prove that he has surpassed the master. And when that day comes, Vixyy will deal the cards, and the game will begin. She does not know who will win. For the first time in centuries, the odds are uncertain. And that, she thinks, is the most exciting gamble of all.

lower.feet: Long black leather boots, high sharp heels, pointed toes

attire.headwear: Top hat, dented crown, wide brim, gold band with small silver skull
accessories: Gold rings on fingers (rubies and emeralds)
attire.distinctFeatures: Floating deck of obsidian cards orbiting her; serpent-headed ebony cane

upper.chest: Full G-cup, prominent

weapons.name: Ace of Spades
weapons.fulldesc: The Ace of Spades is a deck of cards that floats around Vixyy like a school of deadly fish, each card a sliver of cursed potential. The cards are not paper; they are thin slivers of obsidian, etched with symbols of luck and fate, and they orbit her in a constant, shifting halo. With a flick of her wrist, she can send them flying like razors, each one capable of stealing luck, draining life, or sealing a soul contract. The Ace of Spades itself—the card that started it all—she keeps tucked into the band of her top hat, its face a grinning skull wreathed in emerald fire. The deck is bound to her soul; as long as she holds the Ace, she cannot be killed—but every card she plays brings her closer to the infernal bargain's final payment.
weapons.look: Obsidian cards orbiting, etched with luck and fate symbols; Ace shows grinning skull with emerald fire
weapons.aura: Green and black, trailing smoke

passive.name: House Advantage
passive.description: Vixyy gains 5% critical strike chance and 5% dodge chance for each enemy within 10 meters, up to 25%.

passive.name: Soul Contract
passive.description: When Vixyy kills an enemy, she gains a stack of Soul Debt. At 5 stacks, she can activate a special ability (see ultimate).

active.name: Card Throw
active.description: Throws three obsidian cards in a spread, each dealing 70% physical damage. Enemies hit lose 10% movement speed for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 5 seconds.

active.name: Luck Drain
active.description: Selects an enemy, reducing their critical strike chance by 50% for 4 seconds and granting Vixyy that critical chance for the same duration. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Ace in the Hole
ultimate.description: Vixyy reveals the Ace of Spades from her hat, dealing 300% true damage to a single target. If the target dies, Vixyy's Soul Debt stacks reset and she gains a permanent 5% damage bonus for the rest of the match (stacks up to 3 times). Cooldown: 60 seconds. Requires 5 Soul Debt stacks.

special.name: Stacked Deck
special.description: Vixyy's basic attacks have a 15% chance to fire an additional card, dealing 50% damage.

ratings.notes: Ranged carry with scaling damage, critical manipulation, and execution potential.

skills.name: Lady Luck
skills.description: Vixyy's basic attacks have a 10% chance to deal double damage and refund the attack's cooldown (if any).

skills.name: Wild Card
skills.description: Vixyy draws a random card, gaining a temporary buff: Heart (heal), Diamond (gold), Club (armor), Spade (damage). Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Fold
skills.description: Vixyy becomes untargetable for 1.5 seconds, leaving behind a decoy card that explodes for 100% damage after the duration. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Student's Challenge
skills.description: When Harley is an ally, Vixyy's cooldowns are reduced by 10%. When Harley is an enemy, both take 10% increased damage from each other.

heroes.lore: Vixyy Joe was a street rat who won a cursed deck of cards from a demon, trading her soul for the power to never lose. She runs the infernal casino, teaching deception and magic to those who seek her out. Her finest student was Harley, who left to make his own legend. Vixyy waits for his return, uncertain who will win their inevitable confrontation.

relationships.description: Vixyy taught Harley everything he knows. She is proud of him, but knows he will one day challenge her.
The demon who gave her the deck occasionally visits. Their relationship is a tense standoff of mutual respect and suspicion.

pet.name: Supreme Jade Owl
pet.description: A supreme jade owl, feathers carved from a single massive piece of polished jade, eyes two glowing emerald orbs. Not living but not merely a statue; a construct bound to Vixyy by a contract older than her own. Can speak in a voice like rustling leaves, can fly, its screech shatters glass and rattles souls.
pet.fulldesc: The supreme jade owl is Vixyy's most prized possession, a construct that has been with her since she won the infernal casino. Its feathers are carved from a single, massive piece of polished jade, each plume distinct, each edge sharp. Its eyes are two glowing, emerald orbs that see through all deception—lies, illusions, even the hidden cards in an opponent's hand. The owl is not a living creature, but it is not merely a statue either; it is a familiar, bound to her by a contract older than her own. It can speak, in a voice like rustling leaves, and it offers her counsel when the odds are uncertain. It can fly, its wings spreading wide, its jade feathers catching the light, and when danger threatens, it can unleash a screech that shatters glass and rattles the souls of the living. It is her only confidant, the one being in the universe she trusts with her secrets, and it watches over her with the silent, patient wisdom of a creature that has witnessed the rise and fall of a thousand gamblers.
abilities: All-Seeing Eye – reveals hidden enemies and traps within 15 meters.
Soul Screech – stuns all enemies within 8 meters for 1 second.
Jade Shield – grants Vixyy a shield that absorbs 200 damage.

heroes.specialty: Critical-based marksman with scaling damage, luck manipulation, and execution potential. Excels in extended fights and high-stakes duels.

heroes.name: Axe Blade
heroes.shortDescription: A mountain carved from the heart of a storm, a being of raw primal power tempered by an unbreakable sense of duty. Axe Blade is the orc king who once sought to level all civilization—until he saw the cost. Now he wanders the world righting the wrongs committed by his brother Balmond, waiting for the day when he can bring the destroyer back from the darkness.
heroes.fullDescription: He stands like a mountain carved from the heart of a storm, a being of raw, primal power tempered by an unbreakable sense of duty. Axe Blade appears as an orc of immense stature, yet where his brother Balmond is a crimson‑tanned engine of destruction, Axe Blade is a figure of regal, jade‑blue majesty. His skin is rough, textured like the bark of an ancient oak, yet it gleams with a faint, metallic sheen, the color of deep sea ice mixed with the pale blue of a winter sky. Tribal scars crisscross his chest and arms, each one a record of a battle fought to protect the weak, and his muscles bulge with the density of forged iron. His face is a map of honorable combat: a network of scars from brow to jaw, tusks painted with white war marks, hair styled in a samurai topknot of thick black hair. His eyes are deep royal blue, burning with quiet fire. He wears a white fur loincloth robe, half-torn blue pants, Shinto-style silver and royal blue armor, a necklace of skeletal fingers from demon lords he has slain. His weapon is the Cataclysm, a giant great battle axe that can split the earth and throw like a blazing comet. He has no mount, for he walks the earth as a savior.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering, when the orc tribes of the badlands were scattered and warring, two brothers were born to the chieftain of the Jade Clan. The elder was Balmond—crimson-skinned, hot-tempered, born with a rage that would one day consume him. The younger was Axe Blade—jade-blue, calm, born with a stillness that belied his power. They were inseparable, Balmond the sword, Axe the shield. Together, they united the orc tribes under a single banner, forging an empire that stretched from the eastern badlands to the western sea. But power corrupts, and Balmond began to dream of conquest, of burning cities, of ruling over ash. Axe tried to counsel him, to remind him of the orcish code: 'Strength is for protection, not destruction.' Balmond laughed and called his brother weak.
story.part2: The breaking point came during the Siege of Aethelgard. Balmond led the orc army against the celestial city, burning villages, slaughtering civilians, reveling in the chaos. Axe followed, not as a soldier, but as a conscience. He saw a child crying over her mother's body, a family huddled in a burning barn, a priest trying to shield his congregation. And he understood that his brother had become the very evil they had once sworn to fight. He confronted Balmond on the steps of the celestial temple, and they fought—brother against brother, axe against axe. Axe won, but he could not kill his brother. He banished him instead, and the orc empire shattered into warring factions once more.
story.part3: Axe Blade walked away from the ruins of his empire, his heart heavy with grief. He had failed his brother, failed his people, failed the code. For decades, he wandered the world, saving villages from bandits, protecting caravans from monsters, healing the wounds that his brother's rampages had caused. He never took a kingdom, never sought followers. He was a shadow, a myth, a green-skinned ghost who appeared in times of crisis and vanished when the danger passed. The people of Kretos began to call him 'The World-Savior,' not knowing that he was also the brother of the World-Destroyer.
story.part4: The Cataclysm axe was forged in the heart of a dying volcano, a weapon of such power that it could level mountains. Axe had taken it from Balmond after their duel, hoping to prevent its use. But the axe was not evil; it was a tool, and tools are only as good or bad as the hands that wield them. Axe learned to use the Cataclysm for salvation—splitting the earth to create barriers against floods, throwing it like a comet to destroy demonic portals, spinning it in a divine whirlwind to protect the innocent. The axe sang in his hands, a weapon of destruction turned to creation.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the world, and Balmond has returned, leading demonic legions in a campaign of annihilation. Axe watches from the shadows, tracking his brother's movements, saving those he can, mourning those he cannot. He knows that the final battle is coming, that he will have to face Balmond again, that one of them will not walk away. He does not want to kill his brother; he wants to save him. He dreams of a day when Balmond will lower his axe, will see the cost of his rage, will remember the oath they swore as children. Until that day, Axe Blade walks the world, the Cataclysm heavy on his shoulder, his brother's name a wound that will not heal. He is the World-Savior Orc King, and his greatest battle is not against demons, but against the hope that his brother can still be saved.
