upper.arms: Bare, muscular, tribal scars, silver bracers on forearms
upper.shoulders: Silver pauldrons shaped like crane wings
upper.neck: Necklace of skeletal demon fingers on braided leather
upper.waist: Silver link belt with leather pouches

lower.hips: Half-torn pants of deep royal blue, frayed edges

attire.distinctFeatures: White war paint on tusks; samurai-style topknot; jade-blue skin

upper.chest: Barrel of corded sinew, dense muscle

weapons.name: Cataclysm
weapons.fulldesc: The Cataclysm is a giant great battle axe that has become synonymous with Axe Blade's name. The axe is taller than he is, its shaft a length of dark, petrified wood wrapped in silver wire for grip, its blade a massive, crescent‑shaped slab of enchanted steel that gleams with a pale, blue light. The blade is wide, its edge sharp enough to split a hair, its back thick and heavy, capable of crushing stone. Etched into the metal are ancient inscriptions, runes of power that flare with brilliant, white lightning when he channels his strength. With the Cataclysm, Axe Blade can split the earth itself, sending tremors that level mountains; he can throw it like a blazing comet, its edge trailing fire that consumes the impure; and he can spin it in a divine whirlwind that cuts through armies like a scythe through wheat. The axe is not merely a weapon; it is an extension of his will, a tool of salvation that he wields with the precision of a surgeon and the force of a god.
weapons.look: Massive crescent blade of enchanted steel with blue glow, petrified wood shaft wrapped in silver, runes that flare with lightning
weapons.aura: Blue and white lightning, divine radiance

passive.name: World-Savior's Resolve
passive.description: Axe Blade gains 5 armor and magic resist for each nearby ally, up to 25.

passive.name: Jade Clan Legacy
passive.description: Axe Blade's abilities deal 15% bonus damage to demon-type enemies.

active.name: Earth Splitter
active.description: Slams the Cataclysm into the ground, creating a fissure that travels 12 meters, dealing 180% physical damage and knocking up enemies hit. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Comet Throw
active.description: Throws the Cataclysm in a line, dealing 150% physical damage to all enemies in its path. The axe returns to Axe Blade after 2 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Divine Whirlwind
ultimate.description: Axe Blade spins the Cataclysm around him for 4 seconds, dealing 80% physical damage per second to all nearby enemies and pulling them toward him. While spinning, he is immune to crowd control. Cooldown: 75 seconds.

special.name: Brother's Keeper
special.description: When Balmond is an ally, Axe Blade's attacks heal Balmond for 5% of damage dealt. When Balmond is an enemy, Axe Blade's damage against him is increased by 20%, but he takes 20% reduced damage from Balmond.

ratings.notes: Tanky fighter with high sustain, crowd control, and anti-demon capabilities.

skills.name: Savior's Aura
skills.description: Allies within 10 meters gain 10% damage reduction and 10 health regeneration per second.

skills.name: Jade Charge
skills.description: Axe Blade charges forward 10 meters, damaging and stunning the first enemy hit for 1 second. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Tribal Resilience
skills.description: Axe Blade removes all debuffs from himself and gains 30% damage reduction for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Hope for Redemption
skills.description: If Axe Blade kills Balmond with his ultimate, Balmond is not permanently killed but instead respawns at base with a 30-second debuff of reduced damage. (Lore-accurate: Axe cannot bring himself to truly kill his brother.)

heroes.lore: Axe Blade was once a conqueror alongside his brother Balmond, but after seeing the cost of their rampage, he turned against his brother and became a wandering savior. He now protects the innocent from the very destruction his brother wreaks, hoping to one day redeem Balmond.

relationships.description: Axe Blade loves his brother and wants to save him from darkness, but knows he may have to stop him permanently.
They have fought alongside each other against demonic incursions; Axe respects Alucard's dedication to protecting the innocent.

heroes.specialty: Tanky fighter with team protection, crowd control, and anti-demon specialization. Excels at initiating fights and protecting allies.

dialogue.selection: I will save this world, brother or no.

heroes.name: Taurus
heroes.shortDescription: A divine minotaur who guards the labyrinth that contains his twin brother Minotaur. Taurus was chosen by the gods to be a Zodiac guardian, but refused immortality to stay with his brother. Now he walks the endless corridors of his own creation, waiting for the day when his brother might emerge from the darkness.
heroes.fullDescription: He stands at the center of an eternal maze, a guardian whose duty has become his prison, whose love for his brother has become his eternal penance. Taurus appears as a being of divine, celestial majesty—a minotaur who was chosen not for destruction, but for the sacred task of containing it. His form is massive, a towering edifice of muscle and fur that seems carved from the very stone of the labyrinth he guards. His fur is thick and lush, a deep jade green with a golden undertone. His physique is a monument to divine craftsmanship: vast shoulders, barrel chest, thick arms, narrow waist. His face is hidden behind a divine helm of gleaming silver and light gold, sculpted to resemble a celestial bull, with jade-tipped horns. His eyes are visible, burning with steady golden light. He wears full plate armor of silver and light gold with jade inlay, adorned with divine accessories—chains, bangles, a torc. His weapon is the Labyrinth's Heart, a massive warhammer that can reshape the maze at will. He has no mount, for he is the keeper of the labyrinth.

story.part1: Before the gods raised their champions to the stars, there were two twin brothers born to a divine bull and a mortal queen. The first was Taurus—calm, patient, born with the wisdom of ages. The second was Minotaur—fierce, hungry, born with a rage that would consume him. They were inseparable as children, playing in the fields of their mother's kingdom, learning the ways of the hunt from their father. But Minotaur's rage grew as he did, fed by the whispers of demons who saw in him a weapon. By the time they were adults, Minotaur had become a monster in truth—not in form, but in heart. He killed without reason, destroyed without purpose, and the people cried out for his death.
story.part2: The gods came to Taurus with an offer: become a Zodiac guardian, ascend to the stars, and be remembered for eternity. In exchange, they would deal with Minotaur. Taurus refused. 'He is my brother,' he said. 'I will not let you kill him.' The gods, impressed by his loyalty, offered another way: build a labyrinth that would contain Minotaur forever, and Taurus would be its keeper. Taurus agreed. He built the maze with his own hands, weaving passages that twisted and turned, sealing the walls with magic that not even a god could break. He led his brother into the heart of the labyrinth, and there he left him, weeping, as the walls closed around them both.
story.part3: For centuries, Taurus walked the labyrinth, maintaining its walls, sealing the cracks that time and demonic influence created. He never saw his brother—Minotaur had descended into the deepest levels, where even the light of Taurus's divine armor could not reach. But he felt him there, raging against his chains, his hunger growing with each passing year. The gods came again, offering to raise Taurus to the Zodiac, to give him a place among the celestial guardians. Taurus refused again. 'I will not leave him alone in the dark,' he said. The gods shook their heads and departed, leaving Taurus to his duty.
story.part4: The Sundering shook the labyrinth to its foundations. New passages opened, old ones collapsed, and for the first time in centuries, Minotaur's roar echoed through the halls. Taurus fought to contain the damage, sealing rifts, rebuilding walls, pushing back the demonic forces that sought to free his brother. He has not slept in months, not rested in years. His armor is dented, his warhammer chipped, his divine light flickering. But he does not stop. He cannot stop. If Minotaur escapes, the world will burn.
story.part5: Now, Taurus stands at a crossroads. He has heard whispers that a hero—a mortal with a pure heart—is seeking the labyrinth, hoping to slay Minotaur and end the threat forever. Taurus does not know whether to let them pass or to turn them away. Part of him wants his brother's suffering to end, wants the burden of being the warden to be lifted. But another part—the part that still remembers playing in the fields with his twin—hopes that Minotaur can be saved, not killed. He waits in the center of the maze, the Labyrinth's Heart heavy in his hands, and he prays to the gods who abandoned him: 'Let me find another way. Let me bring my brother home.' For he is Taurus, the Labyrinth Keeper, and his vigil will not end until the stars themselves burn out.

weapons.name: Labyrinth's Heart
weapons.fulldesc: The Labyrinth's Heart is a warhammer of such immense size that Taurus must grip it with both hands to lift it, its head alone twice the size of his own. The hammer is a masterpiece of divine architecture, its form echoing the great temples and palaces of the celestial realm. The shaft is a length of petrified wood from the first tree that grew in the center of the labyrinth, wrapped in silver wire for grip, its surface carved with the story of his failure and his redemption. The head is massive, a block of light gold and silver, its faces etched with the twisting paths of the maze, its edges sharp enough to cleave stone, its back heavy enough to crush the strongest armor. At its center, a sphere of jade rotates slowly, glowing with a soft, green light that pulses with the heartbeat of the labyrinth itself. When he strikes the ground with the Labyrinth's Heart, new passages open; when he raises it to the sky, the walls of his domain rise higher; when he charges, the hammer trails light, and the very earth trembles at his approach.
weapons.look: Massive gold and silver head with labyrinth etchings, jade sphere at center, petrified wood shaft wrapped in silver
weapons.aura: Green and gold light, pulsing with labyrinth's heartbeat

passive.name: Labyrinth Walker
passive.description: Taurus ignores unit collision and gains 20% movement speed when moving through narrow passages or jungle terrain.

passive.name: Warden's Vigil
passive.description: Taurus takes 15% reduced damage from enemies he has recently attacked.

active.name: Seal Passage
active.description: Taurus slams the hammer, creating a wall of stone in a 6-meter line that lasts 4 seconds. The wall blocks enemy movement and projectiles. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

active.name: Maze Shift
active.description: Taurus teleports to a nearby location, leaving behind a copy of himself that taunts enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Heart of the Labyrinth
ultimate.description: Taurus channels for 1 second, then creates a 20-meter radius maze zone for 8 seconds. Enemies inside have their vision reduced to 5 meters, their movement speed reduced by 40%, and they cannot use dashes or blinks. Allies inside gain 30% movement speed and see through the maze. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Brother's Echo
special.description: When Minotaur is an ally, Taurus's ultimate also reduces damage taken by Minotaur by 30%. When Minotaur is an enemy, Taurus's attacks against him apply a stacking slow (up to 60%).

ratings.notes: Extremely durable tank with zone control, terrain manipulation, and team utility.

skills.name: Guardian's Aura
skills.description: Allies within 8 meters of Taurus gain 15 armor and magic resist.

skills.name: Horn Charge
skills.description: Taurus charges forward 12 meters, impaling the first enemy hit and carrying them for the duration, then stunning them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Labyrinth's Blessing
skills.description: Taurus blesses an ally, granting them 20% damage reduction and 20% movement speed for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Eternal Vigil
skills.description: Taurus cannot be killed by Minotaur's attacks while Minotaur is under 10% health (prevents accidental fratricide).

heroes.lore: Taurus was chosen by the gods to be a Zodiac guardian, but refused immortality to stay with his twin brother Minotaur, whom he sealed in a labyrinth. He now walks the maze as its keeper, waiting for the day his brother might be saved.

relationships.description: Taurus sealed Minotaur in the labyrinth and guards him out of love, not hatred.
The other Zodiac guardians respect Taurus's choice, though they consider him tragic.

heroes.specialty: Tank with zone control, terrain manipulation, and team protection. Excels at controlling team fights and protecting objectives.

heroes.name: Ecanju Ferosta
titles: The Radiant Priestess of Divine Order
Reincarnation of the Goddess of Light
Nemesis of the Queen of Suffering
heroes.shortDescription: She descends upon the world like the first ray of dawn breaking through an endless night—a being of such pure, radiant beauty that even the shadows recoil from her presence. A Japanese nun elevated to the status of a living saint, Ecanju Ferosta is the reincarnation of the goddess of light, the eternal nemesis of Alice, the Queen of Suffering. She has died a hundred times and been reborn a hundred more, her soul burning brighter with each resurrection, her purpose clearer with each passing age.
heroes.fullDescription: In the celestial order of Kretos, Ecanju Ferosta is not merely a priestess—she is a living weapon forged by the divine to oppose the darkness that Alice represents. Born from the light of the first dawn, her soul has been reincarnated across a hundred lifetimes, each death a sacrifice to protect the innocent, each rebirth a testament to the unbreakable will of the light. She wields the ZeinZui, a holy staff that channels the power of dying stars, and commands GodRyu, the Celestial Eastern Dragon King, a creature of such majesty that his roar shakes the foundations of the world. Her beauty is a weapon, her faith a shield, and her purpose is absolute: to face Alice in a final battle that will decide the fate of all worlds. She does not hate her nemesis; she pities her, for she knows that the Queen of Suffering was once a being of light, twisted by grief and betrayal. And she waits, patient and eternal, for the day when she can offer Alice redemption—or, if redemption is impossible, the peace of final death.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering, when the realms of Kretos were still being shaped from the primordial chaos, a goddess of pure light was born from the first dawn. She had no name then—only purpose. She walked the newborn worlds, blessing the first forests with warmth, teaching the first creatures to bask in the sun, and singing the stars into their eternal orbits. She was the Light, and where she walked, darkness fled. But the Light was not alone. In the shadows of that first dawn, another being stirred—a being of shadow and sorrow, born from the grief of a universe that had not yet learned to heal. That being would one day be called Alice, the Queen of Suffering, and she was the Light's shadow, her twin, her opposite, her eternal adversary. They did not know each other then; they only knew that they were drawn together, light and shadow, two halves of a whole that could never be united. And so began the eternal dance, the endless cycle of death and rebirth, that would span a hundred lifetimes and shape the fate of Kretos itself.
story.part2: The first time Ecanju Ferosta died, she was not yet called by that name. She was a priestess in the Celestial Order, serving in the great cathedral of Aethelgard when the first rift to Nyxara opened. Demons poured through, their shadows blotting out the sun, and the priestess raised her staff to hold them back. She fought for three days and three nights, her holy light burning brighter with each passing hour, until her body could take no more. She fell, her staff clattering on the marble floor, and the demons surged forward—only to recoil as her body dissolved into light, scattering like a thousand fireflies. The priestess was gone, but her soul was not. It rose, carried by the winds of fate, and found a new vessel: a newborn child in a distant village, a girl with golden eyes and a birthmark shaped like a sunburst. The child would grow to be Ecanju Ferosta, the Radiant Priestess, and she would remember, in the depths of her soul, the sacrifice of her first life. The demons were pushed back, the rift was sealed, and the legend of the reincarnating light began.
story.part3: The hundredth death was the hardest. Ecanju had faced Alice before—a dozen times, a score of times—and each time, the Queen of Suffering had been too powerful, too consumed by her own grief to see the light that still flickered within her. But the hundredth battle was different. It took place in the ruins of the Moonlight Kingdom, where Alice had slaughtered an entire dynasty of elves in a single night. Ecanju arrived to find the silver halls stained with blood, the bodies of children still warm in their mothers' arms. Her rage was unlike anything she had felt in her previous lives—not the cold, righteous fury of a goddess, but the hot, desperate anger of a woman who had seen too much suffering. She fought Alice with a ferocity that surprised even herself, her staff blazing with the light of a dying star, her dragon roaring with a fury that cracked the sky. In the end, she lost. Alice's shadow blade pierced her heart, and she fell, her blood pooling on the blood-stained marble. But as she died, she reached out and touched Alice's cheek, and for a single, fleeting moment, the Queen of Suffering's eyes cleared. She saw not an enemy, but a sister. And then Ecanju was gone, her soul once again carried on the winds, and Alice was left alone in the darkness, weeping.
story.part4: The scholars of the Natural Balance have studied Ecanju's reincarnations for centuries, and they have concluded that her power grows with each death. The light that burns within her is not diminished by the destruction of her body; it is refined, purified, made brighter by the sacrifice. Each time she dies, she returns stronger, faster, more radiant than before. Her enemies fear her not because she is invincible, but because she is inevitable. She cannot be killed permanently; she cannot be stopped; she can only be delayed. The demons of Nyxara whisper her name as a curse, for they know that every time they strike her down, she will rise again, her light brighter, her purpose clearer. The angels of Aethelgard sing her name as a prayer, for they know that as long as she exists, there is hope. And Alice, the Queen of Suffering, knows that one day, Ecanju will face her for the final time—and on that day, the cycle will end, one way or another.
story.part5: The truth, known only to the oldest gods of Kretos, is that Ecanju Ferosta and Alice are not merely enemies—they are two halves of a single soul, split at the moment of creation. The Light and the Shadow were never meant to be separate; they were meant to be balanced, to dance together in eternal harmony. But something went wrong in the Sundering—a wound in the fabric of reality that tore the soul in two, creating the goddess of light and the queen of suffering. Ecanju does not know this truth; she only knows that she must fight, must sacrifice, must die and be reborn until the balance is restored. But deep in her soul, in the space between heartbeats, she feels the pull of something she cannot name—a longing, a loss, a love that she cannot explain. It is the shadow calling to the light, the sister reaching for her twin. And when the final battle comes, Ecanju will not raise her staff to destroy Alice. She will raise it to embrace her, to offer her the redemption that she has sought across a hundred lifetimes. For she is the Radiant Priestess of Divine Order, and her purpose is not to kill—it is to heal.

weapons.name: ZeinZui
weapons.fulldesc: The ZeinZui is a holy staff of exquisite craftsmanship, appearing as a length of polished white wood that spirals gently, its surface carved with the stories of a thousand holy wars. At its head, the staff blossoms into a crown of golden leaves, each leaf etched with a single, luminous rune, and cradled within the leaves is a sphere of pure, white light—the captured essence of a dying star, purified and sanctified by the goddess's own hand. The staff is taller than its wielder, yet she carries it with the ease of a shepherd's crook. When Ecanju raises the ZeinZui, the sphere blazes with holy fire, and from it pour rays of healing light that can mend the most grievous wounds, resurrect the fallen, and scour the darkness from the souls of the damned. When she strikes the ground with it, waves of pure energy radiate outward, buffeting her allies with divine strength and sending demons fleeing in terror. The staff hums with a low, resonant note when active—a sound like a choir singing in the distance—and the air around it shimmers with heatless light.
weapons.look: White spiraling wood, golden leaf crown at head, floating sphere of captured starlight within; runes glow with soft, golden light; warm to touch
weapons.aura: Golden and white, with rays of healing light

passive.name: Aura of Radiance
passive.description: Allies within 15 meters gain 10% increased healing received and 5% damage reduction. Against shadow or demon-aligned enemies, the aura also deals 2% of their max health as holy damage per second.

passive.name: Eternal Rebirth
passive.description: Upon taking fatal damage, Ecanju has a 30% chance to instead survive with 1 HP and become invulnerable for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 180 seconds. This effect is guaranteed to trigger once per reincarnation cycle.

active.name: Ray of Purification
active.description: Fires a beam of holy light in a straight line, dealing 150% magical damage to enemies and healing allies for 200% of Ecanju's magic power. Passes through all targets in its path. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Divine Intervention
active.description: Target ally is shielded for 300% of Ecanju's magic power for 5 seconds. If the shield breaks, it explodes, dealing 100% magical damage to nearby enemies. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

ultimate.name: Final Dawn
ultimate.description: Ecanju channels the full power of the ZeinZui for 2 seconds, then unleashes a massive explosion of holy light in a 30-meter radius. Allies within the radius are healed for 500% of her magic power and cleansed of all debuffs. Enemies take 400% magical damage and are stunned for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 120 seconds. Passive: Upon activation, the spirit of her previous reincarnation manifests, granting all allies 20% increased damage for 10 seconds.

special.name: Light of Redemption
special.description: When Ecanju dies, she leaves behind a patch of radiant light. Allies who stand in this light heal for 10% of their max health per second. After 5 seconds, the light coalesces into a beacon—if an ally touches the beacon within 10 seconds, Ecanju is resurrected with 50% HP and mana.

ratings.notes: Overwhelming magical support with unmatched healing and resurrection capabilities. Her reincarnation passive makes her incredibly difficult to permanently eliminate.

skills.name: Divine Radiance
skills.description: Ecanju's basic attacks and abilities apply a stack of Radiance to enemies (max 5 stacks). Each stack reduces the enemy's dark resistance by 5% and deals 2% of her magic power as holy damage per second.

skills.name: Blessing of the Dawn
skills.description: Ecanju blesses a target ally, increasing their damage and healing output by 25% for 8 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Shield of Faith
skills.description: Ecanju creates a barrier of holy light around herself, absorbing 400 damage + 100% of her magic power. While the shield holds, she is immune to crowd control. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Judgment of the Goddess
skills.description: When Ecanju falls below 20% HP, she unleashes a pulse of light that knocks back all enemies within 10 meters and heals all allies within the same radius for 30% of their max HP. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: In the celestial order of Kretos, Ecanju Ferosta is not merely a priestess—she is a living weapon forged by the divine to oppose the darkness that Alice represents. Born from the light of the first dawn, her soul has been reincarnated across a hundred lifetimes, each death a sacrifice to protect the innocent, each rebirth a testament to the unbreakable will of the light...

relationships.description: Two halves of a single soul, split at creation—light and shadow, destined to clash across a hundred lifetimes. Ecanju seeks to redeem Alice, not destroy her.
The Celestial Eastern Dragon King, bound to Ecanju since her first reincarnation. He is her protector, her brother in arms, and the only being who has witnessed all of her deaths.

mount.name: GodRyu
mount.description: A creature of breathtaking majesty, GodRyu is a hundred lengths of sinuous, white-scaled muscle, his horns a crown of golden light, his mane a cascade of silver flame. His eyes are twin suns of pure gold, and when he opens his mouth, the roar that emerges shakes the foundations of the world. He wears holy divine armor across his chest, shoulders, and tail—plates of gold and silver etched with runes of protection. He can summon others of his kind with a thought, calling forth a legion of celestial serpents to fight at his mistress's command.
mount.fulldesc: GodRyu is not merely a mount; he is a guardian, a companion, and a brother in arms to Ecanju Ferosta. He can take human form when he wishes—a tall, regal man with flowing white hair and golden eyes, dressed in robes of silver and white—but he prefers to remain in his dragon form, circling above Ecanju, watching over her with the fierce devotion of a creature who has served the light since before the first human drew breath. When she prays, he coils around her, his massive head resting on the ground beside her, his golden eyes half-closed in contentment. When she fights, he descends like a comet, his claws rending, his breath a torrent of holy flame, his very presence a barrier that the darkness cannot cross. His scales shimmer with an inner light that pulses in time with her heartbeat, and his roar—a sound like thunder and the crash of waves—can shatter the morale of entire armies. He has been with her through all hundred of her deaths, and he will be with her at the final battle, when the light and shadow finally unite.
abilities: Holy Flame Breath – Torrent of white-gold fire that deals 300% magical damage and purifies dark magic.
Celestial Roar – Emits a roar that stuns all enemies within 20 meters for 2 seconds and reduces their armor by 30%.
Legion Call – Summons 5 lesser celestial dragons to fight for 30 seconds (cooldown: 180 seconds).
Wings of Light – Can fly at supersonic speeds, leaving a trail of golden light that heals allies who pass through it.

heroes.specialty: Support / Healer / Resurrection specialist with powerful anti-darkness capabilities and reincarnation mechanics.

dialogue.greeting: The light welcomes you, child. Have you come to seek redemption?
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Let the final dawn break upon you!
dialogue.death: I will return... as I always have...
dialogue.respawn: A hundred deaths, a hundred rebirths. The light endures.

heroes.name: Longwei
heroes.shortDescription: He sits upon a throne of obsidian and gold, a being whose mere presence commands the reverence of empires and the terror of his enemies. Longwei is the Dragon Emperor, the Tyrant of Scales, a man whose features blend celestial beauty with draconic majesty. He is the father of Zilong, though his son does not know it—raised in secrecy, hidden from the machinations of the court, allowed to grow strong on his own. And Longwei watches, proud and patient, guiding his son's path with subtle hands.
heroes.fullDescription: Longwei is the pinnacle of dragon-blooded nobility, a ruler who has held the Celestial Throne for centuries. His power is immense—he can transform at will into a golden dragon of terrifying majesty, his breath a river of poison-green fire, his wings vast enough to blot out the sun. Yet he prefers to rule as a man, to walk among his people, to watch over his son from a distance. Zilong does not know that the Dragon Emperor is his father; he was raised in secrecy, hidden from the machinations of the court, allowed to grow strong on his own. And Longwei watches, proud and patient, guiding his son's path with subtle hands, testing him with trials that will make him stronger, protecting him from threats that would crush a lesser warrior. One day, the secret will be revealed. One day, father and son will stand together, dragon and dragon-blood, and the world will tremble at the joining of their power. Until that day, Longwei waits, patient as stone, fierce as fire, the father who loves in silence, the emperor who protects from the shadows.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering, when the dragon-blooded clans of Kretos first rose to power, there was a prince named Longwei. He was the third son of the Celestial Emperor, a boy of unremarkable birth and unpromising future, destined to serve his elder brothers and fade into obscurity. But the dragons had other plans. On the night of his sixteenth birthday, a celestial dragon descended from the heavens and marked him—a burning brand on his chest, the sigil of the Dragon Emperor. His brothers, consumed by jealousy, conspired to kill him. They ambushed him in the jade gardens of the palace, their blades gleaming with poison, their hearts black with betrayal. Longwei fought back, but he was outnumbered, outmatched, and as he fell to his knees, the brand on his chest ignited. He transformed—not into the golden dragon he would one day become, but into something far more terrible: a creature of raw, unbridled fury, scales of obsidian and gold, eyes of molten fire. When the slaughter was over, his brothers lay dead, their bodies scattered across the gardens like broken dolls. Longwei wept over them, for he had not wanted this. But the dragon's blood had spoken, and the throne was his.
story.part2: The first decades of Longwei's reign were a bloodbath. The noble houses, fearful of the young Dragon Emperor, rose against him in a rebellion that consumed the eastern provinces. He met them on the fields of the Jade River, his army outnumbered five to one, his generals urging him to retreat. Longwei refused. He walked to the front lines alone, unarmed, and faced the enemy general—a towering warrior clad in black iron, his sword stained with the blood of Longwei's loyalists. The general laughed and raised his blade. Longwei did not move. He did not flinch. He simply looked at the general, and his eyes began to glow. The transformation was instantaneous: scales erupted from his skin, wings tore through his robes, and in the space of a heartbeat, the Dragon Emperor stood before the enemy army—a golden dragon of such terrifying majesty that the horses bolted and the soldiers dropped their weapons. He breathed once, and the general's army turned to ash. He roared once, and the rebel lords fled. From that day forward, no one dared to challenge the Dragon Emperor openly. They schemed, they plotted, they whispered in the shadows—but they did not fight.
story.part3: The greatest secret of Longwei's reign is not political—it is personal. In his youth, before the dragon's blood consumed him, he loved a woman. Her name was Lian, a warrior of the northern tribes, fierce and free, with hair like a raven's wing and eyes like winter stars. They met on the battlefield, fought to a draw, and fell in love in the space between sword strokes. She was his heart, his anchor, the only person who saw the man beneath the dragon. When she gave birth to their son, Zilong, Longwei wept with joy—and with terror. For he knew that his enemies would stop at nothing to destroy the heir to the Celestial Throne. So he made the hardest choice of his life: he sent Lian and Zilong away, into hiding, and he erased every record of their existence. He visited them in secret, watching his son grow from a distance, teaching him through dreams and whispers, guiding him toward the warrior he was meant to become. Lian understood; she never blamed him. But when she died—killed by assassins who had somehow discovered her location—Longwei's grief was absolute. He did not attend her funeral; he could not bear to see her body. Instead, he flew to the highest peak of the Celestial Mountains and roared for three days and three nights, until his voice gave out and the snow turned to steam. To this day, he carries a lock of her hair in a locket over his heart, and he whispers her name every night before he sleeps.
story.part4: The scholars of the Celestial Order have long debated whether Longwei is a tyrant or a savior. His rule has been absolute, his justice swift and merciless—he has executed entire noble houses for treason, burned cities that dared to defy him, and crushed rebellions with a ferocity that has become legend. But he has also protected the realm from threats that the common people will never know about: demons from Nyxara, abyssal dragons from the void, and the machinations of the Shadow Accord. He has spent his centuries on the throne building walls that cannot be breached, forging alliances that cannot be broken, and training his son in secret to be the warrior who will one day take his place. The people fear him, but they also love him, for they know that the Dragon Emperor's wrath is reserved for their enemies, not for them. And when they see the golden dragon circling above the capital, they bow their heads and whisper thanks, for they know that as long as Longwei rules, the realm is safe.
story.part5: The truth, known only to the oldest dragons and the gods themselves, is that Longwei is dying. The dragon's blood that gave him power is consuming him, turning his organs to jade, his bones to obsidian. He has perhaps another century before he transforms fully into a beast—a mindless, raging dragon that will destroy everything he has built. He does not fear death; he has lived too long for that. But he fears what will happen to Zilong, to his people, to the realm he has protected for so long. So he waits, and he watches, and he guides his son from the shadows, preparing him for the day when he must take the throne. He has left letters for Zilong, hidden in the vaults of the palace, to be opened after his death—letters that explain everything: his mother's fate, his father's love, the burden of the dragon's blood. And when Zilong finally learns the truth, Longwei hopes that his son will not hate him, that he will understand why the Dragon Emperor had to be a stranger, a legend, a myth. For Longwei is not a tyrant; he is a father, and he has sacrificed everything—his love, his family, his very humanity—for the son who will one day save the world.

weapons.name: Dragon's Wrath
weapons.fulldesc: The Dragon's Wrath is a divine mythological spear that has been passed down through Longwei's line for a thousand generations. The spear is longer than he is tall, its shaft a length of petrified dragonbone wrapped in golden wire, its surface carved with the story of the first dragon who pledged fealty to his ancestor. The blade is massive, a wicked curve of black jade edged with gold, its surface etched with runes that blaze with poison-green fire when he channels his power. At the base of the blade, a dragon's head is carved, its jaws open, its throat a conduit for the fire that flows from his soul. When he throws the Dragon's Wrath, it flies true, trailing green flame, and when it strikes, it erupts in a conflagration that can level armies and reduce fortresses to ash. When he holds it, he can breathe fire through it, unleashing torrents of draconic flame that scour the land clean. The spear hums with a deep, resonant vibration when active, and the air around it shimmers with heat haze.
weapons.look: Petrified dragonbone shaft wrapped in gold, massive curved black jade blade edged with gold, dragon's head at base with open jaws
weapons.aura: Poison-green flame, heat distortion

passive.name: Dragon's Fury
passive.description: Each successful hit with Dragon's Wrath applies a stack of Dragonfire (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target explodes, dealing 150% physical damage to all enemies within 8 meters.

passive.name: Scale of the Emperor
passive.description: Longwei's dragon scales grant him 15% damage reduction. This increases to 30% when he is below 30% health.

active.name: Flame Breath
active.description: Longwei breathes a cone of poison-green fire, dealing 200% physical damage and applying a burn that deals 50% damage per second for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Spear Throw
active.description: Longwei throws the Dragon's Wrath in a straight line, piercing all enemies and dealing 250% physical damage. The spear returns to his hand after 2 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

In dragon form, his basic attacks become area-of-effect claw swipes, his Flame Breath cooldown is reduced to 2 seconds, and he gains 50% increased health and 30% increased damage. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: While in dragon form, nearby enemies are slowed by 20% and take 50% damage per second from his aura.

special.name: Emperor's Will
special.description: When Longwei kills an enemy hero with Dragon's Wrath, all nearby allies gain 20% increased damage and 30% movement speed for 10 seconds.

ratings.notes: Overwhelming physical damage dealer with dragon transformation that makes him nearly unkillable. His passive damage reduction and area control make him a top-tier fighter.

skills.name: Dragon Scales

skills.name: Jade Strike
skills.description: Longwei lunges forward 10 meters, impaling the first enemy hit and stunning them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Emperor's Roar
skills.description: Longwei roars, fearing all enemies within 12 meters for 2 seconds and reducing their armor by 30% for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Ancestor's Blessing
skills.description: When Longwei falls below 10% health, he summons the spirits of his ancestors, becoming invulnerable for 3 seconds and healing for 50% of his max health. Triggers once every 180 seconds.

The love of his life, mother of Zilong, killed by assassins. Longwei carries a lock of her hair in a locket over his heart.

dialogue.greeting: Kneel before the Dragon Emperor, or be consumed by flame.

heroes.name: Brigeinda
titles: The Iron Maiden of the Iron Mountain
heroes.shortDescription: She emerges from the smoke of her own forge like a goddess of war forged from steel and spite, a being whose beauty is as dangerous as the weapons she builds. Brigeinda is an eighteen-year-old prodigy of Dwarven-Elf blood, a fusion of two ancient races that has produced something entirely new and utterly formidable. Her lower jaw is a gleaming metal prosthesis, her left arm entirely mechanical, and her weapon—the Siegebreaker—is a hammer that can summon localized earthquakes and call down lightning from clear skies.
heroes.fullDescription: Brigeinda was born to a dwarven father and an elven mother, a union that her clan called an abomination. She grew up in the shadows of the Iron Mountain, her parents dead, her heritage a curse, her only friends the machines she built from scrap. By the age of twelve, she had constructed her first functional war machine; by fifteen, she had bested the master engineers of three clans in a forging contest; by eighteen, she had built the Siegebreaker, a hammer of such devastating power that the Iron Mountain itself seemed to tremble when she raised it. She is the rival of Lolita, the shield-builder, and their rivalry is the stuff of legend. Where Lolita builds to defend, Brigeinda builds to destroy; where Lolita shields, Brigeinda storms. She does not hate her rival; she respects her, in the way that a wolf respects a worthy adversary. But she believes that the only true peace is the peace that comes after total victory, and she will stop at nothing to prove that her way—the way of the siege, the storm, the unstoppable march of progress—is the only way forward.

story.part1: The forge of the Iron Mountain is the oldest in Kretos, its fires burning since before the Sundering. It was there that Brigeinda was born, not of woman, but of fire and iron—or so the legends say. In truth, she was born to a dwarven father named Torvin and an elven mother named Ithiliel, a union that scandalized both clans. The dwarves called her mother a knife-ear; the elves called her father a stone-eater. But Torvin and Ithiliel loved each other, and they loved their daughter, and they taught her everything they knew: the dwarven arts of forging and smithing, the elven arts of precision and elegance. Brigeinda was a prodigy; by the age of five, she had forged her first dagger; by seven, she had repaired a broken siege engine; by ten, she had built a mechanical bird that could fly. But happiness was not meant to last. When she was eleven, a rival clan attacked the Iron Mountain, seeking to claim its forges for themselves. Torvin and Ithiliel died defending the gates, their bodies crushed by a collapsing tower. Brigeinda survived, buried in the rubble for three days, her jaw shattered, her left arm crushed beyond repair. When the rescuers pulled her from the wreckage, she was silent, her eyes empty, her hands still clutching her father's hammer.
story.part2: The recovery took years. Brigeinda rebuilt herself, piece by piece, forging a metal jaw that clicked when she spoke, constructing a mechanical arm that was stronger than flesh could ever be. She did not weep for her parents; she forged her grief into steel, hammering it into the blade of a siege engine that she built in secret. The engine was called the Grudge, and it was designed to level the walls of the clan that had killed her family. On the night of her fifteenth birthday, she rolled the Grudge to the gates of the enemy fortress and fired. The first shot took out the watchtower; the second shattered the main gate; the third collapsed the inner keep. By dawn, the fortress was rubble, and the clan that had murdered her parents was scattered, homeless, broken. Brigeinda stood in the smoke, her metal jaw gleaming, her mechanical arm still smoking, and she felt nothing. Not satisfaction, not grief, not joy—only the cold, empty certainty that she had done what needed to be done. The dwarves called her a hero; the elves called her a monster. She called herself an engineer.
story.part3: The rivalry with Lolita began at the Great Exhibition of Mekhanika, a festival of invention held every decade in the capital of the Forge Pact. Brigeinda was there to display her latest creation—a mobile siege tower that could scale any wall, bristling with cannons and flame throwers. Lolita was there to display her latest shield generator—a device that could project a barrier strong enough to withstand a direct hit from a siege cannon. They met in the exhibition hall, and the air crackled with tension. 'Your weapons are an abomination,' Lolita said, her voice sweet but her eyes cold. 'Your shields are a delusion,' Brigeinda replied, her metal jaw clicking with each word. 'You build cages. I build keys.' They did not fight that day—the Exhibition had rules against violence—but the rivalry was born. In the years since, they have clashed a dozen times, each encounter a battle of wits and will. Lolita has stopped three of Brigeinda's sieges; Brigeinda has shattered four of Lolita's shields. Neither has won, neither has lost, and neither will stop until one of them proves that their philosophy is superior.
story.part4: The Iron Mountain has become Brigeinda's fortress, her forge, her kingdom. She rules it with an iron fist, her dwarven and elven subjects grudgingly respecting the girl who rebuilt herself from scrap. Her workshops are filled with wonders and horrors: clockwork soldiers that march in perfect unison, cannons that fire shells filled with alchemical fire, flying machines that can drop payloads of destruction from the clouds. She does not build for profit; she builds for conquest, for the satisfaction of solving problems that no one else can solve, for the joy of watching her creations perform exactly as designed. The nobles of Mekhanika fear her; the common people admire her; the engineers of the Forge Pact study her designs with a mixture of awe and terror. She is eighteen years old, and she has already changed the face of warfare in Kretos. What she will do by the time she is thirty, no one dares to imagine.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Brigeinda herself, is that she does not build for conquest—she builds to forget. Every rivet she hammers, every gear she turns, every cannon she fires is a distraction from the memory of her parents' bodies being pulled from the rubble. She cannot sleep without the sound of the forge; she cannot rest without the weight of a hammer in her hand. The mechanical jaw and arm are not just replacements—they are armor, shielding her from a world that took everything from her. She does not hate Lolita; she envies her. Lolita builds to protect, to save, to heal. Brigeinda builds to destroy, because destruction is the only thing that makes sense in a world that killed her parents and left her alive. But sometimes, in the quiet hours before dawn, when the forge fires have died down and the smoke has cleared, she looks at her mechanical hand and wonders what it would feel like to hold something without crushing it. She has not found an answer yet. Perhaps she never will. But she keeps building, keeps forging, keeps fighting—because the alternative is to stop, and if she stops, the memories will consume her.

weapons.name: Siegebreaker
weapons.fulldesc: The Siegebreaker is a hammer of such devastating power that it is said to have been forged in the heart of a dying storm. The hammer is massive, its head a block of rust-green steel the size of an anvil, its surface etched with runes of thunder, fire, and earth. The haft is a length of petrified oak wrapped in copper wire, its length adjusted to Brigeinda's grip, its pommel a spike of black iron. When she raises the Siegebreaker, the air around her crackles with static; when she brings it down, the earth shudders, and localized earthquakes ripple outward, shattering walls and toppling towers. With a thought, she can summon storms, the hammer calling down lightning that strikes with the precision of a siege engine; with a swing, she can unleash torrents of fire that consume armies and reduce fortresses to slag. The hammer is warm to the touch, humming with stored energy, and its head glows with a faint, orange light when she channels her power.
weapons.look: Massive rust-green steel head, petrified oak haft wrapped in copper wire, black iron pommel spike, etched with runes of destruction
weapons.aura: Orange lightning, static crackle, heat haze

passive.name: Earthshaker
passive.description: Each time Brigeinda uses a skill, she causes a small tremor that deals 50% physical damage to enemies within 5 meters and slows them by 20% for 2 seconds.

passive.name: Siege Mentality
passive.description: Brigeinda deals 25% increased damage to structures and shields. Additionally, she takes 15% reduced damage from structures and turrets.

active.name: Ground Slam
active.description: Brigeinda slams the Siegebreaker into the ground, creating a shockwave in a 10-meter cone that deals 200% physical damage and knocks enemies up for 1 second. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Lightning Call
active.description: Brigeinda raises the Siegebreaker, calling down a lightning strike at target location. Deals 250% physical damage to enemies within a 5-meter radius and stuns them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Cataclysm
ultimate.description: Brigeinda channels for 1.5 seconds, then unleashes the full power of the Siegebreaker, causing a massive earthquake in a 20-meter radius. Enemies within the radius take 400% physical damage, are stunned for 2 seconds, and have their armor reduced by 50% for 8 seconds. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: After using Cataclysm, Brigeinda's next three basic attacks deal 50% increased damage and chain to nearby enemies.

special.name: Demolition Expert
special.description: When Brigeinda deals damage to a structure, she applies a stack of Demolish (max 10 stacks). At 10 stacks, the structure takes an additional 500% physical damage and is stunned for 3 seconds.

ratings.notes: Exceptional siege damage with powerful area control. Excels at destroying structures and shields, but vulnerable to magic.

skills.name: Overclock
skills.description: Brigeinda's mechanical arm and jaw grant her 15% increased attack speed and 10% increased cooldown reduction. Each stack of Demolish also increases her attack speed by 2%.

skills.name: Siege Mode
skills.description: Brigeinda enters Siege Mode, rooting herself in place but increasing her attack range by 200% and her damage by 30%. Lasts 8 seconds or until she moves. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Napalm Strike
skills.description: Brigeinda fires a canister of alchemical fire at target area, creating a burning zone for 6 seconds that deals 80% physical damage per second to enemies inside. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Emergency Repairs
skills.description: When Brigeinda falls below 30% health, her mechanical arm performs emergency repairs, healing her for 30% of her max health over 5 seconds. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Brigeinda was born to a dwarven father and an elven mother, a union that her clan called an abomination. She grew up in the shadows of the Iron Mountain, her parents dead, her heritage a curse...

relationships.description: They have clashed a dozen times, each encounter a battle of wits and will. Brigeinda respects Lolita but believes that destruction is superior to protection.
A small black tortoise construct that she built in her first year as a master engineer. Its shell is etched with runes, and it breathes cyan poison flame.

pet.name: Mortifer
pet.description: A small black tortoise with a shell of black iron, its legs a scuttle of steel claws. Its shell is etched with the same runes that adorn the Siegebreaker, and when it opens its mouth, a torrent of cyan poison flame erupts—a stream of alchemical fire that can melt through the heaviest armor and leave only ash in its wake.
pet.fulldesc: Mortifer is no ordinary creature; it is a construct, a living machine that Brigeinda built in her first year as a master engineer, and it is as deadly as any of her siege weapons. Its shell is a dome of black iron, etched with runes of destruction that glow with a faint, orange light when it is active. Its eyes are two chips of glowing cyan crystal, and it moves with a deceptive slowness, but it is always at her side, its head swiveling, watching for threats. It can fire jets of cyan poison flame from its mouth, can retract into its shell for protection, and can emit a low, humming vibration that disrupts enemy machinery. It is her only companion, her confidant, the one being in the world that she trusts not to betray her. When she works at the forge, it sits at her feet, its shell warm from the fire; when she fights, it crawls beside her, its flame ready, its loyalty absolute.
abilities: Cyan Poison Flame – Breathes a cone of alchemical fire that deals 150% physical damage and applies a poison that deals 20% damage per second for 5 seconds.
Shell Defense – Retracts into shell, becoming invulnerable and regenerating 10% health per second for 4 seconds (cooldown: 60 seconds).
Machine Disruption – Emits a low-frequency hum that reduces enemy attack speed by 30% within 10 meters.

heroes.specialty: Siege Specialist / Fighter with exceptional structure damage, area control, and demolition capabilities.

dialogue.greeting: Another wall to break? Good. I was getting bored.
dialogue.respawn: The forge calls. Time to rebuild.

heroes.name: Hattori Hanjou
heroes.shortDescription: He moves through the world like a petal caught on a spring breeze—beautiful, fleeting, and capable of cutting deeper than any blade. Hattori Hanjou is a young man of twenty-one years, his features a masterful blend of Japanese refinement and ethereal beauty. He wields Yami, the sword of light, and Kuro, the blade of cherry blossoms, and he moves with the fluid grace of a dancer, each step silent, each gesture precise.
heroes.fullDescription: Hattori Hanjou was born into the shadow courts of the Shogunate, trained from birth to be a weapon for his clan. But where other ninjas learned to kill without remorse, Hanjou learned to kill with beauty. His blades are extensions of his soul, his movements a meditation, his purpose a prayer. He is the distant friend of Hayabusa, the cousin of Kagura, a man who walks the line between shadow and light, between the brutal pragmatism of the ninja and the transcendent beauty of the cherry blossom. He does not seek conflict, but he does not shy from it; he has been trained since birth to protect his clan, his family, his friends, and he will do so with every tool at his disposal. He knows that Hayabusa walks a similar path, and he watches his friend's progress with a mixture of pride and concern, ready to step in when the darkness threatens to overwhelm him.

story.part1: The cherry blossoms of the Shogun's palace are the most beautiful in all of Kretos, their petals a deep, vibrant pink that carpets the ground in spring. It was beneath those trees that Hattori Hanjou took his first breath, born to the head of the shadow clan that served the Shogun for a thousand years. His father was a cold man, a killer who saw his son as a tool to be sharpened; his mother was a poet, a woman who saw beauty in the spaces between violence. Hanjou inherited both: his father's precision and his mother's grace. He could slip a blade between a man's ribs before the man knew he was dead, and he could compose a haiku about the experience that would bring tears to the eyes of the court. The other children of the shadow clan feared him; the elders respected him; his father was proud of him. But Hanjou was lonely, for he had no one who understood that his blades were not weapons—they were brushes, and the blood they spilled was ink, and every kill was a painting.
story.part2: The first time Hanjou met Hayabusa, they were both children, training in the same dojo under the watchful eye of Master Chen. Hayabusa was brash, loud, his movements powerful but imprecise; Hanjou was quiet, controlled, his movements a whisper. They fought a hundred sparring matches, and Hayabusa won exactly none of them. But Hanjou did not gloat; he did not mock. Instead, he offered Hayabusa tea, and he said, 'You are faster than me, and stronger. But you do not listen to your blade. It sings to you, Hayabusa. Can you not hear it?' Hayabusa looked at him like he was insane. But that night, Hayabusa sat alone in the dojo, his blade across his knees, and he listened. And in the silence, he heard it—a low, resonant hum, the voice of the steel. From that day forward, they were friends, bound by a respect that transcended rivalry. They trained together, bled together, and when Hayabusa left the dojo to pursue his own path, Hanjou bowed and said, 'I will be watching, my friend. If you ever need me, I will come.' He meant it.
story.part3: The tragedy that changed Hanjou's life came without warning. The Shogun was assassinated, his body found in the cherry blossom garden, a single blade through his heart. The shadow clan was blamed, and in a single night, the Shogun's loyalists slaughtered Hanjou's family—his father, his mother, his uncles, his cousins, everyone. Hanjou survived only because he was away on a mission, and when he returned to find the palace in flames, he fell to his knees and screamed until his voice gave out. He did not seek revenge; revenge was too simple, too clean. Instead, he disappeared into the shadows, becoming a ghost, a rumor, a figure that parents whispered about to frighten their children. He hunted the men who had killed his family, not quickly, but slowly—one by one, over years, making each death a lesson. Some died in their sleep; some died in the middle of a feast, choking on wine that had been poisoned with cherry blossom extract; some simply vanished, never to be seen again. When the last of them fell, Hanjou did not feel satisfaction. He felt only the cold, empty certainty that he was now truly alone.
story.part4: The cherry blossoms still bloom in the Shogun's garden, though the palace is a ruin and the Shogun's line is no more. Hanjou visits every spring, sitting beneath the oldest tree, his blades across his knees, his white crow Sakura perched on his shoulder. He does not speak; he simply sits, and watches the petals fall, and remembers. The other ninjas of the shadow courts avoid him, for they sense that he is touched by something beyond their understanding—a grief so deep that it has become beautiful, a sorrow so pure that it has become a kind of peace. He has not spoken to Hayabusa in years, but he watches his friend from the shadows, and he knows that Hayabusa watches him in return. They are two blades forged in the same fire, and though they walk different paths, they are bound by a bond that no distance can break.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Hanjou himself, is that he has already forgiven the men who killed his family. He forgave them the moment he drove his blade into the heart of the last one, not because they deserved forgiveness, but because he could not carry the weight of his hatred any longer. The cherry blossoms taught him that: they bloom for a moment, then fall, and their beauty is not diminished by their brevity. So, too, with life. He does not seek to forget his family; he honors them by living, by protecting the innocent, by ensuring that no one else suffers as he suffered. He is the Cherry Blossom Shadow, and his blades are not weapons—they are prayers, written in steel, offered to the gods who took everything from him and left him with the strength to go on.

appearance.hairStyle: Long, past shoulders, thick, silky, loose, often pulled back

White leather bands on wrists embroidered with ancestor names
