upper.chest: Lean, defined

weapons.name: Yami & Kuro
weapons.fulldesc: Yami and Kuro are twin blades that have become legendary in the shadow courts. Yami is the sword of light, its blade a shimmering, ghostly white that seems to glow with an inner radiance. It is a katana of exquisite craftsmanship, its edge so sharp it can cut through shadow itself, its power the power to banish darkness. When Hanjou draws Yami, the shadows around him recoil, and the blade leaves trails of light that linger in the air like afterimages. Kuro is the blade of cherry blossoms, its blade a deep, vibrant pink, its surface etched with the patterns of falling petals. When Hanjou channels his power through Kuro, the blade blossoms, releasing waves of pink energy that explode into clouds of ethereal petals, each one sharp enough to slice through steel, each one carrying the magic of his bloodline. With concentration, he can focus this power into a single, devastating blast—a cherry blossom storm that can level buildings and cleanse the land of corruption. The blades are warm to the touch, humming with a low, harmonic note when active, and they are bound to him by a bond that transcends the physical.
weapons.look: Yami: ghostly white blade, glowing; Kuro: vibrant pink blade with petal patterns; hilts wrapped in dark textured material, guards etched with circuitry
weapons.aura: White light (Yami) and pink cherry blossom energy (Kuro)

passive.name: Blossom Dance
passive.description: Each time Hanjou switches between Yami and Kuro, he gains 20% increased attack speed and 10% increased movement speed for 3 seconds. Stacks up to 3 times.

passive.name: Shadow Blend
passive.description: When standing still for 2 seconds, Hanjou becomes invisible. Attacking or using a skill breaks invisibility. While invisible, his next attack deals 50% increased damage.

active.name: Light Slash (Yami)
active.description: Hanjou slashes with Yami, releasing a wave of light in a 10-meter cone that deals 180% physical damage and blinds enemies for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Petal Storm (Kuro)
active.description: Hanjou spins with Kuro, releasing a storm of cherry blossom petals that deal 150% physical damage to enemies within an 8-meter radius and reduce their healing by 50% for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Sakura Finale
ultimate.description: Hanjou channels for 1 second, then unleashes the combined power of both blades, creating a massive cherry blossom storm in a 20-meter radius. Enemies within the radius take 350% physical damage, are slowed by 60% for 4 seconds, and take 100% damage per second from swirling petals. Cooldown: 85 seconds. Passive: After using Sakura Finale, Hanjou becomes invisible for 3 seconds and his next basic attack crits.

special.name: Shadow Step
special.description: When Hanjou uses a skill, he can immediately teleport to a nearby shadow within 8 meters (once per skill). Cooldown: 5 seconds.

ratings.notes: Exceptional burst damage and mobility. Invisibility and shadow step make him difficult to catch. Vulnerable to crowd control.

skills.name: Way of the Shadow
skills.description: Hanjou's basic attacks apply a stack of Shadow Mark (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target takes 150% physical damage and is slowed by 30% for 2 seconds.

skills.name: Blade Switch
skills.description: Hanjou switches between Yami (light mode) and Kuro (cherry blossom mode). Each mode changes the effect of his basic attacks and skills. Cooldown: 2 seconds.

skills.name: Shadow Rush
skills.description: Hanjou dashes forward 12 meters, becoming untargetable during the dash. The first enemy hit takes 120% physical damage. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Cherry Blossom Ward
skills.description: When Hanjou falls below 30% health, he releases a cloud of cherry blossom petals, becoming invisible for 2 seconds and healing for 20% of his max health. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Hattori Hanjou was born into the shadow courts of the Shogunate, trained from birth to be a weapon for his clan. But where other ninjas learned to kill without remorse, Hanjou learned to kill with beauty...

relationships.description: They trained together as children and share a bond of mutual respect. Hanjou watches Hayabusa from the shadows, ready to help if needed.
They are cousins, though they rarely interact due to their different paths. Hanjou respects her connection to the spirit realm.

pet.name: Sakura
pet.description: A white crow with feathers the color of fresh snow and eyes of deep sapphire blue that match Hanjou's own. Sakura is no ordinary bird; it is a spirit companion, a fragment of the same magic that flows through his blood, and it serves as his eyes and ears in the world.
pet.fulldesc: Sakura can speak, in a voice like the rustle of leaves, and it offers Hanjou counsel when the path is unclear. It can fly faster than the eye can see, delivering messages and scouting ahead, and when danger threatens, it can unleash a screech that shatters the concentration of his enemies, giving him the opening he needs to strike. Its feathers are tipped with a faint, pink glow that mirrors the cherry blossoms he commands, and it perches on his shoulder when he rests, its head tucked under its wing, its breathing slow and steady. It is the only being that has been with him since before the massacre, and it carries the memory of his family in its songs. When he is sad, it nuzzles his cheek; when he is angry, it circles his head, its screech a warning. It is his partner, his confidant, and the only soul that truly understands him.
abilities: Screech of Shadows – Emits a piercing screech that stuns all enemies within 8 meters for 1 second (cooldown: 60 seconds).
Messenger of the Blossom – Can deliver a message to any ally on the map, appearing at their location after 5 seconds (cooldown: 120 seconds).
Eyes of the Crow – Sakura can scout a 50-meter radius, revealing all enemies and hidden traps (cooldown: 30 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Assassin / Burst damage dealer with exceptional mobility, invisibility, and shadow-based abilities.

dialogue.greeting: The cherry blossoms fall. So, too, will our enemies.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Sakura Finale! Let the petals rain!
dialogue.respawn: The shadow does not die. It only waits.

heroes.name: Frejyah
titles: The Last Valkyrie of the Damned
nicknames: She Who Wept for the Fallen
heroes.shortDescription: She stands alone at the edge of the world, where the frozen mists of Helheim meet the blood-stained fields of what was once Valhalla. Frejyah is a Valkyrie who has fallen from grace not through sin, but through betrayal, and who has risen again as something far more dangerous than any goddess. She wields Hundala, a massive shield forged from a dying star, and Gungnir's Shard, a spear stolen from Odin's own vault, and she hunts the gods who sacrificed her sisters.
heroes.fullDescription: Frejyah was once the most beautiful of the Valkyries, the chosen daughter of Odin, the one who carried the bravest warriors from the battlefields of Kretos to the golden halls of Valhalla. But the gods betrayed her. In a war engineered to cull the weak from the strong, her sisters were slaughtered, and when Frejyah cried out for help, the gods turned away. She learned the truth: that the war between gods and demons was a game, a manipulation designed to reward the powerful and sacrifice the rest. She turned her back on Valhalla that day, and she named herself Frejyah—the last Valkyrie, the one who will not fall. She has spent a hundred years hunting the demons that killed her sisters, and she has spent a hundred years planning her revenge against the gods who sent them. She does not seek justice; justice is a lie that the powerful tell the weak. She seeks vengeance, cold and absolute, and she will not rest until the halls of Valhalla are empty, until Odin kneels before her, until the world knows that the Valkyries were not pawns to be sacrificed, but warriors to be feared.

story.part1: In the golden age of Valhalla, before the Sundering tore the realms apart, the Valkyries were the most honored of Odin's servants. They rode across the battlefields of Kretos on winged steeds, their armor gleaming, their spears singing, and they chose the bravest of the fallen to join the eternal feast in the halls of the gods. Frejyah was the youngest of them, the newest to take the oath, but she was also the most beautiful and the most skilled. Her wings were the brightest, her spear the sharpest, her heart the purest. Odin himself smiled upon her, and the other Valkyries loved her as a sister. She thought she was blessed; she thought the gods were just; she thought her purpose was sacred. She was wrong.
story.part2: The war that broke the Valkyries began without warning. A rift opened in the frozen wastes of Niflheim, and from it poured demons of a kind that even Odin had never seen—creatures of shadow and hunger, their bodies made of teeth and darkness. The Valkyries were sent to seal the rift, a hundred of them, the best of the best. Frejyah led the charge, her spear blazing, her sisters at her back. They fought for three days and three nights, and they were winning—until the gods closed the gates. Odin had decided that the Valkyries were expendable, that their sacrifice would buy the time needed to build a new barrier, that a hundred lives were a small price to pay for the safety of Asgard. Frejyah watched her sisters fall, one by one, their screams echoing in the frozen air. She cried out for help, but the gods did not answer. She cried out for mercy, but the demons did not listen. In the end, only she survived, buried beneath the bodies of her sisters, her wings broken, her spear shattered, her heart turned to ice.
story.part3: The Frejyah who crawled out of that frozen pit was not the same woman who had entered it. Her wings had healed, but they were no longer white; they were the grey of ash and the black of mourning. Her spear was reforged from a shard of Gungnir that she had stolen from Odin's vault, and its blade crackled with the power of the oath that the gods had broken. She did not return to Valhalla; she went to Helheim instead, to the frozen realm of the dishonored dead, and she made a pact with the spirits of the fallen. They would give her power, and she would give them vengeance. She became the Last Valkyrie of the Damned, a creature of ice and fury, and she swore that she would not rest until every god who had abandoned her sisters was dead.
story.part4: The centuries that followed were a blur of blood and ice. Frejyah hunted the demons that had killed her sisters, and she killed them all—every last one, from the lowest imp to the generals who had led the charge. She carved their names into the shaft of her spear, and when she was done, the shaft was covered in runes of death. Then she turned her gaze to Asgard. She could not breach the golden walls—not yet—but she could make the gods pay in other ways. She slaughtered their messengers, burned their temples, corrupted their shrines. She became a legend, a curse, a name that the gods whispered in fear. And all the while, she carried the bones of her sisters in a pouch at her belt, their skulls polished and warm against her hip, a reminder of why she fought.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Frejyah and the spirits of Helheim, is that she does not want to kill Odin. She wants him to kneel. She wants him to look into her eyes and see the faces of the sisters he sacrificed, and she wants him to beg for forgiveness. She will not grant it. She will watch him beg, and she will feel nothing—no satisfaction, no pity, no joy—only the cold, empty certainty that justice has been done. And then she will walk away, and she will find a quiet place, and she will lay her sisters' bones to rest, and she will finally allow herself to weep. But that day is not today. Today, there are still gods to punish, still oaths to avenge, still blood to spill. And Frejyah, the Last Valkyrie of the Damned, will not rest until the last drop has fallen.

appearance.faceShape: Angelic perfection, sharp aristocratic features
appearance.hairStyle: Long, braided in traditional Valkyrie style, interwoven with silver threads and bone beads
appearance.eyeColor: Deep frozen green, shifting like aurora borealis
appearance.facialStructure: High cheekbones, hollowed cheeks, eternal grief etched into features
appearance.skinTone: Alabaster, cold to touch, luminous
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Massive grey-black wings (primary feathers mahogany, secondary ash and charcoal); cracked and scarred Valhalla helm on head; frozen breath

upper.chest: Torn remnants of silver Valkyrie armor, cracked and blood-stained, barely held together by red silk cloth wrapped around torso
upper.arms: Bare, lean and corded, left arm wears single silver vambrace etched with sisters' names
upper.neck: Silver chain with broken hilts of sisters' swords

attire.headwear: Cracked and scarred Valhalla helm with broken wings, visor shattered, still humming with thunder
accessories: Wolf-fur cloak (grey, thick, warm, head still attached)
attire.distinctFeatures: Massive grey-black wings; frozen mist rises from skin; helm crackles with residual divine energy

upper.chest: Voluptuous, full G-cup, taut muscles

weapons.name: Hundala & Gungnir's Shard
weapons.fulldesc: Hundala is a massive shield, forged from the heart of a dying star and blessed by the first Valkyrie herself. It is taller than Frejyah is, its surface a polished silver that seems to absorb the light, its edges sharp enough to cleave through stone. The shield is inscribed with runes of protection, and it is said that it can block any attack, even the blow of a god, even the fury of a demon king. She carries it on her back, its weight a constant comfort, and when she raises it, the world holds its breath. Gungnir's Shard is her spear, a fragment of Odin's own weapon, stolen from the vaults of Valhalla when she turned her back on the gods. The spear is longer than she is tall, its shaft a length of petrified Yggdrasil wood, its head a shard of the original Gungnir, still crackling with the power of the oath that Odin broke. When she throws it, it never misses its mark, and when it strikes, it does not merely wound; it seals the soul of its victim, trapping them in the frozen wastes of Helheim for eternity.
weapons.look: Hundala: massive silver shield, star-forged, rune-inscribed; Gungnir's Shard: petrified wood shaft, crackling spearhead, runes of the fallen
weapons.aura: Frozen silver light (Hundala), crackling golden electricity (Gungnir's Shard)

passive.name: Valkyrie's Ward
passive.description: While holding Hundala, Frejyah gains 30% damage reduction from the front and 15% damage reduction from all other directions. Additionally, she reflects 10% of incoming damage back to the attacker.

passive.name: Oathbreaker
passive.description: Gungnir's Shard deals 25% increased damage to divine or celestial enemies. Each hit applies a stack of Oathbreaker (max 3 stacks), reducing the target's healing received by 15% per stack.

active.name: Shield Charge
active.description: Frejyah charges forward with Hundala raised, knocking back all enemies in her path and dealing 150% physical damage. Enemies knocked into walls are stunned for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Spear Throw
active.description: Frejyah throws Gungnir's Shard in a straight line, piercing all enemies and dealing 200% physical damage. The spear returns to her hand after 2 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Helheim's Judgment
ultimate.description: Frejyah raises both shield and spear, channeling the power of Helheim for 1.5 seconds. She then unleashes a wave of frozen energy in a 25-meter radius, dealing 400% physical damage and freezing all enemies for 3 seconds. Frozen enemies cannot act and take 50% damage per second from the ice. Cooldown: 100 seconds. Passive: Enemies killed during Helheim's Judgment have their souls trapped in Gungnir's Shard, permanently increasing Frejyah's physical attack by 5 per kill (max 50).

special.name: Sister's Vengeance
special.description: When an ally dies within 15 meters of Frejyah, she gains 50% increased damage and 30% increased movement speed for 10 seconds. Additionally, her next basic attack or skill crits.

ratings.notes: Extremely durable with high damage output. Exceptional against divine enemies. Shield makes her difficult to kill from the front.

skills.name: Frozen Heart
skills.description: Frejyah's aura of cold slows nearby enemies by 15% and reduces their attack speed by 10%. Enemies within 5 meters also take 20% damage per second.

skills.name: Valkyrie's Flight
skills.description: Frejyah takes flight with her wings, becoming untargetable and moving at 200% speed for 3 seconds. When she lands, she deals 100% physical damage in a 5-meter radius. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Shield Bash
skills.description: Frejyah bashes with Hundala, stunning the target for 1 second and dealing 120% physical damage. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

skills.name: Frozen Resilience
skills.description: When Frejyah falls below 30% health, she encases herself in ice, becoming invulnerable for 3 seconds and healing for 40% of her max health over the duration. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

heroes.lore: Frejyah was once the most beautiful of the Valkyries, the chosen daughter of Odin, the one who carried the bravest warriors from the battlefields of Kretos to the golden halls of Valhalla...

relationships.description: Frejyah seeks to make Odin kneel and beg for forgiveness for sacrificing her sisters. She will not rest until the All-Father pays for his betrayal.
She made a pact with the spirits of the dishonored dead. They give her power; she gives them vengeance against the gods.

heroes.specialty: Tank / Fighter with exceptional durability, crowd control, and anti-divine damage. Her shield makes her a formidable frontliner.

dialogue.greeting: The Valkyries fell. I did not. What does that make me?
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Helheim's Judgment! Feel the cold of the dishonored!
dialogue.death: Sisters... I come... to join you...
dialogue.respawn: The damned do not die. They only wait.

heroes.name: Mara
heroes.shortDescription: She descends from the canopy of the eternal forest like a shard of moonlight given form, a being of such radiant purity that the shadows recoil from her presence. Mara is an elf of the highest order, the sister of Karina, and her eternal adversary. Where Karina embraced the darkness, Mara chose the light, and they have crossed blades a hundred times across the ages. She wields the Behemothsting, twin blades that inject the power of purity into the very essence of their targets.
heroes.fullDescription: Mara was born under the light of the World Tree, her soul blessed by the goddess of dawn, her purpose clear from her first breath: to banish the shadows that threaten the innocent. She is the sister that Karina could have been, the shadow banisher who chose the light while her twin chose the darkness. She does not hate Karina; she grieves for her, for she knows that the shadow that consumes her sister was not born of malice, but of loss. They were once inseparable, two halves of a whole, trained together, hunted together, loved together. But the war took their family, their home, their future, and where Mara found solace in the light, Karina found power in the darkness. Now, they are eternal adversaries, light and shadow, bound by blood and divided by purpose. Mara hunts the darkness that Karina serves, and Karina serves the darkness that Mara hunts. They have crossed blades a hundred times, and each time, Mara has offered her sister redemption, and each time, Karina has refused. But Mara does not give up; she is patient, eternal, and she knows that the light always finds its way, even into the deepest shadow.

story.part1: In the heart of the Eternal Forest, where the World Tree's roots drink from the waters of creation, twin sisters were born on the night of a lunar eclipse. The elder, Mara, came first, her cry a beam of light that pierced the darkness of the delivery chamber. The younger, Karina, followed moments later, her first breath drawing the shadows around her like a shroud. The elves of the forest rejoiced, for the prophecy had spoken of twins—one of light, one of shadow—who would together maintain the balance of the world. Mara was trained by the priests of the sun, learning to channel holy energy, to heal the sick, to banish the demons that lurked at the edges of the forest. Karina was trained by the shadow dancers, learning to move unseen, to strike from darkness, to embrace the power that the light rejected. They were inseparable as children, playing in the branches of the World Tree, sharing secrets, dreaming of the future. Mara would become the guardian of the forest; Karina would become her shadow, protecting her from threats that she could not see. That was the plan. That was the dream. That was the lie.
story.part2: The Sundering tore the Eternal Forest apart. Rifts opened in the sky, and from them poured demons of shadow and flame, their hunger endless, their mercy absent. The elves fought, and they died, and the World Tree itself wept sap like blood. Mara fought at the front lines, her blades blazing with holy light, her voice calling out for her sister. Karina fought in the shadows, her daggers finding the throats of demons, her eyes searching for her sister. They found each other in the chaos, back to back, and for a moment, they thought they would survive. Then the rift exploded, and a wave of pure darkness washed over the battlefield. Mara raised a shield of light, protecting herself and a handful of survivors. Karina was not fast enough. The darkness consumed her, and when it passed, she was changed—her skin pale, her eyes black, her heart cold. She looked at Mara without recognition, and then she fled into the shadows, and Mara did not see her again for a decade.
story.part3: The next time they met, Karina was no longer an elf—she was a creature of shadow, a servant of the darkness, a weapon pointed at the heart of the Eternal Forest. Mara had spent the intervening years hunting rumors of her sister, following trails that led to dead ends and empty graves. When she finally found her, standing in the ruins of their childhood home, she wept. 'Come back to me,' she begged. 'Please, Karina. The light can heal you.' Karina laughed—a cold, hollow sound that echoed in the darkness. 'The light abandoned me, sister. It left me to die in that rift. The darkness saved me. The darkness gave me power. The darkness is my family now.' They fought, and Mara won—but she could not bring herself to land the killing blow. She watched her sister slip back into the shadows, and she swore that the next time, she would not hesitate. She has broken that oath a hundred times.
story.part4: The scholars of the Natural Balance have studied the bond between Mara and Karina, and they have concluded that the twins are not merely siblings—they are two halves of a single soul, split by the trauma of the Sundering. The light in Mara is the light that was once in Karina; the shadow in Karina is the shadow that Mara rejected. They cannot be separated; they cannot be reconciled; they can only dance, light and shadow, forever. Mara does not believe this. She believes that Karina can be saved, that the shadow can be purged, that her sister can come home. She has spent centuries searching for a cure, traveling to the farthest corners of Kretos, consulting with priests and mages and demons, all in the hope of finding a way to bring Karina back to the light. She has not found it yet. But she will not stop looking.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Mara and the spirits of the Eternal Forest, is that she has already forgiven Karina for everything she has done. She forgave her the moment she saw her standing in the ruins of their childhood home, her eyes black, her heart cold. She forgives her every time they fight, every time Karina tries to kill her, every time her sister's shadow blade comes within an inch of her heart. She forgives her because she knows that the darkness is not Karina—it is a disease, a curse, a wound that needs healing. And she will keep forgiving her, and keep fighting her, and keep offering her redemption, until the day that Karina finally accepts. Or until the day that Mara dies—but even then, she will not give up. For she is Mara, the Shadow Banisher Elf, and her love for her sister is stronger than any darkness.

weapons.name: Behemothsting
weapons.fulldesc: The Behemothsting are twin blades that have become legendary in the eternal war between light and shadow. The blades are curved, elegant, their hilts wrapped in white leather, their guards shaped like unfurling lotus petals. The blades themselves are forged from a metal that seems to glow from within, a pale, jade-green light that pulses with the rhythm of her heartbeat. When she draws them, they sing, a high, clear note that resonates with the power of holiness, and when she strikes, they do not merely cut flesh; they inject the power of purity into the very essence of their target. Shadows dissolve, darkness recoils, and the unholy are purified or destroyed, depending on the depth of their corruption. The Behemothsting are not weapons of death; they are instruments of redemption, tools that Mara wields with the precision of a surgeon and the mercy of a saint.
weapons.look: Curved, elegant, jade-green glow, lotus petal guards, white leather wraps
weapons.aura: Jade-green light, holy resonance

passive.name: Purifying Strike
passive.description: Each hit from Behemothsting applies a stack of Purification (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target is cleansed of all buffs and takes 150% holy damage.

passive.name: Light's Blessing
passive.description: Mara's attacks heal nearby allies for 5% of the damage dealt. Against shadow or demon enemies, the healing is increased to 10%.

active.name: Blade of Dawn
active.description: Mara dashes forward, striking all enemies in her path for 180% physical damage and applying 2 stacks of Purification. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Radiant Spin
active.description: Mara spins, releasing a wave of holy energy in a 10-meter radius, dealing 150% physical damage and knocking back enemies. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Final Purification
ultimate.description: Mara channels for 1.5 seconds, then unleashes a massive blast of holy light in a 20-meter radius. Enemies within the radius take 350% holy damage and are stunned for 2 seconds. Allies within the radius are healed for 300% of Mara's magic power. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: Enemies killed during Final Purification cannot be resurrected for 30 seconds.

special.name: Shadow Hunter
special.description: Mara deals 30% increased damage to shadow-aligned enemies. Additionally, she can see invisible enemies within 15 meters.

ratings.notes: Balanced fighter with strong anti-shadow capabilities. Her healing and purification make her valuable in team fights.

skills.name: Holy Aura
skills.description: Mara's presence radiates holy light, healing nearby allies for 2% of their max health per second and dealing 2% of enemy max health as holy damage per second to shadow-aligned enemies.

skills.name: Wings of Light
skills.description: Mara takes flight, becoming untargetable and moving at 150% speed for 4 seconds. While flying, she leaves a trail of light that heals allies who pass through it. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Purifying Touch
skills.description: Mara cleanses a target ally of all debuffs and heals them for 150% of her magic power. Can also be used on enemies to remove their buffs and deal 100% holy damage. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Light's Embrace
skills.description: When Mara falls below 20% health, she becomes invulnerable for 2 seconds and heals for 50% of her max health. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

heroes.lore: Mara was born under the light of the World Tree, her soul blessed by the goddess of dawn, her purpose clear from her first breath: to banish the shadows that threaten the innocent...

relationships.description: They are twins, light and shadow, bound by blood and divided by purpose. Mara seeks to redeem her sister; Karina refuses.
Mara is blessed by the spirits of the Eternal Forest, who grant her the power to banish shadows.

heroes.specialty: Support / Fighter with healing, purification, and anti-shadow capabilities. Excellent against dark-aligned enemies.

dialogue.greeting: The light welcomes you, hunter. Stay in its embrace.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Final Purification! Let the shadows be cleansed!
dialogue.respawn: The light endures. So do I.

heroes.name: Belleri Fumas
heroes.shortDescription: She burns at the heart of the world, a flame that has never been extinguished, a warmth that has cradled civilizations and reduced empires to ash. Belleri Fumas is a divine spirit of fire, her form a breathtaking fusion of ethereal beauty and elemental fury. She is the sister of Aurora, the Ice Queen, and their eternal clash of fire and ice is the balance that keeps the world from tipping into chaos.
heroes.fullDescription: Belleri Fumas is fire incarnate, a primordial spirit who has existed since the first spark struck the first kindling. She is warmth, life, destruction, and renewal—all the contradictions of flame wrapped in a form of breathtaking beauty. Her sister is Aurora, the Ice Queen, and their relationship is the stuff of legend. They have clashed for millennia, fire against ice, summer against winter, and each clash is a symphony of creation and destruction. They fight, they rage, they hurl mountains and freeze oceans, but at the end of each battle, they find each other, exhausted and laughing, and they remember that they are sisters, that they love each other, that the world needs both fire and ice to survive. Belleri does not seek to destroy her sister; she seeks to balance her, to remind her that even the coldest winter must eventually yield to spring.

story.part1: In the time before time, when the realms of Kretos were still being shaped from the primordial chaos, two sisters were born from the first breath of the cosmos. Aurora came first, born from the frozen void between stars, her skin the color of glacial ice, her breath a winter storm. Belleri followed moments later, born from the heat of the first star's ignition, her skin the color of molten gold, her breath a river of flame. They were opposites in every way, yet they were inseparable, two halves of a whole, fire and ice dancing together in the empty spaces of creation. They shaped the worlds together: Aurora froze the oceans so that Belleri could melt them in the spring; Belleri burned the forests so that Aurora could blanket the ashes in snow. They were balance, they were harmony, they were the dance of the elements made manifest. And they loved each other with a love that transcended the ages.
story.part2: The Sundering changed everything. The primordial chaos that had given birth to the sisters began to twist, to corrupt, to turn the harmony of the elements into a discord of war. Aurora felt the cold growing in her heart, a darkness that whispered that she did not need her sister, that she could freeze the worlds alone. Belleri felt the heat rising in her blood, a fury that whispered that she could burn the realms to ash and rule over the embers. They fought for the first time on the fields of the Frozen Sea, their clash so violent that it cracked the crust of the world and sent shards of ice and flame into the sky. The battle lasted a hundred years, and when it ended, both sisters lay exhausted, their bodies broken, their hearts aching. They did not speak for a century after that. They did not need to. They both knew that something had broken between them, something that might never be repaired.
story.part3: The centuries that followed were a cycle of conflict and reconciliation. Aurora would freeze a river that Belleri needed to melt; Belleri would burn a forest that Aurora needed to preserve. They would meet on the fields of the world, and they would fight, and the mortals who witnessed their battles would write songs about the fury of the elements. But at the end of each battle, when they were too exhausted to continue, they would sit together in the silence, and they would remember. They would remember the time before the Sundering, when they had danced together in the empty spaces of creation, when they had shaped the worlds together, when they had loved each other without reservation. And they would weep, and they would hold each other, and they would promise to try harder, to be better, to find their way back to the harmony they had lost. And then they would go their separate ways, and the cycle would begin again.
story.part4: The scholars of the Natural Balance have studied the relationship between Belleri and Aurora for millennia, and they have concluded that the sisters are not enemies—they are teachers. Their eternal conflict is a lesson to the mortals of Kretos about the nature of balance, about the necessity of opposition, about the beauty that can only exist when fire and ice dance together. Belleri's flames are not meant to destroy; they are meant to clear away the old so that the new can grow. Aurora's ice is not meant to kill; it is meant to preserve, to hold the world in stasis until the time is right for change. They are the heartbeat of the world, the systole and diastole of creation, and without them, the realms would fall into chaos. The mortals do not understand this; they see only the destruction, the wildfires and the blizzards, the clash of the elements. But the sisters know the truth, and they carry the weight of that knowledge with every battle, every reconciliation, every tear.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Belleri and Aurora themselves, is that they are not two separate beings—they are one being, split in two by the Sundering. They are the primordial element of temperature, the dance of hot and cold that gives life its shape. They were never meant to be separate; they were meant to be balanced, to exist in harmony, to love each other as they loved themselves. But the Sundering tore them apart, and now they are doomed to fight, to clash, to rage against each other for all eternity—unless they can find a way to heal the wound that divides them. Belleri does not know if that is possible. She does not know if the harmony they once shared can ever be restored. But she hopes. She hopes with the heat of a thousand suns, with the warmth of a billion hearth fires, with the flame that has never been extinguished. She hopes that one day, she and her sister will dance together again in the empty spaces of creation, fire and ice, summer and winter, two halves of a whole made whole once more. Until that day, she burns, and she waits, and she loves her sister with a love that even the Sundering could not destroy.

weapons.name: Belleri's Flames
weapons.fulldesc: Belleri does not carry a weapon; she is the weapon. The fire that flows through her veins can take any form she imagines, solidifying into blades of pure flame, lashing out as whips of living fire, coalescing into shields of superheated air. In one moment, she can summon a katana of white-hot flame, its edge so sharp it can cut through stone; in the next, she can call forth a bow that fires arrows of condensed fire, each one a meteor in miniature. She is limited only by her imagination, and her imagination has been honed over millennia of creation and destruction. The flames respond to her will instantly, flowing from her hands like water, and they burn with a heat that can melt mountains—yet they never harm her, for she is fire, and fire cannot burn itself.
weapons.look: Flames of orange, red, and gold, shifting and flowing at will
weapons.aura: Heat haze, embers, flickering light

passive.name: Eternal Flame
passive.description: Belleri's abilities apply a stack of Burning (max 10 stacks). Each stack deals 5% of her magic power as fire damage per second. At 10 stacks, the target explodes, dealing 200% fire damage to all nearby enemies.

passive.name: Heat Absorption
passive.description: Belleri absorbs heat from the environment, gaining 1% increased magic power per second while in combat (max 30%).

active.name: Fireball
active.description: Belleri launches a fireball at target location, dealing 180% magical damage in a 5-meter radius and applying 2 stacks of Burning. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

active.name: Flame Whip
active.description: Belleri lashes out with a whip of fire in a 12-meter cone, dealing 150% magical damage and pulling enemies toward her. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Inferno
ultimate.description: Belleri channels for 2 seconds, then unleashes a massive wave of fire in a 25-meter radius. Enemies take 500% magical damage and are stunned for 2 seconds. Burning stacks are instantly applied to all enemies. Cooldown: 100 seconds. Passive: During Inferno, Belleri is immune to crowd control and takes 50% reduced damage.

special.name: Phoenix Rebirth
special.description: When Belleri dies, she explodes, dealing 300% magical damage to all enemies within 15 meters. After 5 seconds, she is reborn at the explosion site with 50% HP and mana. Cooldown: 180 seconds.

ratings.notes: Exceptional magical burst damage with powerful area control. Her rebirth passive makes her incredibly difficult to permanently eliminate.

skills.name: Radiant Heat
skills.description: Enemies within 8 meters of Belleri take 30% magical damage per second and have their armor reduced by 20%.

skills.name: Flame Dash
skills.description: Belleri transforms into a bolt of fire, dashing 15 meters and leaving a trail of flames that deals 80% damage per second for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Fire Shield
skills.description: Belleri surrounds herself with a shield of fire that absorbs 400 damage and deals 100% magical damage per second to nearby enemies. Lasts 5 seconds. Cooldown: 16 seconds.

skills.name: Cauterize
skills.description: When Belleri falls below 30% health, she instantly heals for 30% of her max health and gains 50% increased magic power for 5 seconds. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Belleri Fumas is fire incarnate, a primordial spirit who has existed since the first spark struck the first kindling. She is warmth, life, destruction, and renewal—all the contradictions of flame wrapped in a form of breathtaking beauty...

relationships.description: Fire and ice, eternal adversaries and eternal friends. They clash, they reconcile, they love each other across the ages.
A Divine Phoenix, a fragment of the first fire, bound to Belleri since the dawn of creation.

mount.name: Phepe
mount.description: A Divine Phoenix of breathtaking majesty, Phepe's feathers are a gradient of orange, red, and gold, shifting like living flame. Its eyes are two points of pure, white light that burn with the intensity of a star. Its wingspan is vast, capable of blotting out the sun, and when it flies, it leaves a trail of embers that fade slowly, like the memory of a sunset.
mount.fulldesc: Phepe is not merely a mount; it is a fragment of the first fire, a spark from the moment of creation given form. Its feathers are warm to the touch, and when it sings—a sound like crackling flames and distant thunder—the air around it shimmers with heat. It is bound to Belleri by a bond that transcends the physical, a connection that has existed since before the first humans learned to fear the dark. It can breathe torrents of flame that can level mountains, can summon storms of embers that blind enemies, and can wrap Belleri in its wings, shielding her from harm. When she rests, it curls around her, its warmth a comfort; when she fights, it flies above her, a blazing sun against the sky, a warning to all who would dare to face the fire.
abilities: Solar Flare – Emits a blinding flash of light that stuns all enemies within 15 meters for 2 seconds (cooldown: 60 seconds).
Ember Storm – Summons a storm of embers that deals 200% magical damage over 5 seconds in a 10-meter radius.
Wings of Protection – Wraps Belleri in its wings, granting her invulnerability for 3 seconds (cooldown: 90 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Mage / Burst damage dealer with exceptional area control, damage over time, and a rebirth passive that makes her extremely durable.

dialogue.greeting: Feel the warmth of the eternal flame. It welcomes you.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Inferno! Let the world burn and be reborn!
dialogue.respawn: From the ashes, I rise again. As I always have.

heroes.name: Waguri
titles: The Monster Summoner of the Inverted Shrine
heroes.shortDescription: She drifts through the twilight like a dream wrapped in silk and shadow, a beauty so profound that it borders on the terrible. Waguri is a nineteen-year-old woman whose grace has been honed by centuries of practice, though her face holds the freshness of eternal youth. She is the evil twin of Kagura, the shadow to her light, the darkness that her sister tries to forget. She wields the Kagegasa, a tattered parasol that holds the power of a thousand shadows, and she commands a legion of monsters bound to her will.
heroes.fullDescription: Waguri was born under the shadow of the Inverted Shrine, a place where the light of the sun never reaches, where the only illumination comes from the bioluminescence of the monsters that serve the Plum Clan. She is the twin of Kagura, but where her sister found peace in the light of the sun, Waguri found power in the shadows of the moon. She does not hate her sister; she pities her, for Kagura has chosen to blind herself to half of the world, to reject the darkness that is as much a part of her as the light. Waguri loves Hayabusa, the shadow ninja who walks between the worlds, and she knows that he sees her, truly sees her, in a way that her sister never could. She waits in the shadows of the inverted shrine, her parasol open, her monsters waiting, her heart beating in time with the pulse of the darkness. She knows that Kagura will come, that they will meet, that they will clash, as they have a thousand times before. And she will fight, and she will laugh, and she will offer her sister the darkness, again and again, until Kagura finally understands that light cannot exist without shadow, and that the only way to be whole is to embrace them both.

story.part1: The Inverted Shrine was built in the shadow of the World Tree, on the spot where the first demon fell to an elven blade. Its architecture is inverted—spires pointing downward, altars on the ceiling, prayers written in reverse—because the Plum Clan believes that the darkness is not the absence of light, but its mirror, its reflection, its necessary opposite. Waguri was born there, on the night of a lunar eclipse, when the shadows were deepest and the monsters of the shrine were most active. Her mother died in childbirth, and her father—the head of the Plum Clan—raised her in the ways of the shadow summoners, teaching her to bind monsters to her will, to open gates to the abyss, to walk the line between the living and the dead. Kagura was born at the same moment, but in a different place—on the surface, under the light of the full moon, in the care of the sun priests. They were separated at birth, two halves of a whole, raised in ignorance of each other's existence. Waguri did not learn that she had a twin until she was sixteen, when Kagura came to the Inverted Shrine to destroy it. They fought, and Waguri won, and Kagura fled. That was the first time Waguri saw her sister's face, and she wept, because she recognized something in those light-filled eyes—a loneliness that matched her own.
story.part2: The Plum Clan had served the Inverted Shrine for a thousand years, binding monsters to their will, using them to protect the realm from threats that the sun priests could not see. Waguri was the most gifted summoner in the clan's history; by the age of ten, she had bound a greater oni to her service; by twelve, she had tamed a tengu lord; by fifteen, she had opened a gate to the abyss and walked among the shadows, returning with a legion of monsters at her command. The elders of the clan feared her, but they also revered her, for they knew that she was the fulfillment of a prophecy—the Shadow Keeper, the one who would unite the darkness and use it to protect the world from threats that the light could not defeat. Waguri did not want to be a prophecy; she wanted to be a girl, with friends, with family, with someone who understood her. But the shrine was her home, and the monsters were her companions, and the darkness was her only comfort. She learned to accept her fate, to embrace the shadows, to find beauty in the darkness that others feared.
story.part3: The first time she met Hayabusa, she was hunting a rogue oni that had escaped its binding. He was hunting the same oni, sent by the Shadow Accord to eliminate a threat to their operations. They fought—not each other, but the oni, and when it fell, they stood back to back in the darkness, breathing heavily, their blades dripping with shadow ichor. Hayabusa looked at her, and she looked at him, and for a moment, the world stood still. 'You're not what I expected,' he said. 'Neither are you,' she replied. They did not become friends; they became something more—two creatures of the darkness, bound by a shared understanding that the light could never provide. They meet in secret, in the spaces between the worlds, where the light of the sun never reaches and the shadows are deepest. They talk, they spar, they share the silence that only those who have walked the abyss can understand. Waguri loves him, though she has never said it aloud, and she knows that he loves her in return, though he has never said it either. They are two shadows, dancing in the darkness, and for Waguri, that is enough.
story.part4: The rivalry with Kagura is the great tragedy of Waguri's existence. They are twins, bound by blood, divided by light and shadow. Kagura believes that the darkness is evil, that the shadows must be banished, that the only path to salvation is through the light. Waguri knows that this is a lie—that the darkness is not evil, merely different, that the shadows are not enemies, merely misunderstood. They have fought a hundred times, each battle a clash of ideology as much as steel. Kagura always fights with righteous fury, believing that she is saving her sister from damnation. Waguri always fights with sorrowful resignation, knowing that she cannot win, cannot lose, cannot change her sister's mind. At the end of each battle, when Kagura flees into the light, Waguri watches her go, and she whispers, 'One day, sister. One day, you will understand.' She does not know if that day will ever come. But she hopes, because hope is the only light that the darkness cannot extinguish.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Waguri and the spirits of the Inverted Shrine, is that she is not the evil twin—she is the necessary twin. The balance between light and shadow requires both, and Kagura's rejection of the darkness has made her incomplete, a half-being, a soul that cannot be whole until she accepts the shadow that is her birthright. Waguri does not seek to corrupt her sister; she seeks to complete her, to show her that the darkness is not a curse, but a gift, a tool, a part of the world that is as beautiful as the light. She waits in the shadows of the inverted shrine, her parasol open, her monsters waiting, and she knows that one day, Kagura will come to her—not to fight, but to understand. And on that day, Waguri will embrace her, and they will be whole, light and shadow, two halves of a single soul, united at last. Until that day, she waits, and she hopes, and she loves her sister with a love that even the darkness cannot dim.
