skills.name: Descendant's Challenge
skills.description: When facing Sun, Wukong cannot use his ultimate for the first 2 minutes of the match (wants to see Sun's potential first). After 2 minutes, his ultimate deals 30% bonus damage to Sun.

heroes.lore: Wukong was born from a stone on the Mountain of Flowers and Fruit. He rebelled against heaven, was trapped under a mountain by Buddha, and later redeemed himself by journeying to the West. Now he watches over his descendant Sun, waiting to test the young monkey's worth.

relationships.description: Wukong watches Sun with amusement and pride, waiting for the day they will meet in battle.
Wukong still laughs at the memory of their conflict, but bears no true grudge.

mount.name: Badal
mount.description: Badal is Wukong's cloud, a swirling mass of white mist and golden light that carries him wherever he wishes. It can carry him across the universe in a heartbeat, shield him from the eyes of the gods, expand to hold a thousand clones or shrink to a wisp that hides in his ear.
mount.fulldesc: Badal is Wukong's cloud, a swirling mass of white mist and golden light that carries him wherever he wishes. It is not merely a mount; it is a fragment of his own soul, a companion that has been with him since he first leaped from his stone womb. It can carry him across the universe in a heartbeat, can shield him from the eyes of the gods, can expand to hold a thousand of his duplicates or shrink to a wisp that hides in his ear. It is as wild and untamable as he is, and it responds only to his call. When Wukong rides Badal, he leaves a trail of golden mist, and the cloud's laughter—a soft, tinkling sound—echoes in the wind.
abilities: Cloud Speed – increases Wukong's movement speed by 60% while riding.
Celestial Shield – blocks one incoming ability while riding.
Clone Carrier – can carry Wukong's clones into battle.

heroes.specialty: Mobile fighter with clone mechanics, crowd control, and high sustain. Excels at skirmishing and confusing enemies.

dialogue.selection: Heaven trembled once. It will tremble again.
dialogue.death: This body... was just a stone...

heroes.name: Kei Kai
heroes.shortDescription: A woman of sixteen years who has lived for twelve millennia, her face frozen in the eternal youth of the necropolis. Kei Kai is the grandmother of Irithel, a necromancer who seeks to make all beasts her slaves. She wields the Bone Bow, which fires cursed bone shards, and rides Kai, a corrupted orange smilodon. She seeks to bind her granddaughter as she has bound all her other beasts.
heroes.fullDescription: She rises from the bone‑strewn plains like a forgotten goddess returning to claim her dominion, a being of such ancient, terrible beauty that the very dead rise to greet her. Kei Kai appears as a woman of sixteen years, though her existence spans twelve millennia; her face is frozen in the eternal youth of the necropolis, a visage of sharp, aristocratic grace. Her features are a masterful blend of Indian nobility and the ethereal perfection of the undying: a soft, heart‑shaped visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, pale lips perpetually curved in a knowing, serene smile. Her skin is healthy, luminous, the color of warm ivory, smooth and flawless, with a faint, golden undertone. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, dark lashes, their irises a deep, luminous ember, the color of dying coals and the first light of dawn. Her hair is a cascade of rich, ruby red, falling past her waist in thick, lustrous waves. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She wears leather armor stitched from hides of beasts she has tamed, pale white with a yellowish cast, minimal and revealing: a breastplate cupping her breasts, a short ragged skirt, bone gauntlets, a smilodon skull helmet with glowing orange gem eyes. She wears bone jewelry—a necklace of finger bones, bracelets of rib segments, earrings of fangs. Her weapon is the Bone Bow, taller than she is, limbs carved from fused femurs, string woven from hair, firing shards of cursed bone. Her companion is Kai, a corrupted orange smilodon of terrifying majesty, her first and most loyal beast.

story.part1: Twelve thousand years ago, in the jungles of what would become the Wild Zones, a girl was born with a gift: she could speak to the dead. Kei Kai was the daughter of a tribal shaman, and her power was seen as a blessing. She could call the spirits of departed animals, ask them for guidance, and send them to their rest. The tribe prospered under her protection, and she grew to be a wise and beloved leader. She married, had children, and watched her grandchildren play in the fields. One of those grandchildren was Irithel, a girl with wild eyes and a fierce heart, who loved the great cats of the jungle more than any human.
story.part2: The tragedy came when a plague swept through the tribe. Kei Kai's daughter, Irithel's mother, was one of the first to fall. Kei Kai tried to call her spirit back, but the plague had corrupted the souls of the dead, twisting them into hungry ghosts. In her desperation, Kei Kai made a pact with a death god: her soul for the power to bind the dead, to make them serve instead of destroy. The death god agreed, and Kei Kai's power transformed. She could no longer speak to the dead; she could command them. The plague spirits were bound, forced to serve as her army. But the cost was her humanity. She stopped aging. She stopped feeling warmth. And she began to see all creatures—living and dead—as tools to be used.
story.part3: Irithel grew up watching her grandmother change. The kind, wise woman who had taught her to hunt became cold, calculating, obsessed with power. Kei Kai began to bind not just the dead, but the living—great beasts of the jungle, forcing them into servitude with cursed bone arrows. Irithel tried to stop her, but Kei Kai was too powerful. The grandmother banished her granddaughter, telling her that she was weak, that she did not understand the necessity of control. Irithel fled into the wilds, vowing to free the beasts that her grandmother had enslaved.
story.part4: Centuries passed. Kei Kai built a necropolis in the heart of the jungle, a fortress of bone and shadow where her undead army waited. She tamed Kai, a great smilodon, as her personal mount, corrupting its spirit with necromantic power. The sabertooth's fur turned a deep, vibrant orange, its stripes black as the void, its eyes embers of molten fire. It became her most loyal servant, the first beast she ever tamed, and it has been with her for ten thousand years. Kei Kai's power grew, but so did her loneliness. She began to miss Irithel, to regret the harsh words she had spoken. But her pride would not let her reach out.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the jungle, and Irithel has returned, a grown woman riding a great leopard, her heart full of defiance. Kei Kai watches her granddaughter from the walls of her necropolis, and for the first time in millennia, she feels something other than cold purpose: hope. She does not want to fight Irithel. She wants to bind her, to add her to the legion of the dead, to keep her granddaughter by her side forever. She believes that this is love—the same possessive, desperate love that made her make the pact with the death god. She does not understand that Irithel would rather die than be enslaved. The Bone Bow is drawn, Kai is growling, and the grandmother and granddaughter stand on opposite sides of a war that neither wants but neither can avoid. For Kei Kai is the Tamer of the Undead, and she will not let her granddaughter escape again—even if it means breaking her spirit to keep her.

weapons.name: Bone Bow
weapons.fulldesc: The Bone Bow is a weapon that has become the symbol of Kei Kai's dominion over the dead. The bow is taller than she is, its limbs carved from the fused femurs of a hundred beasts, its string woven from the hair of a thousand maidens, its surface etched with the curses that bind her army to her will. The bow is pale, almost white, with veins of yellow and orange that pulse with a sickly light when she draws. The arrows she fires are not wood; they are shards of bone, each one cursed with the essence of a creature she has tamed, and when they strike, they do not merely wound—they bind, enslaving the soul of the victim, adding them to her eternal legion. With the Bone Bow, she has tamed the great beasts of the world, has raised armies from the dust, has made the dead march at her command.

weapons.aura: Orange smoke, bone dust, sickly light

skills.description: Creates a wall of bones in a 8-meter line that blocks enemy movement and projectiles for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Eternal Longing
skills.description: When Irithel is below 20% health, Kei's damage to her is reduced by 50% (cannot bring herself to kill her granddaughter). Instead, she gains 50% movement speed toward Irithel, trying to reach her.

heroes.lore: Kei Kai was a tribal shaman who made a pact with a death god to save her tribe from a plague. The pact granted her immortality and the power to bind the dead, but cost her humanity. She now rules a necropolis, having tamed countless beasts including the smilodon Kai. Her granddaughter Irithel fled from her, vowing to free the enslaved beasts. Kei Kai now seeks to bind Irithel as well, believing that only by controlling her can she protect her.

relationships.description: Kei Kai loves Irithel but expresses that love as a desire to control and bind her, believing it's for her protection.
The entity that granted Kei Kai her powers; she serves it in exchange for immortality.

mount.name: Kai
mount.description: A sabertooth tiger twisted by necromantic power, fur deep vibrant orange with black stripes, eyes two embers of molten fire. Larger than any mortal beast, shoulders level with Kei's head, fangs longer than her forearm. Bound to Kei by a bond that transcends death.
mount.fulldesc: Kai is a sabertooth tiger twisted by necromantic power, its fur a deep, vibrant orange, its stripes black as the void, its eyes two embers of molten fire. It is larger than any mortal beast, its shoulders level with Kei's head, its paws capable of crushing chariots, its fangs—two wicked curves of bone—longer than her forearm. Kai is not merely a mount; it is the first beast she ever tamed, the creature that has been with her since the dawn of her power, and it is bound to her by a bond that transcends death. It moves with a silent, predatory grace, its head lowered, its eyes watching, and when she calls, it roars, a sound that shakes the foundations of the necropolis and calls the dead to rise. Despite its corruption, Kai still remembers the wild jungles where it once roamed free, and sometimes, in the quiet of the night, Kei Kai strokes its fur and whispers apologies that the sabertooth cannot understand.
abilities: Corrupted Roar – stuns enemies in a 8-meter cone for 1 second.
Pounce – leaps to a target location, dealing 150% physical damage.
Eternal Bond – Kei Kai takes 10% reduced damage while riding Kai.

heroes.specialty: Summoner marksman with binding effects, minion generation, and crowd control. Excels at extended fights and controlling areas.

dialogue.selection: The dead rise. The living kneel.

heroes.name: Seraphine
heroes.shortDescription: An angel of the highest order, a being of such radiant, terrible beauty that the darkness itself recoils from her presence. Seraphine is the angel that Argus fell from, the sister who watched him choose darkness over light. She has hunted him across the ages, not to destroy him, but to save him, wielding the holy sword Excalibur and accompanied by a golden falcon.
heroes.fullDescription: She descends from the heavens like a dawn that has chosen to remain, a being of such radiant, terrible beauty that the darkness itself recoils from her presence. Seraphine appears as an angel of the highest order, her form a masterwork of Byzantine artistry and celestial perfection. Her face is a study in divine symmetry: a soft, heart‑shaped visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, rose‑pink lips perpetually parted in a serene, sorrowful smile. Her skin is fair, luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint, golden undertone. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, golden lashes, their irises a deep, luminous jade blue, the color of the sea at twilight, flecked with light. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk past her waist, worn loose. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She wears a white silk dress cut deep at the chest, with sky‑blue cords, overlaid with gleaming white armor shaped like wings, a gold and sapphire collar, a belt of gold chains with tiny bells, and a floating halo of pure white light. On her back, vast golden wings with sky‑blue veins. Her weapon is Excalibur, a longsword of pure white steel that ignites with holy fire. Her companion is a golden falcon that soars above her.

story.part1: In the earliest days of the Celestial Order, when the angels were first formed from the light of creation, two siblings were born from the same star: Argus and Seraphine. They were twins in spirit if not in flesh, inseparable, their souls intertwined like strands of gold thread. Argus was the warrior, fierce and proud, his blade always ready. Seraphine was the healer, gentle and wise, her touch mending wounds that swords could not. Together, they led the heavenly legions against the demons of the abyss, and together, they were invincible. The other angels called them 'The Twin Stars,' for their light seemed to banish all darkness.
story.part2: The corruption began slowly. Argus grew weary of the endless war, weary of watching his brothers fall, weary of a heaven that seemed indifferent to the suffering of its soldiers. He began to question the Celestial Council, to doubt their wisdom, to resent their distance. Seraphine tried to counsel him, to remind him of their duty, to hold him in the light. But Argus had begun to listen to other voices—whispers from the abyss, promises of power that did not require obedience. One night, without a word, Argus spread his blackening wings and descended into the darkness. Seraphine woke to find his side of the bed cold, his sword gone, and a single feather—once gold, now ash—on her pillow.
story.part3: The hunt lasted centuries. Seraphine tracked Argus across the realms, watching as he fell deeper into corruption, as his golden armor turned to black iron, as his healing hands became claws. She fought him a hundred times, and each time, she offered him redemption. Each time, he refused. The other angels called her a fool, a sentimentalist who was wasting heaven's resources on a lost cause. But Seraphine knew something they did not: Argus's fall had not been a choice, but a wound. Something had poisoned him, something that could be healed if she could only reach him. She did not give up. She could not give up. He was her brother, her other half, the only being in existence who understood her completely.
story.part4: Excalibur was forged in the heart of a dying star, blessed by the archangel Michael himself. The sword does not merely kill; it redeems, stripping away the darkness that clings to the souls of the fallen, offering them one last chance to return to the light. Seraphine has wielded it for a thousand years, and she has never once used it on a soul that did not deserve its mercy. She prays every night that she will not have to use it on Argus, that he will see her reaching hand and take it before the blade falls.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the barriers between worlds, and Argus has emerged from the abyss at the head of a demonic army. Seraphine stands in his path, Excalibur in her hand, her golden wings spread wide, her halo blazing. She does not want to fight him. She wants to hold him, to heal him, to bring him home. But she knows that the Argus she loved may be gone, replaced by a creature of shadow and rage. She has prepared herself for the worst, even as she hopes for the best. She will offer him redemption one more time, and if he refuses, she will do what she must. For she is Seraphine, the Angel of Light, and her love is stronger than any darkness—even the darkness that has consumed her brother.

appearance.hairStyle: Long, straight, past waist, shimmering curtain
appearance.eyeColor: Deep luminous jade blue, flecked with light
appearance.skinTone: Fair, luminous, fresh cream, golden undertone

upper.shoulders: Golden pauldrons shaped as crescent moons
upper.neck: Collar of gold and sapphire with seraphim pendants
upper.waist: Belt of gold chains with tiny golden bells

weapons.name: Excalibur
weapons.fulldesc: Excalibur is the sword that has become the symbol of Seraphine's hunt for Argus. The sword is longer than her arm, its blade a length of pure, white steel, its edge sharp enough to cut the fabric of reality, its surface etched with the names of the angels who have fallen in the war against darkness. The hilt is wrapped in white leather, its pommel set with a sapphire that glows with a soft, blue light, and when she draws it, the blade ignites with holy fire—a golden flame that does not burn the righteous but consumes the impure, turning heresy to ash and demons to cinders. She wields it with the precision of a master duelist, her thrusts swift and sure, her cuts deliberate and final. Excalibur does not merely kill; it redeems, stripping away the darkness that clings to the souls of the fallen, offering them one last chance to return to the light.
weapons.look: White steel blade with etched angel names, white leather hilt, sapphire pommel, ignites with holy golden flame
weapons.aura: Golden flame, white light

passive.name: Holy Flame
passive.description: Basic attacks deal bonus magic damage equal to 5% of the target's max health against demon or undead enemies.

passive.name: Purifying Light
passive.description: Allies within 10 meters of Seraphine gain 10 health regeneration per second.

active.name: Righteous Strike
active.description: Seraphine dashes to an enemy and strikes, dealing 160% physical damage and silencing them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Holy Barrier
active.description: Creates a barrier around an ally that absorbs 300 damage and reflects 20% of damage taken back to attackers for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Redemption
ultimate.description: Seraphine channels for 2 seconds, then unleashes a wave of holy light in a 20-meter radius. Enemies take 300% magic damage and are stunned for 2 seconds. Allies are healed for 400 health plus 20% of their max health and have all debuffs removed. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Sister's Love
special.description: When Argus is an ally, Seraphine's healing on him is increased by 50%. When Argus is an enemy, her damage against him is reduced by 30% (cannot bring herself to hurt him), but his damage against her is also reduced by 30%.

ratings.notes: Hybrid fighter/support with healing, crowd control, and anti-demon specialization.

skills.name: Angelic Presence
skills.description: Enemies within 8 meters of Seraphine have their healing reduced by 30%.

skills.name: Heavenly Flight
skills.description: Seraphine flies to a target location, becoming untargetable for 0.5 seconds and healing allies she passes over for 100 health. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Blessing of Light
skills.description: Grants an ally 30% attack speed and 20% movement speed for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: The Long Hunt
skills.description: Seraphine always knows the location of Argus on the minimap (if he is in the match). Her movement speed toward him is increased by 15%.

heroes.lore: Seraphine is an archangel, the twin sister of Argus. When Argus fell to darkness, Seraphine vowed to hunt him across the ages, not to destroy him, but to save him. She wields Excalibur, a blade of holy fire that redeems the fallen, and she has never given up hope that her brother will one day return to the light.

relationships.description: Seraphine loves Argus and has hunted him for centuries, offering redemption each time they meet.
They share the burden of watching loved ones fall to darkness; they pray together when their paths cross.

pet.name: Golden Falcon
pet.description: A golden falcon of breathtaking majesty, its feathers a gradient of gold and white, its eyes two points of sapphire blue. It soars above Seraphine, serving as her scout and messenger.
pet.fulldesc: The golden falcon has been with Seraphine since the earliest days of heaven, a gift from the archangel Michael. Its feathers are a gradient of pure gold at the tips to pristine white at the breast, and its eyes are two points of sapphire blue that mirror Seraphine's own. It can fly faster than the wind, can see farther than any mortal eye, and when Seraphine needs to send a message, it carries her words on its wings. In battle, it dives at enemies, its talons leaving trails of holy light, and it can summon a burst of golden feathers that blinds foes. It is her most trusted companion, the only being that has never doubted her mission, and it perches on her shoulder when she rests, its head tucked under its wing, a small, warm presence in the cold of the hunt.
abilities: Holy Dive – dives at an enemy, stunning them for 0.5 seconds.
Messenger – can deliver a message to any ally on the map (global).
Feather Storm – creates a burst of feathers that blinds enemies in a 6-meter radius.

heroes.specialty: Hybrid fighter/support with healing, crowd control, and anti-demon specialization. Excels at protecting allies and redeeming fallen enemies.

dialogue.selection: The light guides me. And him.

heroes.name: Viper
heroes.shortDescription: A woman who moves through the fog‑shrouded streets of Whitechapel like a ghost in a snowstorm, a figure of such cold, calculated beauty that the whispers of the underworld fall silent at her passing. Viper is Lesley's sister, a silencer who hunts those who twist the truth, wielding the Venom sniper rifle and accompanied by Humpum, a white hummingbird fitted with silver armor.
heroes.fullDescription: She moves through the fog‑shrouded streets of Whitechapel like a ghost in a snowstorm, a figure of such cold, calculated beauty that the whispers of the underworld fall silent at her passing. Viper appears as a woman in her early twenties, her features a masterful blend of Victorian elegance and lethal precision. Her face is heart‑shaped, with a soft, rounded jawline; a small, straight nose; full, pale lips perpetually pressed into a thin, contemplative line. Her skin is fair, luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint, cool undertone. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, blonde lashes, their irises a pale, icy blue. One eye is fitted with a corrective lens visor, a sleek piece of silver tech that wraps around her temple. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk past her shoulders in loose waves. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust, with a cross scar etched across her left thigh. She wears a white fur long coat over a white tattered crop top, a white leather mini skirt with silver patterns, leather white gloves, white short boots, and a furry silver pauldron over her right shoulder. Her weapon is Venom, a silenced killer sniper rifle of matte black steel with white polymer stock. Her companion is Humpum, a small white hummingbird fitted with silver armor.

story.part1: The Fog District of London is where secrets go to die. And Viper is the one who kills them. Born into a family of information brokers, Viper and her sister Lesley were trained from childhood to listen, to observe, to remember. Lesley was the idealist, believing that truth should be shared with the world. Viper was the pragmatist, believing that some truths were too dangerous to be spoken. Their father, the head of a secret network of spies, was murdered by a rival organization when the sisters were sixteen. Lesley vowed to expose the murderers. Viper vowed to silence them. They have not spoken since.
story.part2: Viper built her reputation one bullet at a time. She became the most feared silencer in London, a ghost who could kill a man in a crowded room and leave no witness. She never missed, never hesitated, never left evidence. Her sniper rifle, Venom, was custom-built for her by a gunsmith who owed her a debt. The rifle is silenced, its report no louder than a cough, and it fires bullets that fragment on impact, leaving no trace. Viper's victims simply... stop. The rumors stop with them.
story.part3: The cross scar on her thigh is a reminder of her first kill. She was seventeen, and her target was a man who had sold the names of her father's agents to the rival organization. She followed him for three days, learning his habits, his weaknesses, his fears. When she pulled the trigger, he was looking at her, and she did not flinch. The bullet entered his throat, and his blood sprayed across her leg. She carved a cross into her skin that night, marking the spot, and she has added a notch for every kill since. There are over three hundred notches now.
story.part4: Lesley became a sharpshooter for the righteous, exposing corruption and bringing criminals to justice. Viper watched from the shadows, proud and resentful. Lesley's way was slower, messier, more dangerous. But it was also more honorable. Viper sometimes wondered what her life would have been like if she had chosen her sister's path. But the wonder never lasted. She had made her choice, and she would live—and die—with it.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought new secrets to London, and new enemies who must be silenced. Viper has learned that the rival organization that murdered her father is rising again, allied with demonic forces. Lesley is hunting them, and she is walking into a trap. Viper has a choice: let her sister fall, or break her own rules and intervene. She has loaded Venom, strapped on her armor, and taken to the rooftops. She will not let Lesley die. She will not let the truth die. She will silence the enemies of her family, one bullet at a time. For she is Viper, the Silencer of Whitechapel, and her sister's blood is the only truth she cannot ignore.

weapons.name: Venom
weapons.fulldesc: Venom is a silenced killer sniper rifle that has become the symbol of Viper's reign over the rumor mills of London. The rifle is longer than her arm, its barrel a length of matte black steel, its stock a sleek, white polymer, its scope a masterpiece of optical engineering that can see through walls and into the hearts of her targets. The rifle is silenced, its report no louder than a cough, and when she fires, the bullet flies true, killing without sound, without warning, without trace. She has used it to silence a hundred liars, a thousand rumormongers, and each kill is a lesson: the truth is sacred, and those who defile it will answer to Viper.
weapons.look: Matte black steel barrel, white polymer stock, silenced barrel, high-tech scope
weapons.aura: Cold blue glow from scope

passive.name: Silent Killer
passive.description: Viper's basic attacks do not reveal her location on the minimap. Enemies killed by Viper leave no death marker for 5 seconds.

active.description: Throws a smoke grenade that creates a 6-meter cloud for 4 seconds, blinding enemies inside and silencing their abilities. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

ultimate.name: The Silencer
ultimate.description: Viper fires a devastating shot in a line, dealing 350% physical damage to the first enemy hit and 150% to subsequent enemies. The shot ignores 50% of the target's armor. If the target dies, Venom's cooldown is reset. Cooldown: 40 seconds.

special.name: Sister's Shadow
special.description: When Lesley is an ally, Viper's smoke grenade also heals Lesley for 100 health. When Lesley is an enemy, Viper's critical strikes against her deal 50% less damage (cannot bring herself to kill her sister).

ratings.notes: Sniper marksman with stealth, high burst damage, and execution potential.

skills.name: Ghost Walk
skills.description: Viper makes no sound when moving and is invisible to enemy wards and traps while not attacking.

skills.name: Grapple Hook
skills.description: Viper fires a grappling hook to a wall or tower, pulling herself to that location. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Marked for Death
skills.description: Viper marks an enemy for 5 seconds, revealing their location and increasing her damage against them by 20%. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Blood Bond
skills.description: When Lesley is below 20% health within 50 meters, Viper gains 50% movement speed toward her and her next basic attack deals 100% bonus damage to the enemy threatening Lesley.

heroes.lore: Viper is Lesley's sister, a silencer who hunts those who twist the truth. She operates from the shadows of Whitechapel, eliminating targets with her silenced sniper rifle Venom. Though estranged from her sister, she still watches over Lesley and will not let her fall.

relationships.description: Viper loves her sister but disagrees with her methods. She protects Lesley from the shadows without her knowledge.
They have crossed paths in the underworld; Claude owes her a favor.

pet.name: Humpum
pet.description: A small white hummingbird fitted with silver armor, wings a blur of white, eyes two points of red light. No larger than a palm. Serves as Viper's scout, lookout, and messenger.
pet.fulldesc: Humpum is a small, white hummingbird fitted with silver armor, its wings a blur of white, its eyes two points of red light. The bird is no larger than Viper's palm, its feathers pristine, its beak sharp. Humpum is not merely a pet; it is her scout, her lookout, her second pair of eyes in the fog‑shrouded streets. It can fly faster than the eye can see, can deliver messages across the city in minutes, can distract a target with its brilliant, darting flight long enough for Viper to line up her shot. It is the only being she trusts, the only soul that has never betrayed her, and it perches on her shoulder when she waits, its tiny heart beating in time with her own. When Viper is sad, Humpum hovers before her face, its red eyes blinking, and she feels a little less alone.
abilities: Scout – reveals a 10-meter area around Humpum.
Distraction – causes an enemy's next attack to miss.
Quick Message – can deliver a short message to any ally on the map.

heroes.specialty: Sniper marksman with stealth, high burst damage, and execution potential. Excels at eliminating high-value targets from range.

dialogue.selection: Truth is a bullet. I'm the gun.

heroes.name: Keingzyu
heroes.shortDescription: A hive queen of the deepest cosmos, a being of such alien, terrible beauty that entire systems dim in her passing. Keingzyu is the rival hive leader who seeks to consume Zhask's world, a primordial entity of cosmic hunger. She commands a swarm of bio-drones and wields the Devourer, a living staff that consumes the life force of worlds.
heroes.fullDescription: She drifts through the void between stars like a malignant nebula given will, a being of such alien, terrible beauty that entire systems dim in her passing. Keingzyu appears as a hive queen of the deepest cosmos, her form a fusion of chitinous armor, silk‑soft flesh, and the hungry light of dying suns. Her face is a mask of gleaming black chitin that sweeps back from a crown of sensory antennae. Her eyes are not two, but hundreds: a cluster of compound eyes set into the sides of her head, each facet a perfect hexagon. Her primary eyes are at the front, their irises a deep, swirling red with pupils of absolute black. Her skin, where chitin does not cover, is cosmic black shot through with veins of sickly yellow and vibrant green. Her 'hair' is a cascade of wiry black filaments tipped with glowing nodes. Her physique is tall, slender yet dense, with chitinous armor, hive appendages from her lower back, and digitigrade legs ending in three-toed claws. She wears a gown of shimmering iridescent green silk beneath chitinous plates. Her weapon is the Devourer, a living staff of fused chitin and cosmic wood, topped with a cluster of mandibles and a sphere of condensed cosmic energy. Her companions are her bio-drones, a swarm of mosquito-like creatures.

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the first sentient life blinked into existence, there was the Swarm. Keingzyu was the first hive queen, born from the collision of two dying galaxies, her consciousness forged from the chaos of their destruction. She has consumed a hundred worlds, a thousand civilizations, and each consumption made her stronger, more evolved, more hungry. She does not hate the worlds she devours; she does not even think of them as enemies. They are food. And food does not have feelings.
story.part2: The cosmic tree was her greatest conquest. It grew at the center of the universe, a sentient being of pure life energy, and it resisted her for eons. She sent wave after wave of bio-drones, each one adapted to counter the tree's defenses. The tree withered, its leaves turning black, its bark cracking, and when it finally fell, Keingzyu consumed its essence. The withered vine that now hangs from her wrist is a trophy, a reminder that even the most powerful life can be devoured. But the tree's death also changed her. She began to dream of something other than hunger. She began to wonder if there was more to existence than consumption.
story.part3: The dream faded when she sensed Zhask's world. It was a planet of immense life energy, a world that pulsed with the same power as the cosmic tree. Keingzyu felt a hunger she had not felt in millennia. She crossed the void between galaxies, her swarm trailing behind her like a cloak of wings, and she arrived at the edge of Zhask's system. She could feel him on the planet below, a hive king of the same species, but different. He had chosen to protect his world instead of consuming it. She did not understand. Protection was weakness. Consumption was strength.
story.part4: The Devourer grew stronger with each world she consumed. It was a symbiote, a fragment of her hive mind given form, and it whispered to her constantly: 'Hunger. Feed. Grow.' Keingzyu listened, because the Devourer had never been wrong. But sometimes, in the quiet between stars, she wondered if the Devourer was speaking for her, or she for it. She had become a weapon of consumption, but had she ever been anything else? She could not remember a time before the hunger.
story.part5: Now, Keingzyu waits at the edge of Zhask's system, her compound eyes fixed on his world, her swarm buzzing with anticipation. She knows that Zhask will fight, that he has prepared his defenses, that he will not surrender his world without a struggle. She respects him for that, in the way that a predator respects prey that does not run. But respect will not stay her hunger. She will consume his world, and she will consume him, and then she will move on to the next galaxy, and the next, until the universe is silent. Unless... unless he can show her something she has never seen before. Unless he can remind her what it felt like to be more than hunger. She does not expect that to happen, but she leaves the possibility open. For she is Keingzyu, the Death Star, and even a world-eater can dream.

weapons.name: Devourer
weapons.fulldesc: The Devourer is a living staff that has become the symbol of Keingzyu's hunger. The staff is taller than she is, its shaft a length of fused chitin and cosmic wood, its surface etched with the names of the worlds she has consumed. At its head, a cluster of mandibles unfolds, their edges rimed with yellow light, and within them, a sphere of condensed cosmic energy pulses, red and black, hungry and patient. The Devourer is not a tool; it is a symbiote, a fragment of her own hive mind given form, and it grows and evolves with each world she helps her consume. When she raises it, it emits a beam of pure, destructive fire that can cut through the crust of a planet; when she strikes the ground with it, it sends out tendrils of living energy that consume everything they touch, converting matter into fuel for her ever‑growing hive.
weapons.look: Fused chitin and cosmic wood, mandible cluster at head, red/black energy sphere
weapons.aura: Yellow, red, black, consuming tendrils

skills.name: Galactic Grudge
skills.description: When facing Zhask, Keingzyu's ultimate deals 20% bonus damage and has 15 seconds reduced cooldown.

heroes.lore: Keingzyu is a primordial hive queen who has consumed a hundred worlds. She has crossed the void to consume Zhask's world, seeing him as a rival who chose protection over consumption. She commands a swarm of bio-drones and wields the Devourer, a living staff of cosmic hunger.

relationships.description: Keingzyu seeks to consume Zhask's world and prove that consumption is superior to protection.
The withered vine on her wrist is a trophy from the cosmic tree she consumed.

heroes.specialty: Summoner mage with area damage, sustain, and anti-structure capabilities. Excels at extended fights and consuming objectives.

heroes.name: Lamia
heroes.shortDescription: An empress of twenty‑eight years, frozen in the eternal prime of a ruler blessed by the light. Lamia rules with wisdom and compassion, her power drawn from the sun itself. She wields the Suneir staff and is accompanied by Gham, a sun‑and‑beach‑colored falcon. She seeks to offer warmth and salvation to all, even her nemesis Vexana, whom she pities rather than hates.
heroes.fullDescription: She rises over the marble spires of her ancient kingdom like the sun breaking free of the horizon, a being of such radiant, benevolent beauty that the shadows themselves bow in reverence. Lamia appears as an empress of twenty‑eight years, her features frozen in the eternal prime of a ruler blessed by the light. Her face is a masterwork of Grecian perfection: a soft, oval visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, coral‑pink lips perpetually curved in a warm, knowing smile. Her skin is luminous, the color of honeyed cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint, golden undertone. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, fiery lashes, their irises a deep, glowing ember, the color of molten gold and the first light of dawn. Her hair is a cascade of sunfire, a torrent of orange and red past her waist in thick, lustrous waves shot through with gold. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She wears a deep liquid gold gown cut deep at the chest, overlaid with an orange silk empress's robe with white fur lining, a hammered gold crown with an orange gem, a collar of gold and amber, and gold bracelets. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. Her weapon is the Suneir, a staff topped with an orange crystal sphere containing a miniature sun. Her companion is Gham, a sun‑and‑beach‑colored falcon.

story.part1: Lamia was born during a solar eclipse, a portent that the old priestesses said would make her a ruler of two worlds—the world of light and the world of shadow. Her mother, the previous empress, died in childbirth, and Lamia was raised by the sun priests of the golden temple. They taught her that light was not a weapon, but a gift; that the sun's warmth was not for burning, but for nurturing. Lamia learned quickly, her natural affinity for solar magic manifesting when she was only seven years old. She called down a beam of sunlight to heal a dying bird, and the priests wept with joy. They knew she was the one who would restore the kingdom.
story.part2: The kingdom was in ruins when Lamia took the throne at sixteen. A century of war with the Shadow Accord had left the fields barren, the cities in rubble, and the people starving. Lamia did not raise an army. She raised her staff, the Suneir, and called upon the sun. For forty days and forty nights, she channeled solar energy into the land, healing the soil, purifying the water, bringing warmth to the frozen fields. The people called her a miracle, but Lamia knew it was simply the sun doing what the sun had always done—giving life. She was just the conduit.
story.part3: Vexana came to her kingdom during the fifth year of Lamia's reign. The necromancer was not an invader; she was a supplicant, seeking the sun's warmth to revive her dead husband. Lamia refused, not out of cruelty, but out of wisdom. 'The dead cannot be warmed,' she told Vexana. 'Only the living can feel the sun.' Vexana did not accept this answer. She left the palace in tears, and over the following years, she became Lamia's nemesis, raising armies of the dead to march against the kingdom of light. Lamia mourned the loss of what could have been a friendship, and she swore that one day, she would offer Vexana warmth again.
story.part4: The Sundering brought new challenges. Demons poured through rifts, drawn by the life energy of Lamia's kingdom. She fought them not with armies, but with barriers of golden light, protecting her people while healing the wounded. She never took a life, not even in self‑defense. Her soldiers were sworn to capture, not kill. The demons, confused by this mercy, began to retreat. Some even surrendered, and Lamia offered them redemption, purifying their corruption with the Suneir's light. Word spread across Kretos: the Light Sun Empress was not a conqueror. She was a healer.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has weakened the barriers between worlds, and Vexana has returned, more powerful than ever, her armies of the dead threatening to overrun the kingdom. Lamia stands on the walls of her palace, Gham on her shoulder, the Suneir in her hand. She does not want to fight Vexana. She wants to heal her, to offer her the warmth she was denied all those years ago. She knows that Vexana's darkness is not malice, but grief—a wound that has festered for centuries. Lamia believes that even the deepest wound can be healed, if only the sun is allowed to shine on it. She raises her staff, the crystal blazing, and she calls out to her nemesis: 'Vexana! The sun still rises! Will you not feel its warmth?' She waits for an answer, hoping against hope that this time, the necromancer will accept.

weapons.name: Suneir
weapons.fulldesc: The Suneir is a staff that has become the symbol of Lamia's reign. The staff is taller than she is, its shaft a length of polished olive wood wrapped in gold wire, its surface carved with the story of her kingdom's founding. At its head, a sphere of pure, orange crystal floats within a cage of golden filigree, and within the crystal, a miniature sun turns, its light pulsing with the rhythm of her heartbeat. When she raises the Suneir, the sphere blazes with light, and from it pour rays of warmth that heal the sick, guide the lost, and illuminate the darkest paths. The staff does not destroy; it nurtures, offering guidance to those who seek it, light to those who wander in darkness. She has wielded it for her entire reign, and she has never used it to harm a soul that did not first threaten her people.
weapons.look: Olive wood shaft wrapped in gold, floating orange crystal with miniature sun
weapons.aura: Golden and orange light, warmth shimmer

passive.name: Sun's Warmth
passive.description: Allies within 12 meters of Lamia gain 15 health regeneration per second.

passive.name: Daybreak
passive.description: Lamia's abilities deal 15% increased damage during daytime (in-game day/night cycle).

active.name: Healing Ray
active.description: Heals an ally for 250 health plus 15% of their max health. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Solar Flare
active.description: Unleashes a burst of sunlight in a 8-meter radius, dealing 150% magic damage to enemies and blinding them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Eternal Dawn
ultimate.description: Lamia channels the full power of the sun for 10 seconds, creating a 20-meter radius zone of daylight. Allies inside gain 50% attack speed, 30% movement speed, and 50% damage reduction. Enemies inside are slowed by 30% and take 100 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Mercy's Light
special.description: When Vexana is an enemy, Lamia's healing abilities on Vexana's minions are increased by 100% (she tries to redeem them). When Vexana is an ally, Lamia's damage reduction on her is doubled.

ratings.notes: Support mage with healing, area denial, and team buffs.

skills.name: Golden Aura
skills.description: Lamia's basic attacks heal the nearest ally for 30 health per hit.

skills.name: Sun Shield
skills.description: Grants an ally a shield that absorbs 300 damage and reflects 15% of damage back to attackers. Lasts 5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Guiding Light
skills.description: Reveals all enemies within 30 meters for 3 seconds and grants allies 20% movement speed toward revealed enemies. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Undying Hope
skills.description: If Vexana is on the enemy team and falls below 10% health, Lamia automatically casts Healing Ray on her (once per match).

heroes.lore: Lamia is the Light Sun Empress, a ruler blessed by the sun who healed her kingdom with solar magic. Her nemesis is Vexana, a necromancer she once tried to help but who turned to darkness after being refused. Lamia continues to offer Vexana redemption, believing that even the deepest wound can be healed by the sun's warmth.

relationships.description: Lamia pities Vexana and continues to offer her redemption, hoping to heal her grief.
The celestial guardian respects Lamia's commitment to peace and often aids her kingdom.

pet.name: Gham
pet.description: A falcon of breathtaking majesty, feathers a gradient of golden yellow at the breast to deep orange at the wingtips, eyes two points of molten gold. Wingspan vast, talons sharp, cry a clarion call. Serves as Lamia's messenger, scout, and companion.
pet.fulldesc: Gham is a sun‑and‑beach‑colored falcon of breathtaking majesty. Its feathers are a gradient of golden yellow at the breast to deep orange at the wingtips, and its eyes are two points of molten gold that match Lamia's own. It is larger than any mortal bird, its wingspan vast, its talons sharp, its cry a clarion call that echoes across the plains. Gham is not merely a pet; it is her messenger, her scout, her link to the farthest corners of her kingdom. It can fly faster than the wind, can see farther than any mortal eye, and when she needs to send a decree, it carries her words on its wings. It perches on her shoulder when she holds court, its head turning, its eyes watching, and it is the only being that knows her without the weight of the crown. When Lamia is sad, Gham rests its head on her crown, and she feels less alone.
abilities: Royal Message – can deliver a message to any ally on the map.
Sun Strike – dives at an enemy, dealing 100% magic damage and blinding them.
Golden Sight – reveals all enemies within 15 meters.

heroes.specialty: Support mage with healing, area denial, and team buffs. Excels at protecting allies and redeeming enemies.

heroes.name: Tiamat
heroes.shortDescription: A primordial sea goddess who has swum in the oceans of the world since before the first fish crawled onto land. Tiamat rules the abyss, wielding the Trident of the Abyss and riding Pristi, an abyssal sawfish. She watches over her daughter Kadita, waiting for the day when she will be ready to rule the seas.
heroes.fullDescription: She rises from the crushing depths like a dream of the deep given form, a being of such ancient, terrible beauty that the oceans themselves remember her birth. Tiamat appears as a primordial sea goddess, her form a masterful fusion of Babylonian myth and the eternal, hungry grace of the abyss. Her face is a study in timeless perfection: a soft, oval visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, silver‑blue lips perpetually parted in a knowing, sorrowful smile. Her skin is fair, luminous, the color of moonlight on still water, smooth and flawless, with a faint, cool undertone. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, white lashes, their irises a deep, luminous sea‑green, the color of the abyss where the sun never reaches. Her hair is a cascade of pure white silk past her waist, worn loose, floating as if underwater. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She has no tail; she walks upon the water with a shimmering, watery aura around her legs. She wears a scaled armor of deep dark blue, overlapping like sea serpent scales, with silver edges, a coral crown of blood red with pearls, obsidian accessories, and a belt of interlocking obsidian plates with sea beast skulls. Her feet are bare, touching the water only when she wills. Her weapon is the Trident of the Abyss, a coral and obsidian trident that commands the seas. Her companion is Pristi, an abyssal sawfish of terrifying majesty.

story.part1: Before the first land rose from the waters, Tiamat ruled the primordial sea. She was born from the salt and the darkness, the first consciousness to emerge from the deep. For eons, she swam alone, her only companions the creatures of the abyss—fish that had never seen light, whales that sang songs older than the stars. She was content, but she was lonely. So she created the first mermaids, shaping them from coral and foam, breathing life into them with her own breath. They called her Mother, and Tiamat wept with joy.
story.part2: The gods of the sky grew jealous of Tiamat's domain. They raised mountains from the sea, creating land, and they filled the land with creatures that walked and flew. Tiamat watched as her children—the mermaids—were pushed to the edges of the world, hunted by humans, forgotten by history. She raged, summoning tsunamis that swallowed coastal cities, calling storms that battered the shores. The sky gods fought her, and their battle lasted a thousand years. In the end, Tiamat retreated to the abyss, not because she was defeated, but because she was tired. She sank into the deepest trench and slept for millennia.
story.part3: Kadita was born during Tiamat's sleep, a mermaid princess of a lost kingdom. When Tiamat awoke, she sensed the girl's power—a spark of the primordial deep that had not been seen since the first mermaids. She watched Kadita from the abyss, guiding her, protecting her, but never revealing herself. She knew that Kadita must grow on her own, must face her own trials, must learn the weight of the crown that she would one day inherit. Tiamat was patient. She had waited eons; she could wait a little longer.
story.part4: The Sundering tore open the ocean floor, and demons of the void began to seep into the deep. Tiamat fought them, her trident blazing, her sawfish Pristi tearing through their ranks. She protected the mermaid kingdoms that still survived, and she watched as Kadita rose to become a hero of the surface world. She was proud of her daughter, prouder than she had ever been of any of her creations. But she was also sad, for she knew that Kadita did not know her mother's name.
story.part5: Now, Tiamat swims through the abyss, Pristi at her side, the Trident of the Abyss humming with power. She has sensed that Kadita is in danger, that a demon lord of the void seeks to claim the mermaid princess as a bride. Tiamat will not allow it. She rises from the deep, her watery aura blazing, her coral crown glowing. She will reveal herself to her daughter for the first time, not as a goddess, but as a mother. She will fight beside Kadita, and when the battle is won, she will offer her a choice: return to the deep and rule the abyss, or stay on the surface and continue her hero's journey. Either way, Tiamat will love her. For she is Tiamat, the Abyssal Mermaid Maiden, and her daughter is the greatest treasure in all the seas.

weapons.name: Trident of the Abyss
weapons.fulldesc: The Trident of the Abyss is a weapon that has become the symbol of Tiamat's dominion over the deep. The trident is taller than she is, its shaft a length of petrified coral wrapped in silver wire, its surface carved with the story of the first ocean. Its three tines are forged from obsidian and silver, their edges sharp enough to split the waves, their tips crackling with the pressure of the abyss. When she raises it, the seas rise with her; when she strikes the water, whirlpools open, swallowing ships; when she points it at the sky, storms gather, their rain falling in sheets, their lightning striking the waves. The Trident of the Abyss commands the deep, and there is no ocean that does not answer its call.
weapons.look: Petrified coral shaft with silver wire, three obsidian and silver tines crackling with abyssal pressure
weapons.aura: Deep blue, silver, crackling pressure waves

passive.name: Pressure of the Deep
passive.description: Enemies within 10 meters of Tiamat have their movement speed reduced by 15%.

passive.name: Ocean's Blessing
passive.description: Tiamat gains 20% damage reduction while in water (rivers, ocean, or standing in her own abilities).

active.name: Tidal Wave
active.description: Sends a wave of water forward 15 meters, dealing 150% magic damage and knocking back enemies. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Whirlpool
active.description: Creates a whirlpool at target location for 4 seconds, pulling enemies toward the center and dealing 80 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

ultimate.name: Primordial Storm
ultimate.description: Tiamat calls a massive storm in a 25-meter radius for 8 seconds. Enemies inside are slowed by 40%, take 100 magic damage per second, and are periodically struck by lightning (200 magic damage). Allies inside gain 30% movement speed and heal for 20 health per second. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Mother's Love
special.description: When Kadita is an ally, Tiamat's damage reduction on her is doubled. When Kadita is an enemy (unlikely), Tiamat's abilities deal 50% less damage to her.

ratings.notes: Control mage with area denial, crowd control, and team utility.

skills.name: Abyssal Adaptation

skills.name: Deep Dive

skills.name: Salt Curse

skills.name: Eternal Vigil
skills.description: If Kadita is below 20% health within 50 meters, Tiamat automatically casts Deep Dive to reach her and gains 50% damage reduction for 5 seconds.

heroes.lore: Tiamat is the primordial sea goddess, mother of all mermaids. She has ruled the abyss since before land existed. She watches over her daughter Kadita from the depths, waiting for the day when she will reveal herself and offer Kadita the choice to rule the seas.

relationships.description: Tiamat watches over Kadita from the abyss, proud of her daughter's heroism.
They fought for control of the world eons ago; Tiamat retreated to the deep, but the truce is uneasy.

mount.name: Pristi
mount.description: An abyssal sawfish of terrifying majesty, larger than any mortal vessel, body a sleek silver-blue torpedo of muscle and scale, snout a massive serrated saw. Eyes pits of silver light, fins edged with venomous spines, back armored in obsidian plates.
mount.fulldesc: Pristi is an abyssal sawfish of terrifying majesty, larger than any mortal vessel, its body a sleek, silver‑blue torpedo of muscle and scale, its snout a massive, serrated saw that can cut through the hulls of ships like paper. Its eyes are two pits of silver light, its fins edged with spines that drip with venom, its back armored in plates of obsidian that reflect no light. Pristi is not merely a mount; it is the first creature Tiamat tamed, the beast that has served her since the dawn of the oceans, and it obeys her with the devotion of a child. It swims in her wake, its saw slicing the waves, and when she calls, it rises, carrying her across the seas faster than any ship, its bulk parting the water, its presence a warning to all who would defy the queen of the deep. When Tiamat is sad, Pristi nuzzles her with its massive snout (carefully), and she feels the bond of eons.
abilities: Saw Slash – deals 200% physical damage in a cone.
Venom Spines – applies a poison that deals 50 damage per second for 5 seconds.
Obsidian Armor – reduces damage taken by 20% while mounted.

heroes.specialty: Control mage with area denial, crowd control, and team utility. Excels at zone control and protecting allies.

dialogue.selection: The deep remembers. So do I.

heroes.name: Lunara
heroes.shortDescription: An elf empress of eighteen years whose existence spans a hundred millennia, her face frozen in the eternal youth of the high elves. Lunara is the mother of Selena, the queen who waits for her lost daughter to return to the light of the World Tree. She wields divine moonlight and is accompanied by Fika Fika, a blob‑like being of pure moonlight energy.
heroes.fullDescription: She drifts through the eternal twilight of the World Tree's canopy like a memory of the first dawn, a being of such ancient, gentle power that the stars themselves pause in their courses to witness her passing. Lunara appears as an elf empress of eighteen years, though her existence spans a hundred millennia; her face is frozen in the eternal youth of the high elves, a visage of such perfect, serene beauty that it seems carved from moonlight itself. Her features are a masterwork of celestial refinement: a soft, oval visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, rose‑pink lips perpetually curved in a warm, knowing smile. Her skin is fair, luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint, silver undertone. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, silver lashes, their irises a deep, luminous sapphire blue, the color of the sky at the moment the first star appears. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk past her waist, worn loose, catching the light of the World Tree. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She wears a white silk robe cut deep at the chest, overlaid with silver armor shaped like swan's wings, a crescent crown of silver and sapphire, a collar of silver and moonstone, and a royal blue sash. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. Her weapon is her divine moonlight—a field of pure healing light. Her companion is Fika Fika, a small blob‑like being of pure moonlight energy.

story.part1: When the World Tree first sprouted from the seed of creation, Lunara was there. She was not born; she was woven from the first moonbeam that touched the tree's leaves, a consciousness formed from silver light and the whisper of wind through branches. For eons, she watched as the world took shape, as the first elves emerged from the tree's roots, as civilizations rose and fell beneath her canopy. She became their queen, not through conquest, but through love. The elves called her 'Mother,' and Lunara accepted the title with a humility that belied her power.
story.part2: Selena was her youngest daughter, a child of starlight and shadow, born with a wildness that none of Lunara's other children possessed. Lunara loved her fiercely, perhaps too fiercely, for she tried to protect Selena from the darkness that whispered at the edges of the World Tree. Selena, feeling smothered, fled into the night. She fell in with the Shadow Accord, was corrupted by a demon lord, and became a creature of twilight—neither light nor dark, but something in between. Lunara wept for a thousand years, her tears watering the roots of the World Tree, and the elves learned to mourn with their queen.
story.part3: Lunara did not send armies to retrieve Selena. She did not curse her daughter's name. She waited, patient as the moon, knowing that the light in Selena's heart could not be extinguished, only buried. She sent dreams to her daughter, visions of the World Tree, whispers of home. Selena never responded, but Lunara felt her daughter's heart flutter, felt the shadow flicker. There was hope.
story.part4: The Sundering tore rifts in the sky, and demons poured through, seeking to corrupt the World Tree. Lunara fought them, her moonlight blazing, her healing light mending the wounds of her warriors. She never took a life, not even a demon's. She purified them, turning their corruption to ash and sending their cleansed souls back to the void. The demons learned to fear the silver light of the elf empress, and they began to whisper that she was more dangerous than any warrior, for she did not kill—she unmade.
story.part5: Now, Lunara stands at the base of the World Tree, Fika Fika floating beside her, her crown glowing. She has sensed that Selena is coming home, that the Sundering has forced her daughter to confront the darkness within herself. Lunara does not know if Selena will accept her embrace or reject it again. But she knows that she will be here, waiting, as she has always waited. She will offer her daughter the same warmth she offered a hundred thousand years ago, when Selena was a child playing in the tree's roots. For she is Lunara, the Moonlight Seeker Elf Empress, and a mother's love is the one light that no darkness can extinguish.

weapons.name: Divine Moonlight
weapons.fulldesc: Lunara does not wield a physical weapon; her very presence is a weapon in itself, a field of pure, healing light that purifies corruption and banishes darkness. When she walks, the shadows flee; when she speaks, the wounded heal; when she raises her hand, the World Tree answers, its roots reaching through the earth to protect her children. She can summon light that heals the sick and purifies the corrupted, can call upon the power of the World Tree to nourish the land and protect her people. Her moonlight is not a tool of destruction—it is a tool of salvation, and she has never used it to harm a soul that did not first threaten her children.
weapons.look: Silver and blue glowing aura, beams of moonlight from her hands
weapons.aura: Silver, sapphire, healing warmth

passive.description: Allies within 15 meters of Lunara gain 20 health regeneration per second and 10% damage reduction.

passive.name: World Tree's Blessing

Allies inside are immune to death (cannot fall below 1 HP) and heal for 10% of their max health per second. Enemies inside are slowed by 50% and take 100 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 100 seconds.

skills.name: Silver Veil
skills.description: Lunara is permanently invisible to enemies more than 30 meters away.

skills.name: Tree's Embrace
skills.description: Lunara wraps an ally in roots, granting them immunity to crowd control for 3 seconds and healing them for 200 health. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Starlight Path
skills.description: Creates a path of moonlight for 6 seconds. Allies on the path gain 40% movement speed; enemies are slowed by 30%. Cooldown: 16 seconds.

skills.name: The Long Wait
skills.description: If Selena is on the enemy team, Lunara cannot be the one to land the killing blow on her. Instead, Selena is left at 1 HP, and Lunara gains a movement speed boost toward her.

heroes.lore: Lunara is the elf empress, mother of the World Tree, and mother of Selena. She has waited a hundred thousand years for her lost daughter to return to the light, never giving up hope. She wields divine moonlight and protects her people with healing and purification.

relationships.description: Lunara loves Selena and has waited millennia for her return, never giving up hope.
Lunara is bound to the World Tree; its health is tied to her own.

pet.name: Fika Fika
pet.description: A small, blob‑like being of pure moonlight energy, body a soft glowing cloud of silver and blue, head shaped like a tiny dragon, eyes two points of golden light. Moves with floating, crawling motion, slithering through air or ground as if swimming through moonlight.
pet.fulldesc: Fika Fika is a creature of impossible cuteness, its body a soft, glowing cloud of silver and blue, its head shaped like a tiny dragon's, its eyes two points of golden light that shine with a gentle, loving warmth. It moves with a floating, crawling motion, slithering through the air or the ground as if it were swimming through moonlight, and it follows Lunara everywhere, curling around her shoulders when she rests, nuzzling her cheek when she is sad. Fika Fika is not merely a pet; it is a fragment of the World Tree's light, a spirit of pure joy that Lunara has carried with her since the first age, and it is the only being that has never known sorrow. It chitters softly when she speaks, its golden eyes blinking, its cloudy body pulsing with the rhythm of her heartbeat, and it brings her comfort in the long, patient years of waiting. When Lunara cries, Fika Fika absorbs her tears and glows brighter, as if trying to warm her.
abilities: Joyful Glow – heals nearby allies for 10 health per second.
Moonlight Bubble – creates a shield around an ally that absorbs 150 damage.
Fika's Blessing – increases an ally's movement speed by 20% for 3 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Pure support with massive healing, purification, and team invincibility ultimate. Excels at keeping allies alive and redeeming fallen enemies.

dialogue.selection: The World Tree watches. So do I.

heroes.name: Mika Sah
heroes.shortDescription: A warrior of Roman Greek heritage who tears across the sky like a storm that has chosen a single, perfect shape. Mika Sah is Fanny's twin, faster, stronger, and more precise than her sister. She wields the Fanananana—a combo of dual blades and grappling hooks—and rides Pere Pere, a peregrine falcon of lightning energy. She waits for the day when Fanny will finally accept her help.
heroes.fullDescription: She tears across the sky like a storm that has chosen a single, perfect shape, a being of such breathtaking, dangerous beauty that the clouds themselves part to witness her passage. Mika Sah appears as an eighteen‑year‑old warrior of Roman Greek heritage, her features a masterful blend of classical perfection and wild, untamed energy. Her face is a study in divine symmetry: a soft, oval visage with a delicate jawline; a small, straight nose; full, crimson lips perpetually curved in a fierce, knowing smile. Her skin is fair, luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint, golden undertone that catches the lightning that dances around her. Her eyes are large, almond‑shaped, framed by thick, crimson lashes, their irises a deep, lightning‑yellow that shifts and swirls like the heart of a storm. Her hair is a cascade of fiery crimson, layered and practical, swept back in a messy half‑up ponytail that allows the rest to flow behind her like a banner of flame. Her physique is classic hourglass: slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, G-cup bust. She wears a furred brown long coat over a minimal bra‑like bronze and gold breastplate, an armored mini skirt of dark red leather, silver short armor boots, spiked shoulder armor, and bronze and gold bracelets. Her weapons are the Fanananana—dual blades with lightning aura, plus grappling hook gears on her hips. Her companion is Pere Pere, a peregrine falcon with dark bronze to bright gold feathers and lightning‑yellow eyes.

story.part1: Mika and Fanny were born under a storm, twins whose cries were drowned by thunder. Their mother, a warrior of the ThunderSky tribe, died in childbirth, and their father, a scholar of aerial combat, raised them alone. He taught them to fly before they could walk, to fight before they could speak. Mika was always the faster twin, her reflexes sharper, her instincts truer. Fanny was the strategist, the one who thought before she acted. They complemented each other, and together they were unbeatable.
story.part2: The rift between them came when they were sixteen. Their father was killed by a demon of the Shadow Accord, and the twins were separated in the chaos. Fanny blamed herself, believing that if she had been faster, she could have saved him. Mika blamed Fanny, not for his death, but for her grief. 'You're always thinking,' Mika screamed. 'Sometimes you just have to act!' Fanny withdrew into herself, while Mika threw herself into training, pushing her body to its limits, determined to become strong enough to protect everyone she loved.
story.part3: They reunited years later, both having become legendary sky riders. Fanny had developed a style based on precision and control, while Mika had perfected speed and aggression. They fought side by side against demonic incursions, but the old rift remained. Mika would dive into danger without hesitation, and Fanny would hang back, calculating. Mika saw it as cowardice; Fanny saw it as wisdom. They never spoke of their father's death, and the silence between them grew wider than any canyon.
story.part4: Mika developed the Fanananana, a combat system that combined dual blades with grappling hooks, allowing her to swing through the air with impossible grace. She could strike from any angle, disappear into the clouds, and reappear behind her enemies before they could blink. She became a legend, known as the ThunderSky Rider, and she was offered a place in the Celestial Order. She refused. 'I don't serve orders,' she said. 'I serve the wind.'
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought the demons to every corner of Kretos, and Mika has heard that Fanny is struggling, that her precision style is not enough against the chaos of the rifts. Mika does not gloat. She does not say 'I told you so.' She simply appears at Fanny's side, her blades crackling, her falcon screeching. 'I'm not here to save you,' she says. 'I'm here to fight with you. Like we were meant to.' She does not know if Fanny will accept her help. But she knows that she will not let her sister fall alone. For she is Mika Sah, the Dualblade ThunderSky Rider, and the storm is finally coming home.
